229570.explanation: Every vote in Amsterdam deserves to be heard. As long as there is still a large group of Amsterdammers who do not vote or feel politically distant, we will continue to make extra efforts to involve this group. A citizens’ council can contribute to this.

229571.explanation: Discrimination and racism are unacceptable; we must act firmly against them. An urban coordinator can play a role in tackling discrimination and racism in Amsterdam, as part of a larger package of measures.

229572.explanation: Decisions that directly affect people should be made close to them. District administrators and district committee members know the neighborhoods well, so we ensure the strengthening of the districts.

229573.explanation: The PvdA wants July 1st (Keti Koti) to be an official national holiday. That authority lies with the national government. Until then, the municipality must commemorate the abolition of slavery with a day off for its employees.

229574.explanation: The PvdA does not automatically support a decrease or increase of the property tax (ozb); this depends on national policies and developments in other cities. Based on this, we decide the level of property tax. We apply the principle: the strong shoulders bear the heaviest burdens.

229575.explanation: We will gradually increase the tourist tax and invest the proceeds in the livability of the city, which is under heavy pressure in some areas.

229576.explanation: Snack bars may stay open longer when this does not harm livability, for example in entertainment districts. Restaurants can apply for an exemption to stay open later.

229577.explanation: We want fewer tourists in the city. We encourage tourists already here to visit more diverse neighborhoods and use facilities we want to preserve, such as culture, local businesses, and public transport.

229578.explanation: Users/tenants are often not owners of a property but still pay tax on its value. This tax should apply to ownership, not usage. This way, we spare local entrepreneurs who do not benefit from the property's value increase upon sale.

229579.explanation: In the city center, we invest in real estate for an economy that works for residents. We buy back properties for the city to make it more livable for Amsterdammers. This prevents new tourist shops and allows us to steer toward a diverse and attractive shopping offer.

229580.explanation: Schiphol must take the health of local residents and employees more into account. Unbridled growth frustrates ambitions for environment, climate, and housing construction. We use our position as activist shareholder to influence airport policies.

229581.explanation: The municipality acquires billions of euros in goods and services annually. Through procurement policy, we can exert broad influence. For the PvdA, it is important this is done socially and environmentally responsibly. Therefore, we do not procure from parties that violate human rights or (in)directly contribute to genocide.

229583.explanation: We will definitely maintain the provisions for undocumented people, as well as (legal) support. Amsterdam is a compassionate city and will continue supporting this group regardless of national decisions. We stand firmly with the caregivers and volunteers who assist these people.

229584.explanation: The plan to remove priority housing for status holders must be withdrawn. We lobby the national government on this. The plan is inhumane, unconstitutional, and causes people to stay in shelters far longer than necessary.

229585.explanation: Speaking the language is important for every Amsterdammer. Many labor migrants do not currently learn the language, leading to parallel lives, loneliness, and less mutual understanding. Therefore, the municipality, together with companies, must provide opportunities to learn the language.

229586.explanation: We welcome people fleeing with love and dignity in our city. They receive good guidance to participate in society, work, or attend school.

229587.explanation: Collecting bulky waste by appointment is necessary to make the city cleaner and collection more effective. A key condition is that waste collection remains affordable, reliable, and quick. This aligns Amsterdam with other large municipalities that already do this.

229588.explanation: Public toilets are only truly public when accessible to everyone. From this standpoint, we want more public toilets in popular places throughout the city, including outside the center.

229589.explanation: We want to maintain space for events in parks, but parks must always remain (largely) accessible for Amsterdammers to use for recreation, and we protect the green spaces. The current policy is, in the PvdA's view, appropriate for this.

229590.explanation: Disposing of waste must remain affordable for all Amsterdammers. Raising costs for those with more waste does not contribute to this and disadvantages (large) families.

229591.explanation: Artis holds special value for many Amsterdammers. Moreover, Artis contributes to nature education, monument care, and nature conservation.

229592.explanation: Cruise ships are extremely polluting. Also, we want to reduce the number of tourists in the city. Therefore, the Passenger Terminal Amsterdam will close in Amsterdam by 2035 at the latest, and cruise ships will no longer have a place in the city.

229593.explanation: Enough green in every neighborhood is essential according to the PvdA. When making choices in public spaces, we always consider the neighborhood's needs. Therefore, we do not make one choice for all neighborhoods in advance but assess what each needs.

229594.explanation: Recently, more space has been allocated for urban agriculture in the Lutkemeer polder. This fits our ambition for a green and sustainable city, which we will continue to pursue in the coming years. At the same time, financially responsible policy will always remain an important condition.

229595.explanation: We make maximum use of the space in the Western Port area. If that is not enough, other promising areas come into view, considering the neighborhood capacity. Wind turbines must not pose unacceptable risks to people or nature. We strictly apply noise standards.

229596.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone. It is healthy, brings joy and development, and connects people. Financial constraints must not prevent sports participation. Therefore, we contribute to reimbursing costs for Amsterdammers with low incomes.

229597.explanation: We invest in neighborhood media, such as local TV and neighborhood newspapers, which ensure people know what is happening in the city and their neighborhood. This fosters autonomy and engagement. Therefore, we want neighborhood media to continue receiving structural financial support from the municipality.

229598.explanation: Top-level sports and community sports go hand in hand. We invest substantially in the community sports sector. Amsterdam’s top athletes also inspire residents to be more active, behave sportingly, and do their best. Hosting top sports events contributes to this.

229599.explanation: Art institutions and the creators given opportunities there should reflect society. We increase the representation of groups that have been systematically disadvantaged for a long time.

229600.explanation: We retain the bonus for secondary schools that have a three-year bridge class. We do this because we believe the selection moment in group eight is too early for children, which can negatively affect their school advice and further learning development.

229601.explanation: Despite the national government’s budget cuts, we invest in pre-schools for all children from age two. Pre-schools enable children to meet each other regardless of income, educational level, or parental background.

229602.explanation: In Nieuw-West the new Meervaart theater is rising; Zuidoost is getting an expansion of the Bijlmer Park Theatre. We are proud of this. We will ensure clear progress is made in realizing both projects in the coming years so these districts have a strong cultural offer.

229603.explanation: Sports must be accessible and as low-threshold as possible for everyone. It is healthy, creates joy and development, and brings people together. Sport is therefore a public facility we must continue investing in.

229604.explanation: There is a great shortage of enforcement officers in Amsterdam, while safety is under pressure in many areas. The PvdA therefore finds it necessary to increase the number of enforcement officers.

229605.explanation: We spare no effort to combat street intimidation. Ultimately, it is up to the police to assess whether their limited capacity is best used to tackle street intimidation.

229606.explanation: The monopoly on the use of force must remain with the police. At the same time, it is important to ensure that enforcement officers can perform their work safely, so we are open to trials testing whether the use of a baton would make a significant difference.

229607.explanation: Strict measures are necessary to improve livability in the city center by limiting tourism nuisance. By stopping the sale of soft drugs to tourists in the city center, this part of the city will become less attractive to tourists and therefore more livable for residents.

229608.explanation: The PvdA supports camera surveillance in areas where we often see illegal bulk dumping. Companies currently get away too easily with illegal dumping. In cooperation with residents, we will implement measures per neighborhood to clean up the area. Camera surveillance can contribute to this.

229609.explanation: According to the PvdA, neutrality lies in the conduct of an enforcement officer; therefore, banning personal expressions related to religion is unnecessary and undesirable in light of the constitutional right to freedom of religion.

229610.explanation: The PvdA sees the ban on face-covering clothing as a restriction of rights and is concerned that this ban leads to discrimination. That is unacceptable. You should be able to pick up your passport or seek help from the municipality while wearing a burqa.

229611.explanation: There is a severe shortage of social rental housing, and every home sold is lost from the stock forever. Moreover, sales cause segregated neighborhoods, while the PvdA believes in the importance of a mixed city where everyone can live side by side, regardless of income.

229612.explanation: Many Amsterdammers are forced to leave the city because they cannot find housing, despite having already built a life here. The PvdA therefore supports priority schemes for people with ties to the city.

229613.explanation: The PvdA believes that all real estate in the city should also serve a social purpose. However, it is a shame if properties are not used, and vacancy must always be prevented. We do favor the municipality repurchasing real estate in other places where the space is indeed utilized.

229614.explanation: The PvdA believes in building complete neighborhoods: a general practitioner, a school, childcare, and other facilities are essential for a pleasant, livable city. The municipality must be able to steer this and not leave it to developers who only look at profit.

229615.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for tourist rental. Especially in the center, tourist rentals put pressure on livability. To ensure there are more homes for residents and to reduce nuisance, a ban is therefore necessary.

229616.explanation: Many elderly people want to move to a more suitable home but often cannot for various reasons, even though this would promote housing turnover. Therefore, we offer personal contact, good moving arrangements with financial compensation, and practical moving assistance. This allows better use of the housing stock.

229617.explanation: The PvdA stands for a city where everyone has a chance at housing, regardless of income level. We will therefore continue to build at least 40% social rental housing in new developments. This prevents the city from becoming a place where only wealthy people can find a home.

229618.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for profit. Therefore, we continue with the owner-occupancy obligation. This prevents commercial parties from buying houses to rent out at high prices, as well as frequent resales and modifications aimed at making a profit.

229619.explanation: The PvdA supports leasehold (erfpacht). The increase in land value beneath a home is not an individual profit but the result of everything Amsterdammers do for their city. Leasehold ensures that this increase in land value benefits us all.

229620.explanation: The PvdA advocates for a social and humane implementation of the Participation Act. With the additional earnings premium, more people have entered the workforce, so we are in favor of it.

229621.explanation: We want pets to receive the care they need, even when their owner lives in poverty. Therefore, we support Amsterdammers who cannot pay their veterinary bills.

229622.explanation: More and more people are becoming homeless due to rising rents and living costs. We do everything possible to prevent people from sleeping on the streets. We offer the homeless protection, guidance, and perspective.

229623.explanation: For too many Amsterdammers, financial security is not a reality. They cannot make ends meet on social assistance, the state pension, or low wages. Increasingly, even people with jobs cannot pay their fixed expenses. They should not have to worry about basic necessities; we provide help where needed.

229624.explanation: Social assistance, state pension, or low wages are often insufficient for living. More and more people, including those who work, cannot pay their fixed costs. We ensure that Amsterdammers earning up to 130% of the social minimum receive financial support.

229625.explanation: The PvdA opposes mandatory work without pay. However, it is important that people can participate in society, and the municipality must play a role in guiding people in that.

229626.explanation: Amsterdam is a city of solidarity. Evicting low-income Amsterdammers by ending the exemption from waste disposal charges for this group does not fit that spirit.

229627.explanation: Poverty is not a choice but a consequence of an unfair economic system. Everyone should be able to make ends meet; therefore, we stand for a livable social minimum. A basic income could be a way to achieve this. That is why we support this pilot.

229628.explanation: Public transport in our city must be accessible to all Amsterdammers regardless of their financial means. The PvdA has ensured that young children can travel free on public transport, with good results. We will expand this because public transport is a public service.

229629.explanation: Public transport in our city must be accessible to all Amsterdammers regardless of their financial means. The PvdA has ensured that young children can travel free on public transport, with good results. We will expand this because public transport is a public service.

229630.explanation: Volunteering is indispensable for a social, connected, and caring city. However, there is a growing shortage of volunteers. By offering free parking hours, we lower barriers, strengthen communities, and support this indispensable group in our city.

229631.explanation: Metro line 53 is an important connection for many residents in Southeast with other parts of the city. Therefore, the PvdA wants to maintain this connection.

229632.explanation: We keep an eye on residents and entrepreneurs in the center who depend on cars. Tourists can only park in garages and at the city edges. The space from removed parking spots is returned to residents, for example with greenery, benches, and playgrounds.

229633.explanation: Extending the North/South Line is crucial to create space on the busy railway, allowing more capacity for domestic Intercity trains and international trains. This provides a good alternative to short polluting flights and improves national connectivity.

229634.explanation: It is important to consider residents and entrepreneurs in the center who rely on cars. Very busy neighborhoods will only be accessible to cars of residents and entrepreneurs. Tourists can only park in garages and at the city edges.

229635.explanation: Clean, healthy air and reducing pressure on public space are vital for the city's livability. Creating more car-free streets ensures, for example, that children can play safely outside and that more greenery can be added to the city.

229636.explanation: The 30 kilometers per hour speed limit has improved traffic safety and the city's livability. We will maintain these speeds. At the same time, we will continue to evaluate this speed limit and adjust it where necessary to a more suitable level.

229637.explanation: Sex work is legal work, and window prostitution can be a relatively safe way for sex work to take place. Completely banning this form of sex work will lead to more illegal prostitution, with associated insecurity and nuisance, and is therefore irresponsible.

229639.explanation: The PvdA believes in caring neighborhoods where people of all ages can live. We must continue developing new residential care concepts, with enough space for social interaction in the neighborhood, and cluster elderly housing as much as possible to provide care efficiently.

229640.explanation: Every child deserves loving, prompt, and good care. That way, we work toward a future in which every child gets the chance to grow up well. Youth care institutions that repeatedly perform poorly do not fit this future.

229641.explanation: Care improves for all Amsterdammers when there is attention to everyone’s background. We encourage care institutions to be aware of cultural and religious differences.

229642.explanation: In Amsterdam, we give young people space. Every area must therefore have at least one youth center. The youth center targets the entire group, offering a broad program for both boys and girls.

229643.explanation: No child should go to school without breakfast. If the national government does not provide school breakfasts in all primary schools, we will arrange this as a municipality.

229644.explanation: Fishing is currently not allowed everywhere in Amsterdam, and it should remain that way. We continue working toward a city with clean water and improving biodiversity. We also provide space on the city’s waterways for waste collection and logistics where possible.

229570.explanation: Some of the hardest problems are solved by randomly selected citizens coming together. They generate good ideas, listen to each other, and engage in meaningful consultation. GroenLinks therefore considers citizens' assemblies a very good addition to the current political system.

229571.explanation: Far too many Amsterdammers face racism and discrimination daily. That is unacceptable. We appoint a citywide coordinator against discrimination and racism to tackle this in all areas.

229572.explanation: Each district has its own needs. The district administrations and the administrative committee of Weesp play an important role: they listen to residents, understand local concerns, and translate worries and dissatisfaction into policy. They must continue to do so.

229573.explanation: The legacy of slavery is a low point in history. We want the abolition of slavery to become a national public holiday. In Amsterdam, we want to set a good example by granting this day off to municipal employees. For GroenLinks staff, this is already one of the fixed public holidays.

229574.explanation: Amsterdam homeowners pay proportionally less property tax (ozb) than most municipalities in the Netherlands. GroenLinks believes it is better to tax wealth rather than income, and therefore does not want to reduce the property tax.

229575.explanation: More tourists come to Amsterdam than the city can handle. Many Amsterdam residents experience the consequences of this daily. GroenLinks finds it only fair to ask tourists to contribute and use that money to improve the city.

229576.explanation: We want snack bars and restaurants in certain areas, such as entertainment districts, to have the option to remain open until three a.m. But in residential neighborhoods, it is often better if closing time is earlier than three a.m.

229577.explanation: There are too many tourists in the city. GroenLinks wants to do everything possible to limit the number of tourists and give Amsterdammers more space again. That means we absolutely should not invest money in attracting more tourists.

229578.explanation: Currently, shopkeepers who rent pay part of the tax based on the property's value. We want to tax wealth and ensure that small entrepreneurs can remain in the city.

229579.explanation: In the city center, we now have far too many fast food chains and Nutella and stroopwafel shops, and too little space for general practitioners, artists, and places where residents can meet. That is why GroenLinks wants to buy properties in the center to make room for what we do want.

229580.explanation: Amsterdam must practice activist shareholding. Our shares in Schiphol are our only chance to exert influence. Schiphol must shrink, emit less, operate socially responsibly, and be a decent employer.

229581.explanation: We cannot and must not look away from the genocide and human rights violations in Gaza and the West Bank. GroenLinks believes Amsterdam, as a city of human rights, must take responsibility.

229583.explanation: No person is illegal. Everyone in our city deserves a dignified existence, whether documented or not. That is why we continue to provide shelter.

229584.explanation: Only a small portion of available social housing is allocated to status holders. They receive priority for housing, just like many other groups-and rightly so, because in Amsterdam we do not let anyone down.

229585.explanation: Migrant workers help build the homes we need, deliver mail and meals to fellow Amsterdammers, and work day and night in hospitality and cleaning. Despite their contribution to our city, migrant workers are often undervalued. We must therefore strengthen their position.

229586.explanation: We will shelter people fleeing war and violence no matter what.

229587.explanation: The current waste system leads to illegal dumping and is expensive. It is more efficient and sustainable to collect bulky waste on request.

229588.explanation: There are currently too few public toilets for women and people with disabilities. That must change.

229589.explanation: GroenLinks wants parks to have space for liveliness and culture, but without irreversible damage to the park. In recent years, we have therefore developed new policies, resulting in a good balance for us now.

229590.explanation: GroenLinks wants Amsterdammers who produce less waste to pay less. Ideally, no one should pay anymore.

229591.explanation: The municipality supports Artis because it plays an important role in nature education, culture, and heritage. Artis is making great progress toward becoming more animal-friendly. GroenLinks will continue to scrutinize them strictly.

229592.explanation: Sea cruise ships do not belong in Amsterdam. They are bad for the climate, cause unhealthy air, and bring even more tourists.

229593.explanation: GroenLinks wants the city to become car-restricted and more space for greenery. Stationary cars take up too much public space, while green streets are healthy and prepare us for the changing climate.

229594.explanation: Thanks in part to us, a large food park is coming to a section of the Lutkemeer area. Ideally, we would stop the rest as well, but past agreements prevent stopping everything. However, an alternative use as a food hub is being explored for the business park.

229595.explanation: The climate crisis is relentless. We must switch to sustainable energy as soon as possible. That is why we place wind turbines where it can be done responsibly.

229596.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate in sports regardless of income. This is already possible for children up to 18 with a city pass. We find it important that this also becomes possible for adults.

229597.explanation: Local media bring the stories and news from our city to Amsterdammers and contribute to connection within neighborhoods. The municipality must continue to support this.

229598.explanation: GroenLinks prefers to spend this money on new sports fields, affordable sports for everyone, and sports education.

229599.explanation: Art and culture must be accessible to all Amsterdammers. When the municipality co-finances, it asks recipients to consider how they can involve and reach everyone in our city, including people from different socio-economic backgrounds.

229600.explanation: Treating children differently at such a young age fosters inequality, which we want to counteract. That is why we support the broad first-year secondary school class.

229601.explanation: Preschool ensures all children start primary school with equal opportunities. It is therefore important that all children can attend preschool, and that money is not a barrier.

229602.explanation: There are 170,000 Amsterdammers living in the Nieuw-West district. This requires a theater with enough space to meet the great demand for art and culture and where neighborhood residents can meet.

229603.explanation: Physical activity is important for everyone. All Amsterdammers must have the opportunity to play sports and be active, as it is essential for our physical and mental health. We must invest extra in this.

229604.explanation: There are currently staff shortages in municipal enforcement. GroenLinks wants to address this by broadening recruitment and improving pay.

229605.explanation: It is difficult to catch perpetrators in the act during street intimidation. That is why we support plainclothes officers monitoring busy places.

229606.explanation: The monopoly on violence must remain with the police. We take other measures to improve the safety of municipal enforcement officers because they must feel safe in their work daily.

229607.explanation: GroenLinks does not believe in repressive drug policies. Banning drug sales to tourists does not solve problems and moves drug trade to the streets. This causes us to lose sight and control over the trade.

229608.explanation: GroenLinks is very cautious about deploying camera surveillance. Such a serious measure should only be used if our safety is under threat.

229609.explanation: GroenLinks believes people should be free to practice their religion. Therefore, enforcement officers should be allowed to wear a kippah or headscarf.

229610.explanation: Even if you wear a burqa, you should be able to collect your passport, attend a wedding, or receive help from the municipality. Prohibiting that is discrimination.

229611.explanation: There is a crying shortage of social rental housing. We must cherish every social rental home and encourage housing corporations to do the same.

229612.explanation: Amsterdammers must be able to build a life in the city. Currently, for example, families often leave because they cannot find suitable housing. By reserving homes, we build strong and social communities so people can put down roots.

229613.explanation: We must combat vacancy in the city at all costs. The municipality must make good use of its buildings. If that doesn't happen, we ensure that social organizations can use the premises and therefore do not sell those buildings.

229614.explanation: Land in Amsterdam is very scarce and highly sought after. You can only build on community land once, so we should make sure that something good is built there. Affordable homes, sustainable buildings, and green, healthy neighborhoods.

229615.explanation: The tourist pressure in the city center is too high. The inner city must become a place where people can live, work, and meet each other again. Therefore, we restrict holiday rentals in the center. Housing is scarce and should not be used to make money from tourists.

229616.explanation: To tackle the housing crisis, mobility is needed. Many elderly people want a smaller yet more accessible home. Thanks to our policy, elderly can receive compensation for moving to such a home. This way, a larger home becomes available again for a family.

229617.explanation: Amsterdammers gain little from even more expensive homes. People are looking for an affordable house. Therefore, we want to build at least 40% social rental housing and 40% middle rental housing, preferably even higher percentages.

229618.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, not to make money from. That is why GroenLinks introduced a self-occupancy obligation. This prevents entire neighborhoods from being bought up by slumlords or foreign investors who then rent them out at exorbitant prices.

229619.explanation: For more than 100 years, leasehold has contributed to Amsterdam’s development as a social, left-wing city. The leasehold system ensures that increases in land value flow back to the community. This allows us to invest extra in facilities such as public transport, sports fields, and arts and culture.

229620.explanation: It's hardly possible to live on social assistance. Thanks to GroenLinks, people in Amsterdam can earn additional income alongside their benefits. This provides relief and peace of mind, prevents debts, and enables people to participate in society. Those allowed to earn extra income more often progress to work.

229621.explanation: The veterinarian is an expensive cost for people with low income. Thanks to GroenLinks, city card holders can now visit the vet once a year for free. Good for both people and animals.

229622.explanation: Years of failing housing policy in The Hague have led to an unacceptable increase in homeless and roofless people. Everyone deserves a roof over their head. This is a basic provision. In recent years, we have increased shelter places for homeless people. We will continue to expand these places.

229623.explanation: The personal healthcare deductible is now 385 euros. That is a lot of money, especially for people with minimum income. People avoid going to the doctor or are stressed about this. Nationally, we want to abolish the deductible. Until that happens, we will reduce the health gap between rich and poor in Amsterdam.

229624.explanation: No one should live in poverty. That starts with a decent minimum wage and benefits you can live on. Politics in The Hague are responsible for this, but they do too little. For as long as necessary, we will do everything in Amsterdam to help people in poverty.

229625.explanation: We do not see people on social assistance as benefit claimants, but as people. Therefore, we oppose punishment and mandatory counter-performances, as these do not help in finding work and increase stress. Every Amsterdammer deserves to be treated with dignity.

229626.explanation: The strongest shoulders must carry the heaviest burdens. That is why we waive the waste collection levy for people with low incomes.

229627.explanation: With a basic income, we can abolish the terrible benefits system. It is a dignified way to make the city poverty-free and offers all Amsterdammers more freedom in their life choices.

229628.explanation: Free public transport gives all Amsterdammers the freedom to go wherever they want. This solves all transport poverty at once. Fewer people will need a car, creating more space in our city for playing children and greenery.

229629.explanation: Money must never be a barrier to a child’s school choice or a day out. Young Amsterdammers must be free to move around the city.

229630.explanation: Volunteers do important work in our city. By giving social and religious organizations free parking hours, we lower barriers for volunteers and give them the appreciation they deserve.

229631.explanation: Southeast must be well reachable. Travelers must be able to transfer comfortably and public transport must improve in Southeast. Only when this is better arranged can we reconsider the direct connection of metro 53, considering space must be made on the rail tracks.

229632.explanation: Currently, we use UNESCO World Heritage sites to park stationary cars. What a shame. It takes up a lot of space and harms our quays. GroenLinks therefore wants to reduce the number of parking spaces in the center and make the main canals parking-free.

229633.explanation: Train traffic at Schiphol is a major bottleneck. Extending the North-South metro line relieves the train tracks. This gives us more space for international trains, which can replace budget flights; much more sustainable.

229634.explanation: Cars take up too much space in our city. GroenLinks wants more space for people and nature. However, the center must always remain accessible for crucial traffic and be accessible to everyone.

229635.explanation: By banning cars, we create space for people and greenery. Space is particularly scarce in the city center. GroenLinks therefore wants more car-free streets in the inner city. For critical traffic, such as emergency services, the center must of course always remain accessible.

229636.explanation: In recent years, thanks to GroenLinks proposals, we have introduced a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h in Amsterdam. This has already led to many fewer accidents. A great result.

229637.explanation: Sex workers must be able to work safely. That is impossible if it is forbidden.

229639.explanation: GroenLinks wants elderly to grow old in their own neighborhood. That is why we started the Long Life Home Flats, where elderly live in a community and receive care at home if needed. These flats help combat loneliness and help elderly live independently longer.

229640.explanation: It is important that our youth receive good care. That is why there is inspection and oversight of youth care institutions. If institutions perform poorly, the municipality should of course not cooperate with them.

229641.explanation: Care works better when it connects to people's living environments. Therefore, we want care that respects differences. This includes that people can choose a care provider who matches their faith, culture, or beliefs.

229642.explanation: Young people must have a place where they can be safe, develop themselves, and meet others. This must be within reach for all youth, so we want a youth center in every neighborhood.

229643.explanation: Everyone has the right to good nutrition and education, regardless of how well off their family is. Children who do not eat well also learn less well. Experiments with free school breakfasts had good results.

229644.explanation: There are many places in Amsterdam not suitable for fishing. GroenLinks also wants fishing with lead to be banned. It is extremely harmful to nature.

229570.explanation: D66 believes that we shape the city together. Participation of residents is an important tool in this. We want to involve citizens and entrepreneurs in decision-making where it is necessary and meaningful, and where the final outcome can still be genuinely influenced.

229571.explanation: D66 wants a free and equal society. There is no place for racism and discrimination. That is why we invest long-term in a full-time operational anti-discrimination unit at the police, instead of the current volunteer basis.

229572.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to stand alongside its residents, not opposite. Therefore, we reduce the distance between Amsterdammers and the municipality by strengthening the position of city districts. After all, they are closest to the residents.

229573.explanation: D66 wants Keti Koti to become a national public holiday. This will structurally recognize the legacy of slavery and its impact today. We also want to invest in a National Slavery Museum in Amsterdam.

229574.explanation: D66 does not want the burdens, such as property tax and waste collection levy, to rise for Amsterdammers. At the same time, we want to prevent that lowering leads to not being able to make necessary investments.

229575.explanation: D66 wants visitors to also contribute to the city's challenges. That is why we increase the tourist tax. Of this income, we use €100 million to buy up properties in the inner city (tourist shops) and transform them into homes. We also use this money for a cleaner city.

229576.explanation: D66 believes that a world city like Amsterdam must be alive. In a free and lively city, the municipality does not decide for you that you must be quietly in bed at two a.m. Amsterdam must also live at night; otherwise, our world city risks becoming a village.

229577.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to take measures to reduce the number of tourists and better distribute visitors.

229578.explanation: D66 thinks it is logical that only owners pay property tax.

229579.explanation: D66 is investing 100 million euros to buy more buildings in the city center to transform them into housing and facilities aimed at Amsterdam residents in the city center.

229580.explanation: D66 wants to retain shares in Schiphol so that the city can continue to influence its future.

229581.explanation: D66 does not want to support companies that do not respect human rights.

229583.explanation: D66 stands for the fundamental right to asylum. Even people who end up on the streets must be supported and guided toward a sustainable future perspective.

229584.explanation: D66 wants Amsterdam residents with a residence permit to have the opportunity to build their lives and contribute to society. Therefore, D66 wants them to have a fair chance at housing so they can participate from day one.

229585.explanation: D66 believes that highly educated migrants strengthen our knowledge economy and that labor migrants keep sectors with low-paid work afloat. Therefore, they must be fully included, and we invest in Dutch lessons from day one.

229586.explanation: D66 wants to always shelter people who flee violence and war. For centuries, our city has welcomed people seeking opportunities, safety, or a new life. And we will continue to do so.

229587.explanation: D66 wants smarter waste processing, preservation of raw materials, and a focus on reuse and recycling close to residents. We also want extended opening hours at the recycling center so that people can bring bulky waste on their own schedule.

229588.explanation: D66 wants every major square (in all city districts) to have a women-friendly public toilet that is also accessible to people with disabilities.

229589.explanation: D66 believes that events make Amsterdam what it is: a liberal and free city where everyone can be themselves. Therefore, as much space as possible should be given to events so that Amsterdam can continue to fulfill its role as a leading cultural city in Europe.

229590.explanation: D66 wants Amsterdam residents who produce less household waste to pay less and those who produce more household waste to pay more. The polluter pays.

229591.explanation: D66 sees Artis as a unique place where Amsterdam residents connect with nature and each other right in the city center. Therefore, Artis must remain as accessible as possible.

229592.explanation: D66 believes in a green and clean city. Large cruise ships that produce high emissions do not fit with this vision.

229593.explanation: D66 wants a green and livable city with 25,000 new trees. In a city with limited car traffic, this may come at the expense of parking spaces.

229594.explanation: D66 wants more agricultural land in Amsterdam, including the promised green space in Lutkemeerpolder. We are not willing to pay tens of millions for the rest of the Lutkemeerpolder. With that money, we can better invest unevenly in more housing and green spaces for Amsterdam residents.

229595.explanation: D66 considers wind turbines essential for clean energy and low energy bills for Amsterdam residents. We want wind turbines to be placed in suitable locations, such as the Western Port Area. But if necessary, we will consider alternative locations.

229596.explanation: D66 wants Amsterdam residents to live long and healthy lives. Prevention is the most important tool against health problems. Therefore, we invest in more sports, especially for vulnerable groups.

229597.explanation: D66 believes that neighborhood and local media are important for the city. They increase local ties, strengthen democracy, give residents a voice, and help counter misinformation.

229598.explanation: D66 believes that top-level sports inspire people and contribute to a vibrant sports culture. Therefore, we want to bring major top sports events to Amsterdam, such as the UEFA Women’s Euro 2029, the World Urban Games, and eventually even the Olympic Games (2036 or 2040).

229599.explanation: D66 wants subsidies to focus on quality, impact, and significance for the city. For D66, that means a balanced distribution of resources across different cultural expressions so that the cultural ecosystem remains open, fair, liberal, and accessible to everyone.

229600.explanation: D66 believes it is determined too early which education children follow in secondary school. A broad introductory year positively contributes to the development of teenagers and is also a place where children at different levels meet.

229601.explanation: D66 wants preschool for children between 2 and 4 years old to be free. Preschool helps all children with language, social skills, and confidence. This way, children start primary school with equal opportunities.

229602.explanation: D66 believes Nieuw-West deserves a large cultural center. The current Meervaart is too small for the beautiful art and all the emerging talent in Nieuw-West and surroundings. A new Meervaart is an investment in culture and talent in Nieuw-West and also attracts people from across the city to the district.

229603.explanation: D66 believes sport and physical activity are essential parts of a healthy and enjoyable society. The municipality must invest much more in sport and physical activity.

229604.explanation: D66 believes freedom cannot exist without safety. Public order officers contribute to safe streets. We give them more trust and ensure a direct point of contact in the neighborhood.

229605.explanation: This statement is not about municipal authority. The deployment of undercover agents is a police authority exercised in their criminal law duties. The police fall under the Public Prosecution Service and the minister. The municipality has nothing to do with this.

229606.explanation: D66 believes that enforcers are not disguised police officers. We will not allow them to perform tasks where their safety cannot be guaranteed.

229607.explanation: D66 wants to regulate the production and sale of drugs as much as possible. Criminalizing cannabis sales shifts sales from coffee shops to the streets. This leads to more nuisance in the city center, more youth getting involved in crime, and more insecurity for Amsterdam residents.

229608.explanation: D66 wants visible and effective enforcement against bulky waste and improperly disposed waste. Privacy must of course be safeguarded.

229609.explanation: D66 wants effective and reliable enforcement in Amsterdam. Allowing religious expressions for enforcers increases accessibility of the job for people from diverse faith backgrounds. This benefits reliability and effectiveness.

229610.explanation: D66 believes municipal buildings must be accessible to everyone, including people wearing face-covering clothing. If someone misbehaves inside a government building, they must leave the premises immediately. According to D66, this applies to everyone, regardless of how they are dressed.

229611.explanation: D66 wants more social rental housing. Selling a limited number of homes in neighborhoods that already have many social rental homes contributes to a mixed city and more income. With that income, we ultimately build more social rental homes than were sold.

229612.explanation: D66 believes that Amsterdam residents should also have a chance at social rental housing.

229613.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to use all buildings it owns for social purposes.

229614.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to have control over municipal land and a vision on urban developments. The municipality should decide where to build extra housing, where to add sports facilities, and how to green the city.

229615.explanation: D66 believes that regulation is fairer than a complete ban. Residents should be able to rent out their own homes on a limited basis, as long as they adhere to clear rules.

229616.explanation: D66 wants elderly people to always have a comfortable, suitable place to live. Many elderly who want to move don’t know how or cannot afford it. Therefore, we focus on providing more information and a compensation for moving costs. This allows elderly to move on and frees up large homes for families.

229617.explanation: D66 wants to build as many affordable homes as possible. With 40% social rental housing, housing projects do not proceed and we build too few homes. Also, private sector housing becomes too expensive. D66 wants to build 9,000 homes per year with a lot of social rental housing, a lot of mid-range rent, and more affordable owner-occupied homes.

229618.explanation: D66 believes homes are for living in. Anyone who buys a home should live in it themselves or rent it out to a close family member. We protect Amsterdam residents from investors and speculation with a residency obligation and purchase protection.

229619.explanation: D66 wants to combat speculation on the housing market and therefore supports ground lease. Ground lease prices must be more transparent for Amsterdam residents.

229620.explanation: D66 stands for an Amsterdam where everyone has control over their own life. This is often not possible when living in poverty, so we help those who need assistance. We also believe in the self-reliance of Amsterdammers. By raising the additional income limit, we increase that self-reliance.

229621.explanation: D66 believes that people should be able to properly care for their pets. That is why we support the ADAM scheme for City Pass holders. It fully reimburses chipping and registration as well as the first veterinary visit, and covers part of the medical treatment costs. This way, we target help to those who need it.

229622.explanation: D66 believes that homeless people are part of our city. They have the right to effective support. In the long term, we advocate a Housing First approach: first a home, then guidance. We reserve a buffer for victims of domestic violence.

229623.explanation: D66 stands for an Amsterdam where everyone has control over their own life. This is often not possible when living in poverty. That is why good allowance schemes exist to help people who need it. To keep healthcare affordable, we maintain the deductible for everyone.

229624.explanation: D66 believes in equal opportunities as the foundation of freedom, regardless of the place of your birth. Even people who work hard can still have financial difficulties. That is why we support people with low income and invest in prevention, self-reliance, and talent development.

229625.explanation: D66 believes in a relationship between the municipality and Amsterdammers based on empathy and trust. That is why we do not bind vulnerable persons on social assistance to all kinds of conditions. That leads to undesirable, complex procedures.

229626.explanation: D66 trusts Amsterdammers. Waiving the waste tax for residents with a low income prevents fixed costs from leading to problematic debts.

229627.explanation: D66 wants to put trust at the center. With a basic income, we prevent people from getting stuck in various poverty schemes.

229628.explanation: D66 wants to specifically support those for whom public transport is too expensive, so they can also travel. Because affordable public transport enables everyone to participate in the city. Free public transport for everyone sounds nice, but the money is urgently needed to solve challenges like the housing shortage.

229629.explanation: D66 wants children to be able to move freely and safely through our city. That is why we make public transport free for children up to twelve years old. So that (grand)parents who bring their children places will more often use public transport instead of the car.

229630.explanation: D66 wants to support various social professions and organizations that are of great value to the city, such as healthcare, education, and sports clubs. They all depend on a parking permit, not just social and religious organizations.

229631.explanation: D66 believes that a well-accessible city is the basis of equal opportunities. By redesigning the metro system, we achieve higher line frequencies and shorter waiting times. More public transport! That is why we choose a connection between Gaasperplas and Central Station with one transfer.

229632.explanation: D66 wants to prevent cars from swallowing up public space. Especially in the historic center. Therefore, we give parking spaces in the inner city a different purpose. This creates more room in the city for recreation, greening, and socializing. Fewer cars means more space for people!

229633.explanation: D66 wants better connections to new residential areas and enough space on the tracks for international trains. Therefore, we extend the North-South line from Amsterdam South.

229634.explanation: D66 wants the city center to remain well accessible, especially for children, elderly, and people with disabilities. So we want more space for cyclists and pedestrians. But cars also belong to an accessible city center.

229635.explanation: D66 wants children to be able to move freely and safely through our city. That is why we give space back to pedestrians and cyclists and expand car-restricted areas. Because less space for cars means more space for people.

229636.explanation: D66 wants to protect vulnerable road users, such as children, elderly, and people with disabilities. Traffic safety requires decisive choices. For example, our alderman’s 30 km/h rule has led to many fewer traffic accidents. We continue on that path.

229637.explanation: D66 wants freedom and emancipation for sex workers. Therefore, we want enough safe and future-proof workplaces for sex workers in Amsterdam. If window owners want to sell or relocate their property, we help with the transformation into housing.

229639.explanation: D66 wants caring neighborhoods where there is room for social interaction and where help and care are nearby. We remove barriers that make it difficult for the elderly to age healthily in their own familiar environment. In existing locations, we realize more Long Live Home flats.

229640.explanation: D66 believes vulnerable youth must be protected. That provides future prospects. When youth care institutions perform poorly, it can be especially harmful to them. That is why we do not cooperate with these organizations.

229641.explanation: D66 sees freedom of choice as fundamental because care is personal and requires trust, on the condition that this freedom of choice is fair, accessible, and affordable for everyone.

229642.explanation: D66 wants Amsterdam to be a city where young people can grow. That also means having space to make their voices heard and to participate. Especially given the increasing mental health issues among young people, it is important that they have a meeting place in every neighborhood, with access to help.

229643.explanation: D66 wants children to receive a free meal at school, especially in vulnerable neighborhoods. This prevents children from sitting in class with an empty stomach and not being able to learn properly. We want our children to develop as well as possible; that can't happen if they come to school hungry.

229644.explanation: D66 wants to optimally use Amsterdam’s water for various forms of recreation and sport. For example, we also want more open water areas suitable for swimming.

229570.explanation: Most Amsterdammers do not want extra consultations. They want elected representatives who take responsibility and make decisions. Amsterdam needs execution, not extra layers of consultation.

229571.explanation: The VVD tackles discrimination with enforcement, safety, and equal opportunities. You do that by acting, not by adding extra positions at city hall. Feeling free and safe everywhere must be noticeable on the street, at school, and at work.

229572.explanation: The current system with boroughs doesn't work well and costs a lot of money. It can be more efficient; therefore, the VVD is for abolishing the system.

229573.explanation: The VVD finds remembrance important, but a mandatory day off for all Amsterdam civil servants is not the solution.

229574.explanation: The VVD wants to significantly lower housing costs for Amsterdammers. We will reduce burdens by lowering property tax and waste charges. We can afford this by the municipality being frugal and cutting significantly in the civil service.

229575.explanation: The VVD wants to tackle nuisance without making Amsterdam even more expensive. Attracting quality tourists, smart management of visitor spread, no longer allowing tourists in coffeeshops, and enforcement work better than ever-increasing taxes.

229576.explanation: Being able to eat at night is part of a city like Amsterdam. The VVD gives entrepreneurs space and keeps the city lively. With good agreements on noise and safety, this can go hand in hand well.

229577.explanation: The VVD wants to focus on attracting the right visitors and spreading them out. Smart city marketing can reduce the influx of drink and drug tourists. This prevents nuisance and stimulates Amsterdam’s economy.

229578.explanation: Doing business in Amsterdam is currently extremely expensive. The VVD wants to make entrepreneurship easier and cheaper. Lower burdens for entrepreneurs help shops and companies to survive.

229579.explanation: The VVD wants to counter decay and monotony in the streetscape. Through targeted acquisitions, the municipality can strengthen important streets and functions in the center.

229580.explanation: The VVD chooses to focus on core tasks and solid finances. By selling the shares in Schiphol, the municipality can free up resources for safe and clean streets, affordable housing, and safe, accessible, and green neighborhoods.

229581.explanation: The VVD believes the municipality should focus on Amsterdam and its core tasks: safe and clean streets, affordable housing, and safe, accessible, and green neighborhoods. Foreign policy should not be conducted through municipal procurement.

229583.explanation: The VVD stands for a fair and clear system. That also means that those who have no right to stay must return. The municipality must therefore deploy its capacity for people who are allowed to stay and for sheltering homeless Amsterdammers.

229584.explanation: A large portion of the freed-up social housing goes to status holders. The VVD wants Amsterdammers who have been waiting for years to also have a prospect. The solution lies in building more homes and abolishing the priority rule for status holders.

229585.explanation: The VVD finds language important, but in the case of labor migrants this responsibility primarily lies with employers, sectors, and national schemes. After all, they stay relatively briefly in the Netherlands. The municipality should focus on enforcement, housing, and tackling abuses.

229586.explanation: The VVD wants control and capacity to act. Amsterdam must deliver what it can, but not always on top of what is already requested.

229587.explanation: The VVD wants the basics to be in order first and the city to be truly clean again.

229588.explanation: The VVD supports free public toilets for everyone throughout the city.

229589.explanation: Many Amsterdammers enjoy the events in our parks. The VVD wants to keep Amsterdam lively and vibrant, but with consideration for the neighbors: with good distribution and strict agreements on nuisance.

229590.explanation: The municipality must stop repeatedly raising taxes on Amsterdammers. Instead, the municipality should be more frugal itself and ensure the city becomes clean again.

229591.explanation: The VVD is proud to have an icon like Artis in the city.

229592.explanation: Tourists who come to Amsterdam via cruise ships do not cause nuisance but come here to enjoy our beautiful city. We prevent nuisance by attracting quality tourists, smartly managing distribution, no longer allowing tourists in coffeeshops, and enforcement.

229593.explanation: The VVD supports greening, but sensibly and without making the city inaccessible. Accessibility for residents and entrepreneurs remains essential. We green the city by creating more parks within it.

229594.explanation: The development of the business park in the Lutkemeerpolder is important for the city's economic growth.

229595.explanation: According to the VVD, wind turbines belong at sea and far away from residential areas. Therefore, the Western Port area is the only place in Amsterdam where wind turbines can possibly be installed.

229596.explanation: The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229597.explanation: Local journalism is important for oversight and information in the city.

229598.explanation: The VVD wants events that make the city vibrant and generate economic benefits. With good organization and distribution, all of Amsterdam benefits.

229599.explanation: The VVD chooses quality. Subsidies should go to strong plans, not based on origin or identity.

229600.explanation: The VVD supports freedom of choice. Every child should be able to choose what type of school they want to attend. The municipality should not push for one type of school model.

229601.explanation: The VVD wants children to get a good start regardless of background. By making preschool free, we ensure children can start primary school without a disadvantage.

229602.explanation: The VVD wants the municipality to make sharp decisions in investments. The basics must be in order first: safe and clean streets, affordable homes, and safe, accessible, and green neighborhoods.

229603.explanation: The VVD invests in health and strong, complete neighborhoods. Enough sports facilities are part of this. They prevent problems and bring people together.

229604.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to be safe on the streets. Visible enforcement in every neighborhood is necessary. More enforcement officers means faster action against nuisance and a safer street environment.

229605.explanation: The VVD wants everyone to feel safe on the streets. Enforcement must be genuinely noticeable, with a higher chance of catching offenders.

229606.explanation: The VVD wants enforcers to be able to perform their work safely. With proper training and clear frameworks, this can be done responsibly.

229607.explanation: The VVD wants to reduce nuisance and keep the city livable. A ban on selling cannabis to tourists will ensure less weed tourism, less crowding, and less nuisance.

229608.explanation: The VVD aims for a truly clean city. In such a city, there is no place for people who deliberately dump their waste improperly. Cameras help tackle dumpers and make enforcement more effective.

229609.explanation: The VVD wants one recognizable, neutral government. That provides clarity and prevents arguments on the street.

229610.explanation: The VVD chooses safety and recognizability in government buildings. Rules must be clear and enforceable.

229611.explanation: Selling social rental homes can actually contribute to building more social rental homes or renovating other social rental homes. A sales freeze does not solve anything.

229612.explanation: Amsterdammers without priority rules or urgency do not get housing currently. The VVD wants to significantly cut the number of priority rules, such as abolishing priority for status holders. This way, Amsterdammers have a chance again to get a home.

229613.explanation: The municipality should not be a real estate owner. Therefore, the VVD wants all non-essential real estate to be sold.

229614.explanation: At the moment, the municipality controls every detail. This causes construction to take a very long time. The VVD wants to build more homes faster. This can be done by outlining only the frameworks instead of controlling the details.

229615.explanation: Nuisance must be prevented. But completely banning it is not a solution.

229616.explanation: When elderly people move to a smaller home, a whole chain of moves is set in motion. This is good for mobility. And thus good for young and old. The VVD wants to encourage that.

229617.explanation: Amsterdam builds and has many more social rental homes than the national average (30%). The VVD chooses to build for students, starters, and young families. They currently do not get in and have a great need for affordable (owner-occupied) homes.

229618.explanation: As a temporary measure, the VVD supports purchase protection. This gives starters more opportunities in a tight housing market.

229619.explanation: Ground lease is a huge cost burden for Amsterdammers. The VVD wants to abolish ground lease in the long term. In the meantime, the VVD chooses to let everyone switch to perpetual ground lease under favorable conditions. This can save thousands of euros per year.

229620.explanation: The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229621.explanation: Having a pet is an individual responsibility and not a municipal task. The municipality should focus on its core tasks: safe and clean streets, affordable homes, and safe, accessible, and green neighborhoods.

229622.explanation: Homeless people with economic or social ties to Amsterdam deserve good shelter.

229623.explanation: The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229624.explanation: The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229625.explanation: The VVD wants to enable participation and reduce barriers to work. A meaningful counter-performance helps: it provides structure, networks, and opportunities.

229626.explanation: The lowest incomes must continue to receive exemption from waste disposal charges, but this must be done through poverty funds. This ensures that the rest of the Amsterdammers do not pay for someone else's exemption.

229627.explanation: A basic income keeps people dependent on the government longer and causes them to stand less on their own feet.

229628.explanation: Free public transport for everyone is unaffordable. The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229629.explanation: Free public transport for everyone is unaffordable. The VVD wants to combat poverty in a targeted way. We do not do this with numerous separate schemes but through a single poverty scheme and by allowing people to keep more money in their pockets with a significant tax reduction.

229630.explanation: The VVD values volunteers but does not want extra exemption regimes for specific organizations.

229631.explanation: It is important that Southeast remains well connected to the rest of the city.

229632.explanation: Parking spaces remain necessary for Amsterdammers and their visitors. The VVD focuses on underground parking garages to free up more space on the streets.

229633.explanation: Extending the North-South metro line to Schiphol is good for the city's accessibility. We also improve the connection between East and West by completing the metro ring around the city.

229634.explanation: The city center must always be accessible by car, but the municipality should better control who is allowed to drive into the city by car. For example, foreign license plates should not be permitted.

229635.explanation: The city center must always be accessible by car, but the municipality should better control who is allowed to drive into the city by car. For example, foreign license plates should not be permitted.

229636.explanation: The VVD wants logical traffic rules and smooth traffic flow. In many places, 30 km/h is not logical. Where the infrastructure is safely designed, the speed limit should match that.

229637.explanation: Window prostitution is part of Amsterdam. It should continue to exist but only in places where it causes the least nuisance.

229639.explanation: The VVD wants older people to be able to live independently for longer. By ensuring good housing for the elderly, housing market flow improves. This benefits both young and old.

229640.explanation: The VVD wants quality and safety for children. Poor providers must be removed: that means steering towards results.

229641.explanation: The VVD wants good care for everyone, but healthcare should primarily be driven by quality and availability. The system must remain workable and fair.

229642.explanation: Not every neighborhood needs a youth center.

229643.explanation: The VVD wants children to enter the classroom with a good start. Research shows that children learn better when their stomach is full.

229644.explanation: Fishing is one of the oldest and biggest sports in the Netherlands. The VVD wants space for this type of recreation in the city as well.

229570.explanation: We support citizens' assemblies, provided that the citizens are a representative reflection of society. Also, a citizens' assembly only makes sense if its advice is binding (unless there is a well-founded reason not to follow the advice).

229571.explanation: An anti-racism coordinator monitors reports of racism, exclusion practices, and lack of progression within the municipality. Institutions receiving reports may lose subsidies. Procurement and subsidy agreements have strict requirements for active anti-discrimination policies.

229572.explanation: We are against abolishing district administrations because that reduces local participation. Administrative committees give districts an important voice in the city council. The district administrations ensure better representation of neighborhood interests and make decisions closer to the people.

229573.explanation: The Dutch slavery past has left deep wounds still felt by many Amsterdammers. The municipality of Amsterdam thus gives important recognition and sends a clear signal that this history is part of our shared memory.

229574.explanation: The property tax (ozb) in Amsterdam is quite low compared to other municipalities. The income from this tax helps pay for things like schools, public transport, and safety.

229575.explanation: Mass tourism must be restricted: Amsterdam is not for sale. Increasing the tourist tax generates money that we can invest in public transport, more waste cleanup, and better infrastructure.

229576.explanation: We support snack bars remaining open longer at night because this offers more choice and safety and also helps small entrepreneurs. By keeping snack bars open longer, they can better benefit from the growing night economy in Amsterdam.

229577.explanation: BIJ1 wants an end to mass tourism in the city. Instead of attracting tourists, we should focus on improving services such as education, a housing fund, and public transport for our own residents.

229578.explanation: We are against taxing only property owners and not tenants, because large companies like AH must also contribute. Moreover, the property tax is not high. For small local entrepreneurs, we might consider an exemption based on turnover so they do not get into trouble.

229579.explanation: BIJ1 wants to eliminate private ownership of properties. Therefore, it is good if the municipality buys up buildings and makes them available to the community for public purposes like libraries, museums, cultural spaces, cooperative enterprises, and neighborhood supermarkets.

229580.explanation: Amsterdam is an activist shareholder and, with a 20% stake, pushes for the reduction of the airport. This is very important due to Schiphol's high emissions and nuisance. This share should not decrease but rather increase.

229581.explanation: Amsterdam should not do business with Israeli companies that profit from occupation and apartheid. Public money should not support colonial oppression.

229583.explanation: As far as we are concerned, Amsterdam will become a Sanctuary City that actively stands against the criminalization of people and the penalization of aid to undocumented migrants.

229584.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit must be able to claim emergency housing when homelessness threatens, also to reduce pressure on shelters - despite strict legislation from The Hague.

229585.explanation: Children of labor migrants go to school here. Parents must be able to talk with teachers and other institutions. If people lose their jobs, speaking Dutch helps find new work. Currently, language lessons are mostly provided by volunteers; that is not enough.

229586.explanation: We support accommodating asylum seekers. Especially now that the political climate is hardening and becoming more authoritarian, we must stand for solidarity, human rights, and protection for people fleeing violence and oppression.

229587.explanation: We support more frequent and periodic bulky waste collection. Making an appointment is a barrier for many people and actually causes more illegal waste on the street. Regular collection is more accessible and reduces nuisance.

229588.explanation: There must be more public city toilets, and we want all our city toilets to be free, gender-neutral, and wheelchair accessible. This also helps women, homeless people, and children who currently have reduced access to toilets in the center.

229589.explanation: We want fewer events by big organizers (e.g., KKR) who have no ties to residents and organize only for profit. But events by and for local residents themselves we support, of course with current rules against nuisance and safety issues.

229590.explanation: We are against private payment for household waste processing. Those costs should not be borne by residents, but much more by the companies that produce packaging and make profits. That is fairer and encourages less waste.

229591.explanation: Keeping animals locked up for entertainment is outdated. Education about animals is important, but that can be done in other ways. We would rather see Artis close and the park become publicly accessible again.

229592.explanation: Cruise ships are extremely polluting, emitting large amounts of CO₂ and fine dust, burdening the city, climate, and health, while contributing little to the local community.

229593.explanation: We disagree with a general ban. Removing parking spaces for trees or greenery may be necessary for livability and climate. This requires customized solutions per district, not a rigid rule for the whole city.

229594.explanation: The Lutkemeer polder is an important area for food production and nature. We support the residents' initiative Food Park Amsterdam, which wants to create a place where food, nature, and connections are central.

229595.explanation: We agree with a wind farm in the Western Port Area, provided it meets all standards and prioritizes health and noise nuisance for local residents. We do not support wind turbines in North, Nieuw-West, or Southeast, and there is no space elsewhere.

229596.explanation: There is an overabundance of sports clubs with expensive memberships. We want every Amsterdammer to be able to use them, even if you have a low income.

229597.explanation: Local media are the backbone of democracy. Neighborhood media makers know what is happening, know the districts, and provide space for voices that would otherwise not be heard. We want more funding for independent local journalists and media makers.

229598.explanation: Top sports events mainly provide short-lived spectacles, while putting extra pressure on our already overloaded infrastructure. That money can be better invested sustainably in sports facilities that are permanent and accessible to people with disabilities.

229599.explanation: Cultural background must be taken into account for cultural subsidies because makers with a non-Western background structurally receive fewer opportunities. Active correction is needed. At least 20% of local cultural subsidies should go to creators from marginalized groups.

229600.explanation: It is better to postpone the choice of an educational track as long as possible because this increases equal opportunities and we want to actively encourage this.

229601.explanation: This increases equal opportunities, supports parents, and gives every child a good start.

229602.explanation: A large part of the Sloterplas will be filled in for this new construction, while water in our city is essential to combat heat caused by the climate crisis. Much green space will be lost, which is already very scarce in our city.

229603.explanation: More funding for sports and physical activity is needed because people in vulnerable positions exercise the least and are less healthy. Memberships are expensive and there are too few team sports clubs for young people. Targeted funding can reach them effectively.

229604.explanation: We advocate for fewer public enforcement officers (boa's) in the city and more investment in education, healthcare, and housing. Prevention and social provisions provide safer neighborhoods in the long term, whereas extra boa’s mostly offer symptom management and repression.

229605.explanation: We do not want undercover police officers. Street harassment is a problem rooted in toxic male culture and misogyny. Police officers are part of the problem, not the solution. A cultural change is needed to make real progress in combating harassment and violence.

229606.explanation: We disagree with equipping boa’s with batons and pepper spray. This increases the risk of violence combined with ethnic profiling. Investments in prevention, education, and social provisions are more effective for safety.

229607.explanation: The residency criterion causes an increase in street vending, which we want to counteract.

229608.explanation: We advocate for less municipal camera surveillance because it infringes on people’s privacy. It discourages the dumping of bulky waste, which may then be dumped elsewhere.

229609.explanation: Every civil servant should be free to dress as they wish, including while on the job.

229610.explanation: This rule targets Muslims who wear a burqa or niqab; we do not participate in Islamophobia. During the corona pandemic, having our face covered was not a problem. We advocate that the municipality does not enforce this rule.

229611.explanation: The sale of social rental homes worsens the housing shortage and makes affordable housing increasingly difficult. Securing a stable stock of social housing is essential to meet demand and support vulnerable groups in our society.

229612.explanation: To counter gentrification, we want people who already live here and have their community to have a better chance at housing.

229613.explanation: Buildings should remain public property; we prefer the municipality to assign uses to these buildings that benefit residents.

229614.explanation: Currently, real estate investors have too much influence on what is built, which means, for example, that social housing quotas are not met.

229615.explanation: There are now strict rules to combat holiday rentals, which do not need to become stricter. We also do not want holiday rentals to spread further beyond the city center.

229616.explanation: Many elderly people remain in too-large homes because moving is a major financial challenge. The municipality must assist as much as possible so that large houses become available for families again.

229617.explanation: The 40% norm has not been met for years. To catch up, that norm must rise to 100%.

229618.explanation: This rule is important to prevent speculation on the housing market. Housing is a basic need, not a product for sale.

229619.explanation: Because the municipality charges ground rent, it has a say in how building is done and can better represent residents’ interests.

229620.explanation: The current maximum causes problems for people on social assistance. The social assistance itself must also be increased to enable a dignified existence.

229621.explanation: This keeps pets healthy and helps people in vulnerable positions care for their animals without financial stress.

229622.explanation: Many more places are needed where homeless people can spend the night, with special attention to LGBTQI+ persons who are often extra vulnerable and need safe shelter.

229623.explanation: We believe that people with a minimum income should not have to pay the deductible of the health insurance themselves. This reduces financial pressure and ensures that necessary care remains accessible to everyone.

229624.explanation: Precisely more investment in people with low incomes is needed to combat poverty, increase opportunities, and prevent social problems in the long term.

229625.explanation: We are against mandatory social service; volunteering must remain voluntary to prevent exploitation.

229626.explanation: Waiving waste disposal charges for residents with low incomes is important to fight poverty and ensure everyone can keep disposing of their waste.

229627.explanation: There are many complexities with a basic income: because benefits and allowances stop, people can actually end up worse off financially. A pilot can determine the conditions for introducing a basic income so that everyone benefits.

229628.explanation: Free public transport makes travel possible for everyone, reduces car traffic and pollution, improves air quality and safety, and stimulates the local economy.

229629.explanation: People under 18 do not have their own income (or receive a shamefully low youth wage). Free public transport ensures everyone can travel to school and activities, reduces car traffic, promotes safety and health, and enables participation in sports and culture for all young people.

229630.explanation: More parking spaces are needed for volunteers of social organizations, especially outside the ring road. This is important for people with disabilities and makes volunteering more accessible.

229631.explanation: Eliminating metro line 53 causes an extra transfer and poorer accessibility. This line must be retained.
