229629.explanation: De Vonk wants Amsterdam public transport to be free and extensive. The priority for new routes lies with the needs of Amsterdammers, such as Metro 53, not those of tourists. De Vonk will pay for this by increasing tourist tax and real estate tax.

229630.explanation: De Vonk would like to see Amsterdammers stand up for each other, and a compensation with free parking hours seems like a good first step. Additionally, De Vonk wants to invest an extra 15 million euros per year in community centers and neighborhood collectives, because they make the city.

229631.explanation: The city government is neglecting the Bijlmer with the demolition of line 53. The Bijlmer is always forgotten and overlooked. The lifts and escalators are often broken and repairs take a long time. De Vonk demands focus on and respect for the Bijlmer. De Vonk wants free and extensive public transport. The opposite of this dismantling!

229632.explanation: Our beautiful Amsterdam should belong to all Amsterdammers, not just the canal belt elite who can afford the outrageously expensive parking spaces there. On the other hand, public transport must be free and improved!

229633.explanation: De Vonk wants Amsterdam's public transport to be free and expanded. The priority for new connections lies with the needs of Amsterdammers, not tourists. Think of preserving Metro 53, a metro connection between Central and Sloterdijk, and exploring an East-West line.

229634.explanation: Amsterdam should belong to all Amsterdammers, not just the wealthy elite. Fewer cars in the inner city will make our center healthy and accessible again, but only with free and extensive public transport. Exceptions to the low-car policy are residents, essential professions, and people with disabilities.

229635.explanation: Our beautiful Amsterdam should belong to all Amsterdammers, not just the wealthy elite who sit in traffic jams with their SUVs in front of De Bijenkorf. Fewer cars in the inner city will make our center healthy and accessible again. Exceptions are essential professions and people with disabilities.

229636.explanation: De Vonk supports the policy of the cyclists' union: a maximum speed of 30 km/h on all roads without separate bike lanes. The cyclists' union itself says that on roads with separate bike lanes, a speed limit of 50 km/h can be safe. We are happy to coordinate this per case with residents and experts.

229637.explanation: The interests of sex workers must always come first, regardless of people's opinions about sex work. If the windows are closed, this mainly compromises the safety of sex workers, because on the Wallen mutual solidarity has been built between sex workers and the community.

229639.explanation: A care community center is a fantastic form of housing where elderly people can enjoy their old age with dignity. Moreover, it offers a solution to the increasing loneliness among Amsterdammers.

229640.explanation: 'Care cowboys' with substandard care are lining their pockets. That must stop. Young people have the right to good care and increasingly rely on it. The municipality has an important role in guaranteeing good youth care and must take as much control as possible over quality.

229641.explanation: Tailored care also means that people have the freedom to choose a care provider that suits them. According to De Vonk, this is a right as long as it can be reasonably facilitated. It must not lead to the postponement of necessary care.

229642.explanation: De Vonk wants youth centers for and by young people themselves! There are far too few places in the city for young people and their mental health is poor. De Vonk wants to invest in these young people. Youth centers are a great way to do that.

229643.explanation: About 1 in 5 children in Amsterdam grows up in poverty. Some children go to school without breakfast. That is unacceptable and unnecessary in a wealthy city like Amsterdam. A school breakfast offers a great solution for these children and benefits their academic performance.

229644.explanation: Fish are animals with feelings. They also experience pain and fear. Sport fishing should therefore not be allowed everywhere in Amsterdam, because animals are not there to please humans.

229570.explanation: We support more input and involvement of residents in municipal policy. Citizens' assemblies can help with this. However, we believe this must not lead to delays in important matters, such as housing construction. Participation is important but must remain practical and effective.

229571.explanation: Discrimination and racism increase inequality in the city. We think appointing someone responsible for this is a good idea. In addition, we believe the city should be designed so that people with disabilities can move independently and safely.

229572.explanation: District councils are a suitable body to identify and address problems in specific neighborhoods and areas in a timely manner. They are close to the residents and know the local situation, so solutions better match what is actually needed.

229573.explanation: We advocate for a national slavery memorial day including a public holiday.

229574.explanation: Amsterdam has one of the lowest property taxes (OZB) in the whole of the Netherlands. We support doubling the property tax for homes valued over €600,000.

229575.explanation: We want to increase the tourist tax to reduce the number of (budget) tourists. This extra income should flow directly back to the Amsterdammers, for example, through free public transport within the municipality for Amsterdammers.

229576.explanation: A metropolis like Amsterdam deserves a lively and diverse nightlife. Extending opening hours can contribute to this, provided it is balanced with livability and safety for residents.

229577.explanation: We believe money would be better spent on keeping tourists out.

229578.explanation: The owners will pass the costs on to the users anyway.

229579.explanation: The municipality has other tools to regulate the retail offer in the inner city.

229580.explanation: We find it important to maintain input in decision-making around Schiphol. This aligns with our intention to advocate for relocating Schiphol to the North Sea, with attention to livability, environment, and future-proof solutions.

229581.explanation: The municipality should not do business with any company that does business with a foreign military.

229583.explanation: Rejected asylum seekers often disappear into illegality when they lose contact with authorities. We therefore support an approach where supervision and guidance are maintained, so people are not left to their fate and social problems are prevented.

229584.explanation: Although the housing crisis is not the fault of status holders, we believe the priority arrangement should stop. Through temporary housing and personal responsibility for finding a home, they are encouraged to become independent more quickly and integrate better.

229585.explanation: When migrant workers want to learn Dutch, the municipality must actively facilitate this. Language skills increase self-reliance, improve their position in the labor market, and contribute to better integration in the city.

229586.explanation: Amsterdam should put more effort into temporary housing, for example by using empty office buildings. Status holders can be temporarily housed there, after which they should find a home through the regular process once they obtain a residence permit.

229587.explanation: This is impractical. Large waste is often already removed by others from the street, especially on designated bulky waste days. As a result, the garbage truck may come for nothing because the waste has already been taken away.

229588.explanation: We support more public toilets in the city for women, men, and people with disabilities.

229589.explanation: Many neighborhood residents experience nuisance from the large number of festivals in Amsterdam. There are now many events that can also take place at other locations without diminishing the city's nightlife and cultural life.

229590.explanation: It is illogical to change the household waste system to a model where paying more means producing more waste. In practice, this system already largely works this way, because households with multiple residents at one address pay more waste tax.

229591.explanation: Artis is part of the city's cultural heritage.

229592.explanation: We support reducing the number of tourists. Large cruise ships bring thousands of visitors to the city at once and put a lot of pressure on Amsterdam. We do not want to ban river cruises because they have a different, more small-scale character.

229593.explanation: We believe nature must not be sacrificed for parking space and vice versa.

229594.explanation: We support the development of housing in the Lutkemeerpolder and oppose the establishment of an industrial park.

229595.explanation: We support the installation of more wind turbines. We prefer small-scale projects with small wind turbines that fit within the city. These can be realized together with residents, so they directly benefit as co-owners and by purchasing electricity.

229596.explanation: We support reimbursing sports clubs for people on minimum incomes. Additionally, we want more sports facilities in public parks and squares, with sports equipment for young and old. This promotes meeting each other, exercising together, and strengthens social cohesion in the neighborhood.

229597.explanation: Our local media play an important role in the city, including providing residents with accessible and reliable information.

229598.explanation: Amsterdam hosts many major sporting events, such as the marathon. At such times, the city fills up with visitors, while we actually strive for more calm and balance in the city.

229599.explanation: We believe that everyone deserves equal treatment.

229600.explanation: We support the system with a three-year preparatory class. If schools need extra resources for this, it makes sense for them to request these from the national government, not from the municipality.

229601.explanation: Preschool is important for children aged 2 to 4 because it contributes to their socialization and development. Additionally, it promotes the participation of Amsterdam mothers, for example through better opportunities for work and education.

229602.explanation: We do not consider relocating the theater a priority for the city or for Osdorp.

229604.explanation: More enforcement officers does not mean more safety.

229605.explanation: The municipality must actively work to improve safety for women on the streets.

229607.explanation: To combat street sales, coffeeshops should be able to continue selling soft drugs to tourists so that sales disappear from the streetscape. Additionally, we want Amsterdam to participate in the cannabis experiment to further regulate the chain and reduce crime.

229608.explanation: We do not see increased camera surveillance at underground waste containers as a good solution. It seems like a waste of public money and does not address the problem at its core.

229609.explanation: Enforcement officers have a neutral role within public administration. Visible expressions of religion do not align with that neutrality and therefore do not belong in the exercise of their duties.

229610.explanation: Face-covering clothing does not belong in government buildings. Open and recognizable communication is essential there for safety, accessibility, and mutual trust.

229611.explanation: We are against the sale of social rental homes.

229612.explanation: People connected to the city who have lived here for at least four years should have priority over people with more points but who live outside the city.

229613.explanation: This concerns individual cases.

229614.explanation: The municipality should use as much land as possible for housing development.

229615.explanation: Vacation rentals of homes cause nuisance and put extra pressure on the housing market.

229616.explanation: This scheme already exists and we want to maintain it. We support a wide range of senior housing to make it easy for people to move.

229617.explanation: We support the principle of 40-40-20.

229618.explanation: Homes are for living in, not as investments.

229620.explanation: It is important that people distanced from the labor market return to work; a generous arrangement can contribute to returning to full employment without benefits.

229621.explanation: We advocate for a municipal pet insurance with clear conditions, such as a maximum insurable amount and a limit of one pet per household within the insurance.

229622.explanation: We support a humane approach to the homeless.

229623.explanation: The payment of the deductible should run directly through the municipality. If a resident does not utilize healthcare, this amount should flow back to the municipality instead of to the individual.

229624.explanation: We support reducing income inequality.

229625.explanation: Not everyone on welfare is able to do social work. For those who can work, the municipality should provide support towards suitable work instead of imposing obligations that are not feasible for everyone.

229626.explanation: This increases income inequality.

229627.explanation: We support a premium for Amsterdammers because life has become unaffordable for many. A pilot of a basic income fits well within our vision.

229628.explanation: We want to increase the mobility of Amsterdammers. This contributes to greater self-reliance, reduces loneliness, and strengthens social cohesion in the city.

229629.explanation: We want free public transport for all Amsterdammers.

229630.explanation: Social organizations should not be burdened by high parking costs for volunteers and employees. This also applies to social enterprises and schools, which must remain able to carry out their work for the city accessibly and feasibly.

229631.explanation: We find the accessibility of Amsterdam Southeast very important.

229632.explanation: We are neither for more parking spaces nor for fewer.

229633.explanation: We support (even) better accessibility to Schiphol Airport.

229634.explanation: For people with disabilities and emergency services, the inner city must remain accessible by car. Additionally, we want to reduce the number of garbage trucks and delivery vans by bundling loads per neighborhood, so that there is one parcel carrier per neighborhood per day, operated by a parcel service/waste collector.

229635.explanation: The Amsterdam city center already has some car-free streets. Expanding these is not an end in itself for us, but we are also not opposed where it is appropriate and practical.

229636.explanation: On roads with separate bike lanes, we support raising the maximum speed limit for cars, provided this can be done safely and responsibly.

229637.explanation: Despite the many abuses in window prostitution, a regulated and controlled location is better than illegal prostitution without supervision and protection.

229639.explanation: Such residential places contribute to the turnover of social rental housing in Amsterdam.

229641.explanation: Appropriate and reliable care is essential. The patient must have trust in and with their healthcare provider.

229642.explanation: Youth centers play an important role in the city.

229643.explanation: No child should go to school without breakfast. Facilitating this at school can prevent children from starting lessons on an empty stomach.

229570.explanation: Democracy does not stop at elections. Citizens' assemblies give residents real influence, especially those who are normally not heard. Provided they are representatively organized and followed up clearly, not just token consultation.

229571.explanation: Discrimination is structural, not an incident. Without central coordination, it disappears into separate projects. A coordinator ensures responsibility, monitoring, and consistent policy.

229572.explanation: Power should be closer to residents, not further away. City districts are crucial for local democracy and tailor-made solutions. Improve them, but do not abolish them.

229573.explanation: Slavery was state violence. Commemoration is not a favor but recognition. The municipality must set an example and take responsibility by giving civil servants this public holiday.

229574.explanation: We are in favor of differentiation. Low incomes must be protected, but a generic property tax (OZB) reduction becomes a loophole for the wealthy to contribute less. Fair tax policy centers on ability to pay, not avoidance for the rich.

229575.explanation: Amsterdam is not a cash cow. Those who tax the city contribute to livability, enforcement, and public services for residents.

229576.explanation: Hospitality businesses should be given space where appropriate. Outside hotspots, quiet should prevail. This requires tailor-made solutions with courage: economy and nightlife belong in a world city.

229577.explanation: Amsterdam should remain visible as a city of culture, history, and justice. What must stop is mass-tourism hype and limitless city marketing. Public money should be focused on quality, spreading visitors, and respect for residents, not more crowding.

229578.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is formally paid by owners but often passed on to tenants and small entrepreneurs. PvM chooses a differentiated OZB: higher contributions from large and speculative owners and rents, protection against passing on costs for small ones, so burdens do not fall on those least able to bear them.

229579.explanation: The city is not an investment object. Municipal ownership prevents vacancy, displacement, and monoculture in the center.

229580.explanation: Public shareholding provides influence on climate, health, and working conditions. Selling means giving away power. We choose democratic control, stricter conditions, and less growth, not distancing.

229581.explanation: Human rights are non-negotiable. The municipality must not support companies involved in war crimes.

229583.explanation: No one should have to sleep on the street. Shelter is humane and prevents larger social problems.

229584.explanation: Blaming status holders as scapegoats is unjust and dangerous. They have often lost everything. The real problem is years of failing housing policy. Solidarity and justice are not luxuries, but obligations.

229585.explanation: Labor migrants build this city but are too often exploited. Language education provides protection: against abuse, against dependency, for dignity and future prospects.

229586.explanation: Humanity cannot be captured in quotas. Amsterdam grew through solidarity. Shelter is not a favor but a moral responsibility of a wealthy city.

229587.explanation: Not everyone can schedule additional agreements. Bulky waste days are necessary. Policy must connect with real lives, or else chaos and fines increase for those already under pressure.

229588.explanation: Without accessible toilets, the city is not for everyone. This is about dignity, independence, and equal participation. It is no luxury, it is civilization.

229589.explanation: Parks belong to the city and its residents. Not less life, but better policy. PvM opposes large-scale commercial exploitation of parks, but supports culture, commemoration, and neighborhood initiatives. The problem is commercialization, not social encounters.

229590.explanation: This especially affects families and people with little space or money. Sustainability without social justice is false policy. PvM chooses fair waste policy, not punishment.

229591.explanation: Artis has educational value but must change accordingly. Public support should go hand in hand with animal welfare, education, and future-oriented thinking about human-nature relationships.

229592.explanation: Cruise ships cause extreme pollution and bring little benefit to the city. Amsterdam is not a backdrop for quick consumption, but a living community.

229593.explanation: In some neighborhoods the shortage is already acute. Green space is important, but so is accessibility. PvM chooses balance and neighborhood-oriented policy, not symbolic politics.

229594.explanation: The earth is no reserve land. Fertile farmland is life, not a leftover. PvM chooses a holistic vision in which food, nature, and the future weigh more than short-term gain.

229595.explanation: In Southeast, residents have clearly spoken out against wind turbines. This district already faces unique social and spatial pressure incomparable to the port area. The energy transition requires justice and listening to residents.

229596.explanation: In times of poverty, sports are seen as a luxury, while people on low incomes actually experience more stress, worse health, and faster dropout. Free sports is not a miracle cure, but an essential tool. This should be standard in every neighborhood team conversation.

229597.explanation: Local media keep power visible and communities connected. Without them, stories from neighborhoods disappear, especially those of people without lobby. Democracy does not live only in h

229598.explanation: Top sports events mainly bring mass tourism, crowds, and high costs. The benefits for residents are limited. PvM chooses to invest in local sports: squares, schools, and clubs where everyone can participate.

229599.explanation: Equal opportunities require recognition of inequality. Pretending everyone starts equally is an illusion. Recognizing cultural background is necessary to correct historical exclusion. PvM chooses justice, not color-blind policies that maintain inequality.

229600.explanation: Early selection breaks children down. A longer first year in secondary school provides space to grow, make mistakes, and discover talent. This is proven effective policy and deserves extra support, especially in neighborhoods where opportunities are unequally distributed.

229601.explanation: Every child deserves a fair start. Language, play, and safety in the early years make a lifelong difference. Free preschool is not a cost but a loving investment in our children and the city’s future.

229602.explanation: Culture should not be only in the center. Nieuw-West deserves strong facilities like the Meervaart, just as Southeast has a right to strengthening through expansion of the Bijlmer Park Theatre. Culture is recognition and development.

229603.explanation: Sports is preventive care. It prevents illness, loneliness, and dropout. Every euro invested in sports saves later healthcare costs. PvM chooses movement in the neighborhood, not only in elite clubs.

229604.explanation: More enforcement without trust leads to repression. History teaches us that control often unfairly impacts especially Black and marginalized communities. PvM chooses safety through care, youth work, and community, not through escalating control.

229605.explanation: Street intimidation is a serious problem, but solving it with undercover police increases mistrust and arbitrariness. Organizations like Controle Alt Delete show that prevention, education, and cultural change are more effective and fairer.

229606.explanation: Arming increases escalation and the risk of ethnic profiling. Experiences show enforcement is applied unequally. Safety requires de-escalation, training, and clear boundaries, not extra weapons.

229607.explanation: Exclusion leads to street vending, insecurity, and more crime. Regulation works better than prohibition. This is about health, safety, and realistic policy.

229608.explanation: Extra camera surveillance means more data collection without proven effect. Clean streets are achieved with good facilities, clear rules, and practical solutions, not by continuously monitoring residents.

229609.explanation: Neutrality lies in behavior, not appearance. This proposal excludes people and suggests religion is incompatible with public service. PvM stands for an inclusive government that reflects its city.

229610.explanation: Collective exclusion based on appearance harms freedom and trust. Safety requires tailored approaches and respect, not symbolic politics that primarily stigmatize one group.

229611.explanation: Social housing is not a commodity. Every sold home increases the shortage. Preservation and expansion are necessary for basic security.

229612.explanation: Binding requirements can be fair as long as they do not primarily benefit higher incomes. PvM supports local ties as a tool, provided it is socially designed and does not exclude vulnerable groups.

229613.explanation: Municipal real estate can be used for housing, care, youth, and culture. Selling it is short-term thinking that removes future space.

229614.explanation: Public land is public power. The municipality must focus on affordability, sustainability, and community, not on profits for developers.

229615.explanation: Housing takes precedence over profit. Vacation rentals displace residents and make neighborhoods unlivable. The city center is a living place, not a hotel.

229616.explanation: Flow-through only works voluntarily. Elderly people deserve respect and support, not pressure. This helps both them and young housing seekers.

229617.explanation: PvM wants to go further: at least 50% social, 50% middle-income, and 0% expensive, at least temporarily to reverse the crisis. The city is already expensive enough. Housing is a right.

229618.explanation: Young people sometimes live at home involuntarily for more than ten years due to the housing crisis. Homes are for living in, not for speculation. Owner-occupancy requirements protect neighborhoods and give housing seekers perspective.

229619.explanation: Ground lease requires differentiation. It must be affordable for low and middle incomes, heavier for large and speculative interests. PvM opts for social reform of ground lease, not abolition that increases inequality.

229620.explanation: People on welfare often want to work but are punished if they take initiative. Earning extra income provides breathing room, self-respect, and a step towards independence without fear of repayment.

229621.explanation: Pets are family to some residents, especially for those with little social network. No one should have to choose between food or care for an animal. This is basic security with humanity.

229622.explanation: Homelessness is not caused by policy alone, but also by mental and social breakdowns. Precisely for this reason, our society must be social enough to provide timely shelter. More shelter means more care, more recovery, and more humanity.

229623.explanation: The deductible is a barrier to care. People postpone care and become sicker. That is inhumane and more expensive in the long run. Care must be accessible.

229624.explanation: People with low incomes already bear daily stress, shame, and uncertainty. Cuts deepen that pain and increase inequality. Those who invest in people invest in a city that leaves no one behind.

229625.explanation: People on welfare are often in a vulnerable phase. They deserve trust and the benefit of the doubt. Socially meaningful work should be an opportunity, not an obligation. Recovery requires space, not coercion.

229626.explanation: This affects people who need every euro. You don’t solve poverty by raising fixed costs. Justice requires tailored solutions.

229627.explanation: A basic income provides peace, confidence, and space to work on recovery and participation. Trials show people do not stop working but function better.

229628.explanation: PvM wants 100% free public transport. Amsterdam must fully commit to this and lobby in The Hague. Mobility is a basic right, not a market product. Free public transport connects the city and increases equality.

229629.explanation: PvM has campaigned for free public transport for children during holidays and free transport up to age 14. We support extending this to all children up to 18. Every child deserves free access to school, sports, and friends.

229630.explanation: Volunteers are the quiet pillars of the city. They provide where systems fall short. Make their work easier, not more expensive. This is not a favor but recognition.

229631.explanation: Metro Line 53 is the lifeline of this part of Southeast. Lines 50 and 54 run on the other side; 53 is the direct link to Central Station. Protests against this have lasted for years. Accessibility is equality.

229632.explanation: Fewer cars can help, but not at the expense of residents, caregivers, healthcare providers, and accessibility. Policy must fit people dependent on cars, not only ideals.

229633.explanation: If there is money for expansion, it must first go to affordable public transport and free transport for children up to 18. Otherwise, you increase inequality: better transport for those who can pay, while others are left behind.

229634.explanation: Large families, people with disabilities, caregivers, and multigenerational households sometimes need a car. Accessibility is also a matter of care and inclusion.

229635.explanation: Car-free streets can be livable, but policy must consider people with mobility issues, families with young children, and care situations. Inclusion requires tailored solutions.

229636.explanation: Where 50 km/h now applies, this often occurs on main roads with bicycle paths. Traffic safety requires clear choices. Lower speeds save lives without unnecessarily slowing the city.

229637.explanation: Banning sex work pushes sex workers into unsafe situations. Rights, safety, and freedom of choice must be central, not moralizing.

229639.explanation: The elderly must be able to grow old in their own neighborhood, with care nearby and people around them. This strengthens dignity, cohesion, and caregiving.

229640.explanation: Children are not dossiers or profit models. Structural failures must have consequences. Safety and quality always take precedence over contracts.

229641.explanation: Care works better when people feel seen and understood. Culturally appropriate care increases trust and effectiveness. That is good care.

229642.explanation: Young people need safe places to grow, meet, and be themselves. Prevention and proximity work better than after-the-fact control.

229643.explanation: No child may go to school hungry. School breakfast is simple, humane, and effective. Equal opportunities start with a full stomach.

229644.explanation: Recreation and nature must be balanced. PvM stands for accessibility, with respect for ecology and the environment.

229570.explanation: The municipality must cooperate much more directly with residents and entrepreneurs. Previous participation projects and citizen councils have failed. Making policy differently requires the 10-point plan as in our program: https://namensamsterdammers.nl/bestuur-organisatie-en-samenwerking/

229571.explanation: Combating discrimination and racism is an important responsibility of every alderman, every manager, and all civil servants together. With a coordinator, everyone looks to that person instead of doing something themselves.

229572.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers proposes not to work with an outdated meeting circus but with new district councils and an area-based approach. See our program: https://namensamsterdammers.nl/bestuur-organisatie-en-samenwerking/

229573.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers wants Keti Koti to be a day off once every five years, like May 5th.

229574.explanation: The municipality will have to cut its own costs and not burden citizens more and more.

229575.explanation: Tourists are not a cash cow for a municipality overwhelmed with debts. The municipality will have to cut its own costs and not increasingly burden citizens, tourists, or entrepreneurs.

229576.explanation: A world city deserves worldly hospitality. Street nuisance must be tackled strictly.

229577.explanation: The municipality must make cuts to itself and does not need to hire civil servants for everything again.

229578.explanation: This measure is totally unrealistic and contradicts the current system. The municipality should simply stop raising the property tax (ozb) repeatedly; that is the problem.

229579.explanation: The municipality should expand the proven success of the NV Zeedijk method together with new financially strong partners such as pension funds. And show a collaborative attitude towards well-intentioned city center entrepreneurs.

229580.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., there is a POSSIBILITY to sell PART of the shares exclusively for the purpose of: opening Sixhaven station and/or extending the Metroring West to Central Station or Sixhaven. See also https://namensamsterdammers.nl/financien/

229581.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want to stop having a municipality that thinks it is the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. And with all those civil servants who have to monitor and sanction companies.

229583.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want to stop sheltering undocumented migrants and repurpose the locations used for this to house seekers. For unemployed (EU) labor migrants, Amsterdam's assistance is limited to supporting their return.

229584.explanation: The system with urgency declarations for home seekers works unfairly. It means that the 31-year-old cleaner will never be eligible for housing in Amsterdam because he does have a job and no problems. See also https://namensamsterdammers.nl/wonen/

229585.explanation: That is the responsibility of the employer.

229586.explanation: It is national policy and leads to people staying on cruise ships in the harbor. This must be addressed nationally and we are a local party.

229587.explanation: A completely new approach is needed. On behalf of Amsterdammers., we propose working with an area-oriented approach. See our program: https://namensamsterdammers.nl/bestuur-organisatie-en-samenwerking/

229588.explanation: But certainly not only in the city center. Especially the squares outside the center also need toilets. See also https://namensamsterdammers.nl/een-schone-en-leefbare-stad/

229589.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want to halve the number of large events and festivals in city parks, as well as the municipal events office. The recreational function of city parks and the quality of nature and greenery must be central again. See also: https://namensamsterdammers.nl/economie-en-werk/

229590.explanation: This leads to even more nuisance. During our weekly neighborhood visits, we already hear stories about 'waste tourism,' residents from other municipalities where you have to pay who dump their rubbish here.

229591.explanation: Artis is an indispensable facility in the city for both education and recreation.

229592.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want to focus on quality tourism. Cruise ships carry wealthy tourists who behave properly, so they are welcome. Moreover, 30 million has just been invested in green shore power for cruise ships on the IJ. Do not waste that investment.

229593.explanation: Trees are in poor condition or even dead. Removing parking spaces important to residents for trees that are not maintained or even dying is undesirable and even pointless according to On behalf of Amsterdammers. Those who want more greenery should start with better maintenance.

229594.explanation: At this moment, it is not possible to estimate whether this is feasible or how much it would cost.

229595.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we do not want wind turbines outside the port area. And therefore not at the Noorder IJplas either. See also https://namensamsterdammers.nl/een-schone-en-leefbare-stad/

229596.explanation: Amsterdam actually does too much income policy, see summary at https://namensamsterdammers.nl/armoede-schulden-en-ongelijkheid/. This leads to the infamous poverty trap and higher taxes for other Amsterdammers.

229597.explanation: Local media are important for local democracy.

229598.explanation: The municipality must make cuts to itself and does not need to hire civil servants for everything again.

229599.explanation: Cultural background always plays a role, even if you do not want it to.

229600.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we are against reintroducing the Middle School. We want fewer labels and to focus on the talent of each child, so schools that reintroduce this with a three-year bridge class should not be financially rewarded for it. Naturally, gifted children will then also receive the appropriate education that is often still lacking now.

229601.explanation: Nothing is free. The so-called free preschool is paid from your tax money, and a tax increase for Amsterdammers is then necessary. On behalf of Amsterdammers., we oppose this because too many extra facilities are already offered this way in Amsterdam.

229602.explanation: A new theater may be built, but not in the middle of Sloterplas.

229603.explanation: Sports and exercise are very important, and the municipality can collaborate much more with partners in the field instead of doing everything itself.

229604.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we consider enforcement very important. That can be done through public order officers (boa's) or through the police.

229605.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we find tackling antisocial street behavior very important but want to make choices in consultation with the police.

229606.explanation: No blurring of the roles of the police and the boa's. Moreover, using instruments of force requires training like that of police officers.

229607.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want to introduce the resident criterion. This will have a certain waterbed effect that the police must strictly enforce. But police research shows that it reduces drug tourism. Moreover, it limits the undesirable 'everything is allowed in Amsterdam' feeling.

229608.explanation: Then we would fill the whole city with cameras because our weekly neighborhood visits reveal it happens everywhere. More enforcement is needed and a change in behavior. Therefore, On behalf of Amsterdammers. wants a neighborhood-oriented approach. See our program: https://namensamsterdammers.nl/bestuur-organisatie-

229609.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we strongly value the separation of church/mosque and state. This is a condition to enable coexistence in a diverse city, as well as the basic principles of freedom of expression and non-discrimination. See also https://namensamsterdammers.nl/veiligheid/

229610.explanation: The municipality should simply ask people to show their faces.

229611.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we actually want a buying pilot for 500 sitting tenants. Many tenants already pay 1000 or 1500 euros rent monthly, but they are not allowed to buy for that amount because banks consider the risk too high. We want to tackle that.

229612.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we want a priority scheme for native Amsterdammers. They have hardly qualified for housing for years. This justifies a catch-up. And it is very important for social cohesion in neighborhoods. See https://namensamsterdammers.nl/wonen/

229613.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers., we propose selling non-strategic real estate. There is a large maintenance backlog, and the municipality proves unable to manage so many buildings properly. The proceeds will be used to repay municipal debt. See https://namensamsterdammers.nl/financien/

229614.explanation: It is already evident that various municipal proposals for area development are economically unfeasible. Here too, cooperation and consultation with area developers and housing corporations are very important.

229615.explanation: It is already limited to 14 days per year; that is enough.

229616.explanation: The municipality must better align the existing regulation with the interests and needs of the elderly. During our neighborhood visits, On behalf of Amsterdammers. spoke with elderly people who strangely want to move but are not allowed to take their parking permit with them. See https://namensamsterdammers.nl/wonen/

229617.explanation: Amsterdam must build diverse neighborhoods, of which a part (social rent or purchase, mid-range rent) is truly affordable. And that part is not automatically 40%. See https://namensamsterdammers.nl/wonen/

229618.explanation: Vacancy must be prevented, but if someone goes abroad for a year, it is perfectly fine for someone else to live there. Moreover, the statement is unclear because it leads to unforeseen consequences.

229619.explanation: In fact, the path to abolition has already been set in motion, and now increasing inequality is emerging between those who could make use of the perpetual leasehold arrangement and those who could not.

229620.explanation: Striving for work so that you are less dependent is better than structurally being on social assistance and then being allowed to earn some extra money from the municipality (a patriarchal attitude).

229621.explanation: This is policy for the national government, which is already working on this. We wish all pet owners that the national government ensures affordable veterinarians.

229622.explanation: On behalf of Amsterdammers, we support the creation of new small boarding homes where people with misunderstood behavior can live under supervision. We hold the cold winter shelter in warm regard.

229623.explanation: Amsterdam actually does too much income policy, see summary at https://namensamsterdammers.nl/armoede-schulden-en-ongelijkheid/. This leads to the infamous poverty trap and higher taxes for other Amsterdammers.

229624.explanation: Of course, we help people with a low income, but it is not necessarily about more or less money. What do you do with that money? Do you help people solve problems or do you give away money that they benefit from temporarily but not structurally?

229625.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net and not a trade-off.

229626.explanation: The broad range of regulations the municipality has regarding income policy must be reviewed. See the list at https://namensamsterdammers.nl/armoede-schulden-en-ongelijkheid/

229627.explanation: Free money does not exist. The money the government distributes comes from your tax cents. There is no money tree that will produce the money for the basic income.

229628.explanation: Nothing is free. A government service must be compensated.

229629.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers considers sports and exercise very important. With an eye on good development of fitness and muscles, we advocate attention to cycling especially at a young age.

229630.explanation: It is clear that the current parking policy disproportionately burdens volunteers. Something must be done about that, but not necessarily for all organizations everywhere.

229631.explanation: Every transfer discourages the use of public transport. Moreover, it makes the metro less inclusive because people with disabilities or the elderly suffer from it the most.

229632.explanation: It denies that some people simply need a car and disproportionately affects entrepreneurs. We seek solutions through better cooperation and dialogue. The municipality has acted very piecemeal in recent years and has thrown the consequences over the fence to residents and entrepreneurs.

229633.explanation: Priority must be given to all projects, such as the opening of Sixhaven station, closing Westring, East-West line, and the bridges over the IJ.

229634.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers sees doctors and midwives being made life difficult. Imagine being the patient who needs them! The same goes for caregivers and also plumbers, and so on.

229635.explanation: That is possible but is not a special priority. It requires a balanced, integrated plan instead of the current ad hoc posts, cuts, and other wild ideas causing traffic congestion.

229636.explanation: Almost all roads in Amsterdam are now 30 km/h, regardless of whether there is a separate bike path or not. It is good to promote flow for motorists if there are separate bike paths and allow 50 km/h there. A uniform 30 km/h measure is not useful and few comply because it is illogical.

229637.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers wants to end the public display of sex workers. We do not close the business but the windows. With QR codes or other creative means, prostitution can continue without nuisance or offense. https://namensamsterdammers.nl/veiligheid/

229639.explanation: Namens Amsterdammers does not want to talk about connection but to create connection. Where there is solidarity, people help each other with, for example, Neighborhood Circles. There must also be room for care in the neighborhood, see https://namensamsterdammers.nl/zorg-welzijn-en-gezondheid/

229640.explanation: Logical!

229641.explanation: It is nice if this succeeds, but unfortunately not always possible. Care cannot be postponed because a care provider has a different religion. Health comes first.

229642.explanation: During the many neighborhood visits of Namens Amsterdammers, we saw that in certain neighborhoods (e.g., IJburg, Bos en Lommer, Weespersluis) there is really too little for young people to do.

229643.explanation: Providing breakfast is primarily the task of parents and caregivers.

229644.explanation: We are against fishing with grenades or underwater drones.

229570.explanation: AAA advocates for binding citizen councils. Residents must actually participate in decision-making about policies that affect them, not just advisory but with real authority.

229571.explanation: AAA stands for active protection against discrimination and exclusion. The municipality must implement structural policies to combat institutional inequality.

229572.explanation: AAA advocates decision-making close to residents. The abolition of boroughs weakens local authority and involvement.

229573.explanation: AAA calls for recognition of historical injustice and protection of human dignity. Commemoration should be unifying and substantive, not tied to a mandatory day off. The municipality must provide space for meaningful remembrance, dialogue, and awareness without imposing measures.

229574.explanation: AAA stands for fair distribution of burdens and protection of public services. A generic reduction of property tax (ozb) mainly benefits large property owners and companies, while the municipality needs resources for housing, care, and livability. Relief for low incomes is preferred.

229575.explanation: AAA chooses livability over mass tourism. Increasing the tourist tax should help relieve pressure from tourism on the city.

229576.explanation: AAA advocates more space for nightlife culture and social encounters. Extending opening hours should be possible in consultation with local residents.

229577.explanation: AAA believes municipal policy should serve residents, not actively attract more tourists who pressure livability.

229578.explanation: AAA stands for fair distribution of burdens and protection of local entrepreneurs. Eliminating user-based property tax primarily benefits large companies and chains, while small entrepreneurs and residents benefit little and municipal income for public services comes under pressure.

229579.explanation: AAA advocates protection of local entrepreneurs and vibrant neighborhoods but believes the municipality should not primarily act as a real estate party. Public funds should be used for housing security, care, and public space. Regulations, rental policies, and cooperative models are more effective.

229580.explanation: AAA believes the municipality should maintain influence over public infrastructure with major impact on living environment, health, and environment. By remaining a shareholder, it can manage nuisance reduction, transparency, public interests. Sale increases the power of commercial parties and reduces democracy.

229581.explanation: AAA stands for peace and human rights but believes the municipality must act carefully and consistently. Excluding companies may be justified in cases of proven human rights violations but must be based on clear criteria, legal certainty, and international law.

229583.explanation: AAA stands for human dignity and justice. As long as IND (Immigration Service) procedures fall short, no one should end up on the street. Amsterdam can continue to provide shelter and urge the IND for careful, quick, and fair decisions to ensure protection and, where necessary, support for return.

229584.explanation: AAA stands for fair allocation based on vulnerability, not origin. The municipality must apply customized solutions and consider disabilities, single parents, the elderly, and all vulnerable groups.

229585.explanation: AAA stands for inclusion without coercion. Language education must be available and accessible but not through generic budget increases or obligations. The municipality must provide customized solutions and also hold employers responsible for good support.

229586.explanation: AAA stands for humane shelter within a fair national asylum policy. The municipality should not apply a strict upper limit as long as people need protection, but should focus on capacity, distribution, and prompt decision-making by the IND.

229587.explanation: AAA stands for accessible public services. Bulky waste collection should be simple and low-threshold so residents can do the right thing. Pickup by appointment only raises barriers, leads to more illegal dumping, and especially affects the elderly, people with disabilities, and low-income households.

229588.explanation: AAA stands for inclusive public spaces. Free-access toilets for people with disabilities should be available in central squares.

229589.explanation: AAA chooses to protect vulnerable city parks as the green lungs of the city. Fewer events should contribute to nature conservation, calm, and livability for local residents. Parks are meant for daily use by residents, not for ongoing commercial exploitation.

229590.explanation: AAA stands for affordability and livelihood security. Paying more for waste disposal should not increase pressure on low incomes.

229591.explanation: AAA prioritizes public basic facilities such as housing, care, and livelihood security. Municipal funds should primarily go to services that directly contribute to social justice and livability. Subsidies for tourist attractions do not fit this.

229592.explanation: AAA stands for quality of life and ecological limits. Large cruise ships should not be automatically welcomed due to their impact on air quality, crowding, and use of space. Restrictions and strict conditions are preferable to unlimited admission, with attention to residents and the environment.

229593.explanation: AAA stands for greening and fair mobility. Parking spaces should not automatically disappear because of green measures. Greening must be carried out intelligently and integrally, without disadvantaging residents, the elderly, or people with disabilities. Environmental policy should connect, not compel.

229594.explanation: AAA stands for local food security and protection of open space. Development of the Lutkemeerpolder must stop, even at financial cost.

229595.explanation: AAA believes new wind turbines provide hardly any constant energy and at the same time consume a lot of space, materials, and nature. They are environmentally burdensome and contribute only limitedly to reliable energy supply. Therefore, the municipality should not install new wind turbines, not even in the Western Harbor.

229596.explanation: AAA stands for inclusion and health. Sports participation should be accessible for adults with low incomes as well.

229597.explanation: AAA values independent local media as important for democracy, provided they are pluralistic and allow space for critical voices, including toward the government. Public support should depend on quality, open debate, and representation of diverse perspectives.

229598.explanation: AAA chooses broadly accessible sports and social interaction. Public money should not primarily fund large-scale top sports events.

229599.explanation: AAA advocates for cultural freedom without ideological direction. Subsidies should be based on accessibility and quality, not origin.

229600.explanation: AAA stands for equal opportunities. Schools that select later should be given extra support.

229601.explanation: AAA advocates equal development opportunities. Pre-school facilities should be accessible and free for all children.

229602.explanation: AAA stands for accessible culture in neighborhoods and believes investments in culture must be carefully weighed. New construction can be justified if it contributes to broad accessibility, local embedding, and does not come at the expense of other public basic facilities.

229603.explanation: AAA sees sports and exercise as a public basic service. Investing in them should strengthen health and social cohesion.

229604.explanation: AAA advocates safety through social approaches and prevention. More community enforcement officers (BOAs) should not be a structural solution.

229605.explanation: AAA believes repressive enforcement should not be the core. Social and preventive approaches come first.

229606.explanation: AAA stands for de-escalation and legal protection. Arming BOAs increases tensions and does not fit a humane safety approach.

229607.explanation: AAA supports an open and non-repressive policy. Access to coffee shops should not depend on nationality.

229608.explanation: AAA opposes expanding camera surveillance. Nuisance control should not lead to permanent monitoring of residents.

229609.explanation: AAA stands for inclusion and equal treatment. Banning religious expressions by BOAs leads to exclusion.

229610.explanation: AAA advocates bodily autonomy and freedom of belief. Excluding people based on clothing is incompatible with fundamental rights.

229611.explanation: AAA supports strengthening social housing. Housing corporations should not sell social rental homes.

229612.explanation: AAA values local ties and social cohesion. Long-term connection to the city should be considered in allocation.

229613.explanation: AAA advocates preserving public control. Municipal real estate should not be sold off automatically.

229614.explanation: AAA stands for public control over land but not for micromanagement from city hall. The municipality should set frameworks for affordability, sustainability, and livability, while giving residents, cooperatives, and local initiatives the space to develop that land.

229615.explanation: AAA wants to strictly limit the tourist removal of residential space. A complete ban in the city center should be defensible.

229616.explanation: AAA stands for housing security and flow without coercion. A moving allowance should help elderly people voluntarily downsize.

229617.explanation: AAA advocates strengthening social housing. At least 40% social rental should be the standard in new construction.

229618.explanation: AAA wants to protect housing from speculation. Those who buy a home should live in it themselves.

229619.explanation: AAA criticizes leasehold and division but does not explicitly call for complete abolition.

229620.explanation: AAA advocates freedom in social assistance and sees earning additional income as a step toward a basic income. Increasing the exemption amount enhances livelihood security, stimulates initiative, and reduces control and bureaucracy, in line with experiments with unconditional support.

229621.explanation: AAA stands for humane poverty alleviation. Basic care for pets should be accessible to people with low incomes.

229622.explanation: AAA advocates protection for vulnerable people. Many more places should be available for safe night shelter, and when space is insufficient, Ulmer Nests should be used along with decent assistance to transition to a real home.

229623.explanation: AAA stands for accessible care. The municipality should support people with minimum income in healthcare costs.

229624.explanation: AAA views poverty reduction as a core task. Investing less undermines livelihood security.

229625.explanation: AAA advocates freedom and trust in social assistance. Mandatory counter-performances discipline people instead of supporting them.

229626.explanation: AAA stands for protection of low incomes. Waiver of levies must be maintained.

229627.explanation: AAA explicitly advocates experimenting with an unconditional basic income.

229628.explanation: AAA supports mobility as a public basic service. Free local public transport increases freedom, accessibility, and equal opportunities, reduces transport poverty, and strengthens livability. Moving around in the city should not be a luxury, but a right for everyone.

229629.explanation: AAA stands for equal opportunities from a young age. Free public transport for children increases independence, access to education, sports, and culture, and financially relieves families. Mobility should not be a barrier to development or participation in the city.

229630.explanation: AAA supports societal initiatives. Volunteering should not be hindered by parking costs.

229631.explanation: AAA advocates for accessibility without exclusion. Direct connections must be maintained for residents of Southeast.

229632.explanation: AAA stands for greening the city but believes parking spaces should not automatically disappear. Green spaces must be added smartly without disadvantaging residents, the elderly, or entrepreneurs. Environmental policy should unite and offer tailor-made solutions, not impose.

229633.explanation: AAA supports good public transport and accessibility without extra car pressure. Extension to Schiphol can contribute to sustainable mobility, provided costs, risks, and public interests are transparent and do not come at the expense of investments in neighborhoods and social security.

229634.explanation: AAA believes the city center should remain accessible to residents and entrepreneurs, including by car, provided it is carefully regulated.

229635.explanation: AAA stands for a livable public space, but car-free streets must be introduced carefully and gradually. Accessibility for residents, entrepreneurs, emergency services, and people with disabilities must be guaranteed. Livability must not turn into exclusion or impracticality.

229636.explanation: AAA stands for traffic safety and clarity in public spaces. On roads with separate bicycle lanes, 50 km/h should be the norm because this increases predictability, reduces dangerous speed differences, and contributes to safe flow for all road users.

229637.explanation: AAA stands for autonomy and protection of the vulnerable. A ban pushes sex workers out of sight, increases dependence, and makes it less safe. A ban on sex work violates fundamental rights such as personal autonomy and privacy, right to work, safety, and health. ECHR & UDHR.

229639.explanation: AAA stands for care nearby and in the neighborhood. Living facilities with professional support should enable the elderly to live independently longer.

229640.explanation: AAA explicitly advocates ending cooperation with youth care institutions that repeatedly perform poorly.

229641.explanation: AAA stands for self-determination in care. People must be able to choose a care provider that fits their faith, culture, or beliefs.

229642.explanation: AAA advocates for prevention and social encounters. Youth centers must offer young people perspective, safety, and space in every neighborhood.

229643.explanation: AAA stands for equal opportunities. No child should start the school day hungry.

229644.explanation: AAA supports accessible use of public space. Recreational fishing is a low-threshold, peaceful form of relaxation that contributes to well-being and connection with nature. Restrictions are only justified if there is demonstrable damage to nature or safety, not as a standard ban.

229570.explanation: It's good to know how citizens view problems.

229571.explanation: Discrimination either falls into the category of crime, for which police/enforcement suffice, or into the category of words, which must be condemned with words.

229572.explanation: Decentralization in this case means more attention to each district. The central city can intervene when necessary.

229570.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants citizens to actively participate in political decision-making to create support for major urban and societal issues.

229571.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports municipal anti-discrimination policy, with a coordinator ensuring structural coherence and execution.

229572.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes abolishing the district councils. We believe it is important that Amsterdam residents retain influence over decisions affecting their own neighborhoods. No top-down decisions from the Stopera alone, but embedded in the district, close to the community.

229573.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports commemorating the abolition of slavery (Keti Koti). It is important to face our past and do justice. A day off offers staff space for encounter and healing. This way, we build a city where everyone feels seen and recognized.

229574.explanation: The ChristianUnion is open to lowering the property tax (OZB), which helps housing corporations to build and make homes healthy. The budget gap will be covered by working more efficiently at the Municipality and increasing the tourist tax. This keeps the city affordable and invests in healthy neighborhoods.

229575.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to raise the tourist tax to combat nuisance and restore balance in the city. Cheap mass tourism focused on drugs and prostitution harms residents and the city's dignity. A higher tourist tax attracts visitors who truly come for Amsterdam.

229576.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes entrepreneurs should have the space to serve society and seize economic opportunities on weekends. At the same time, the party believes this must not come at the expense of employees' workload.

229577.explanation: The ChristianUnion sees that tourism increasingly clashes with the livability of our city center. Additional tourist overnight stays put pressure on scarce space. The municipality should not encourage this further: the priority lies with a livable city, strong neighborhoods, and sufficient housing.

229578.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes the property tax (OZB) should be applied as intended, and therefore only levied on owners.

229579.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes it is not the municipality's task to acquire retail properties. The municipality should focus on livability and affordable housing, not on a real estate portfolio. Buying retail spaces is expensive, risky, and detracts from resources for facilities and vulnerable residents.

229580.explanation: The ChristianUnion is against selling the municipality's shares in Schiphol. Through this shareholding, the municipality influences airport policy. We use that influence to reduce nuisance for residents and to help Schiphol become climate-neutral faster.

229581.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes this because the municipality does not import international conflicts nor impose independent boycotts. For example, we also do not do this regarding China, the Emirates, the United States, Syria, Russia, or Morocco.

229583.explanation: The ChristianUnion stands for merciful treatment of everyone residing in the city. Therefore, the bed-bath-bread provision must be maintained as part of the national alien services. During shelter, there must be clarity about return or lawful residence in the Netherlands.

229584.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes the municipality must have the ability to decide for which groups the priority scheme is used. The municipality has a legal duty to house status holders and needs this policy freedom to fulfill that obligation.

229585.explanation: The ChristianUnion encourages everyone to learn the Dutch language and actively participate through work experience or volunteer work. From newcomers, we expect active engagement so they can quickly participate fully. This way, we build a city where everyone can take part.

229586.explanation: The ChristianUnion stands for our city remaining a home for people fleeing war, violence, and persecution. We do not leave vulnerable people out in the cold. However, a fair distribution of shelter through the dispersion law is necessary, so Amsterdam does not have to bear this alone.

229587.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to keep the low-threshold collection of bulky waste because it contributes to reuse and a circular city. A fixed collection system is more accessible and prevents more litter on the streets through an appointment system.

229588.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants free and accessible toilets on all large squares. This is a right according to the UN Disability Convention and is necessary for full participation in public life. The municipality must therefore invest in accessible facilities and good signage.

229589.explanation: The ChristianUnion considers events important because of their contribution to connection, social cohesion, and accessible cultural offerings. But this must not come at the expense of livability or biodiversity. Therefore, we want to carefully limit events and maintain balance with peace, nature, and residents.

229590.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes a clean city requires timely waste collection by the municipality but also responsibility from society. Therefore, we want to explore how citizens who offer exceptionally large amounts of waste can contribute extra.

229591.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants the municipality to continue supporting Artis due to its educational role in animal welfare, nature, and biodiversity. Without public support, a visit becomes a luxury, excluding children from less affluent families.

229592.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants tourism to contribute to residents’ well-being and the city's livability. Large cruise ships cause pollution, nuisance, and safety risks; therefore, we support measures to limit this. Tourism is welcome but not at the expense of Amsterdammers.

229593.explanation: The ChristianUnion advocates for more greenery, such as green parking spaces. Each street and neighborhood must assess where greening is possible and where parking space remains essential, e.g., in Rural North and Weesp. Custom solutions come first: greenery where possible, parking spaces where necessary.

229594.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes Amsterdam should stop developing a business park in the Lutkemeerpolder. Nature, farmland, and biodiversity are more important than the financial loss.

229595.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports sustainable energy and sees the Western Port Area as the most suitable location for new wind turbines. Wind energy is essential for the energy transition, but locations must be chosen carefully. The port area is preferred over residential neighborhoods or nature.

229596.explanation: The ChristianUnion is in favor. Sports connect people and contribute to a healthy, livable city. The ChristianUnion wants adults with low incomes to participate as well and therefore supports reimbursing sports memberships via the City Pass so that sports remain accessible to everyone.

229597.explanation: The ChristianUnion does not want to cut funding for local media. Local journalism is essential for a healthy democracy because it makes local issues visible and better informs residents. Moreover, it provides space for developing young talent.

229598.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that top sports events are good for promoting Amsterdam and bringing people from diverse groups together. They contribute to the city's atmosphere, as seen in the European Athletics Championships and the start of the Giro.

229599.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that art and culture express collective stories and strengthen meaning and social cohesion. The municipality ensures good conditions and accessibility, with extra attention to less visible initiatives to showcase Amsterdam’s cultural richness.

229600.explanation: The ChristianUnion is positive about broad and extended preparatory classes, allowing children to develop at their own pace. Later selection reduces equality of opportunity. Schools should be able to shape this themselves, with attention to child welfare and parental involvement.

229601.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants children with a disadvantage to first attend preschool, based on the Early Education indication. Free preschool for all children can only happen when there is sufficient capacity and vulnerable children are not left behind.

229602.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes the municipality should stop new construction for Theater De Meervaart. There is already a good theater, and a megalomaniac new building project is not needed.

229603.explanation: The ChristianUnion finds sports and exercise essential for strong neighborhoods and health. Investments should be neighborhood-oriented: via sports clubs in the network, affordable sports for everyone, including seniors, and accessible locations near home. This way, we strengthen well-being and livability simultaneously.

229604.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants more enforcement officers (BOAs) for visible enforcement and deployment in neighborhoods. This is also necessary in Amsterdam, partly due to the shortage of police capacity.

229605.explanation: The ChristianUnion is against more plainclothes officers. We combat indecent behavior primarily through strong social norms and neighborhood responsibility.

229606.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that enforcement officers (BOAs) must be able to enforce effectively and defend themselves. In Amsterdam, good equipment is necessary, partly due to the shortage of police capacity. Police officers must also be better able to defend themselves in low-violence situations against increasing aggression.

229607.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes the sale of soft drugs to tourists because it causes nuisance. We want to attract tourists who contribute to culture, economy, and society-not to a cannabis and party culture. By limiting sales to residents, the city remains more livable and safer.

229608.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes there should be more supervision of underground waste containers. This helps keep the city clean, reduces nuisance, and ensures waste is collected properly.

229609.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes banning headscarves or kippahs for enforcement officers (BOA’s). Such a ban excludes religious Amsterdammers from municipal jobs. We choose inclusion and want to work on a uniform that is neutral without excluding people.

229610.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that face-covering clothing may be prohibited in government buildings to allow open communication between people.

229611.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to preserve social rental housing. Therefore, we ask housing corporations not to sell and ensure they can comply by providing financial support, for example through a lower property tax. This way, we maintain enough affordable and good-quality homes in the city.

229612.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes social rental homes should partly be allocated to people who have been part of a community in their neighborhood or district for a longer period. This preserves communities and allows new residents to contribute something valuable.

229613.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports selling municipal properties that are unused. This way, they can be transformed into, for example, housing, a community center, a church, or a sports facility. This reduces vacancy and creates social value for the neighborhood.

229614.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes municipal land should be developed according to the wishes of the (future) community. In housing projects, we reserve space from the outset for meeting places and care, and consciously designate locations for future initiatives. This safeguards sustainability.

229615.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes the municipality should strictly enforce rules against illegal holiday rentals via platforms like Airbnb. In neighborhoods where tourism pressures livability, holiday rentals of homes should be prohibited.

229616.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that elderly people who live in large homes and want to downsize should be supported by the municipality. This makes sustainable living possible for all elderly and encourages voluntary housing turnover, freeing up larger homes for families and starters.

229617.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes building should be for everyone. We opt for a healthy mix of homes that fits Amsterdam's housing needs: 40% social, 30% mid-range, 15% expensive, and 15% senior housing.

229618.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes housing is not a commodity but the foundation for a home and strong community. With a self-occupancy requirement, we prevent vacancy and speculation, protect affordable housing, and strengthen livability and connection in neighborhoods.

229619.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants a fair and accommodating approach to ground lease: everyone should be able to buy off the ground lease under the old, favorable conditions. At the same time, the party favors ownership over ground lease and believes ground lease should eventually be abolished.

229620.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes people need confidence to take the step to work. A (temporary) bridge by increasing the amount that social assistance recipients are allowed to earn can help achieve this.

229621.explanation: The ChristianUnion Amsterdam recognizes that people with low incomes are extra vulnerable to loneliness and that a pet is often an important companion. Therefore, we are positive about targeted support for low-income people with necessary veterinary costs.

229622.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes the municipality must provide shelter to people without a roof over their heads or in unsafe home situations. This shelter should meet basic needs, provide rest, and guide people towards appropriate help and eventually independence. Recovery starts with having one’s own home.

229623.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that in some cases the healthcare deductible can be reimbursed through special social assistance.

229624.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes cuts to support for low-income people. We want everyone in our city to participate and live with dignity; especially for Amsterdammers who face difficulties, the municipality must continue to provide help.

229625.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes that people who receive long-term social assistance should continue to participate in society. Volunteer work can help with this, except where it is genuinely not possible.

229626.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes those with the greatest capacity should bear the heaviest burdens. In that spirit, we consider waiving taxes for low-income individuals a wise policy.

229627.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes our current social system already offers a targeted basic income. Research also shows that a universal basic income is not effective.

229628.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes free public transport for all Amsterdammers. It would have to be paid for somehow, or else it would affect property taxes. Low-income people can already travel at a reduced fare, which works well.

229629.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports free public transport for children up to 12 years old.

229630.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to appreciate and support volunteer work. Volunteers deserve every opportunity to contribute to the city. Therefore, we are positive about offering free parking hours to volunteers so that involvement is never hindered by costs.

229631.explanation: The ChristianUnion finds it important that all districts remain well connected and that citizens are encouraged to use public transport. Therefore, the ChristianUnion Amsterdam wants to preserve line 53.

229632.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports reducing parking spaces in the city center and wants to replace them with bike racks and electric bike stations.

229633.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports more connections on the metro network, such as extending the North-South line. But this should be done in a way that adds more stops in Amsterdam so that more neighborhoods are connected to Amsterdam’s metro network.

229634.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports accessibility of the city center for cars. This is important for destination traffic, people with mobility impairments, and to prevent isolation of people in the center.

229635.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports a car-reduced city center but remains accessible for destination traffic, people with mobility impairments, and to prevent isolation of people in the center.

229636.explanation: The ChristianUnion opposes lowering the speed limit to 50 km/h because the current speed contributes to traffic safety.

229637.explanation: The ChristianUnion wants to end window prostitution because it often leads to exploitation and inhumane conditions. We choose to protect vulnerable women and men and promote a livable city by focusing on phasing out, assistance, exit programs, and alternative workplaces.

229639.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports care neighborhood centers in every district so that elderly people can live independently longer with professional support. They should be able to remain in suitable homes and part of their community. The municipality provides financial contributions for this.

229640.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes care for young people starts at home, at school, and in the community, such as the church, mosque, or community center. When this is insufficient, the municipality must procure qualitative and appropriate care with respect for beliefs and cultural background.

229641.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes care becomes more effective and person-centered when caregivers connect with the experiences, culture, and beliefs of the care recipient. This increases understanding, treatment adherence, prevents miscommunication, and improves wellbeing, especially in mental health care.

229642.explanation: The ChristianUnion supports having a youth center in every neighborhood, combined with outreach youth work.

229643.explanation: The ChristianUnion believes every child should be able to start the day with a healthy breakfast. We support assistance at schools where needed, but do not want a one-size-fits-all approach. The municipality should provide targeted help where children come to school without breakfast.

229644.explanation: The ChristianUnion Amsterdam wants sport fishing to remain possible where it can, but not without limits everywhere; nature, animal welfare, and livability must always be considered.

229570.explanation: This leads to citizen participation, which results in better decision-making on complex issues, more support, and influence for citizens when well guided and facilitated.

229571.explanation: Ellect is against injustice. Therefore, we advocate for a municipal coordinator against discrimination and racism.
