229572.explanation: The existence of separate district administrations causes fragmentation and additional costs. Ellect therefore calls for abolition and one strong municipal government.

229573.explanation: The abolition of slavery deserves recognition. Ellect therefore calls for a day off for municipal employees on Keti Koti.

229574.explanation: Agreed. Housing and entrepreneurship must remain affordable. Therefore, Ellect wants to lower the property tax (ozb).

229575.explanation: To limit the pressure on the city and fund facilities, Ellect wants to increase the tourist tax.

229576.explanation: Ellect wants the municipality to extend the opening hours of snack bars and restaurants on weekends until 3:00 AM, instead of the current 2:00 AM.

229577.explanation: The municipality should not spend money on attracting tourists.

229578.explanation: For businesses and shops the municipality should make only the owners pay property tax, not the users.

229579.explanation: Agreed. Ellect wants the municipality to actively purchase retail properties in the city center to control vacancy and rental prices.

229580.explanation: The municipality should sell its shares in Schiphol.

229581.explanation: To conduct ethical and responsible policy, Ellect wants the municipality to avoid business relations with companies that work for the Israeli military.

229583.explanation: Ellect believes that asylum seekers without a residence permit also deserve protection and support, but this must be done in a balanced way, taking into account the capacity of the city and its residents.

229584.explanation: To relieve the pressure on the housing market and promote equal treatment,

229585.explanation: Good language skills are essential for integration and participation. Therefore, Ellect wants the municipality to spend more on Dutch lessons for migrant workers.

229586.explanation: To respect the capacity of the city, Ellect wants Amsterdam not to receive more asylum seekers than the current level, while existing accommodations should be well maintained.

229587.explanation: Ellect believes that bulky waste should be collected efficiently, but residents should retain freedom of choice in how they offer bulky waste, for example at fixed points or by appointment.

229588.explanation: Ellect advocates for free public toilets on all major squares in the city center, specially designed for people with disabilities and children.

229589.explanation: Ellect believes parks should remain venues for events, but management must be efficient and careful to limit nuisance.

229590.explanation: Ellect believes waste management should be efficient, but residents must not be additionally charged based on the amount of household waste they dispose of.

229591.explanation: Ellect believes that Artis no longer needs municipal subsidies and that its funding can be fully private.

229592.explanation: Large cruise ships remain welcome in Amsterdam, with attention to the living environment and the economy.

229593.explanation: Ellect believes that preserving parking spaces should have priority over planting additional trees or plants.

229594.explanation: Ellect believes the municipality should stop developing the business park in the Lutkemeerpolder, despite costs already incurred.

229595.explanation: Ellect believes that the development of new wind turbines should be limited to the Western Port area to prevent nuisance and impact in other parts of the city.

229596.explanation: Ellect believes sport is important, but adults with low incomes should be responsible for their own memberships. The municipality prefers to focus on other forms of support or public facilities.

229597.explanation: The municipality should give less money to local media.

229598.explanation: More municipal investment in attracting top sports events.

229599.explanation: To ensure equal opportunities and objectivity, Ellect wants cultural background not to be a factor in municipal subsidies for theater and dance groups.

229600.explanation: Ellect believes schools with a three-year preparatory class deserve extra municipal support due to longer student guidance.

229601.explanation: Ellect believes schools with a three-year preparatory class deserve extra municipal support due to longer student guidance.

229602.explanation: To save municipal resources and prioritize public facilities, Ellect wants the municipality not to invest in the new construction of Theater de Meervaart.

229603.explanation: Significantly increased municipal spending on sports and exercise facilities.

229604.explanation: Ellect believes Amsterdam should invest in more enforcement officers (boa’s) to effectively maintain public order and safety.

229605.explanation: Ellect believes the police should deploy more plainclothes officers to effectively address nuisance caused by verbally aggressive behavior in public spaces.

229606.explanation: Ellect believes municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) responsible for safety in Amsterdam should be equipped with batons and pepper spray for their protection and effectiveness.

229607.explanation: To protect health, safety, and livability, Ellect wants Amsterdam to have no coffee shops.

229608.explanation: To reduce litter and nuisance, Ellect wants more cameras installed near underground waste containers where bulky waste is often dumped.

229609.explanation: Ellect believes enforcement officers (boa’s) should wear neutral and uniform clothing during their work, without visible religious symbols such as headscarves or yarmulkes.

229610.explanation: It can increase recognition and safety in government buildings. Staff and visitors can feel safer and more at ease. It supports identification for official matters such as applying for documents.

229611.explanation: Social rental homes are urgently needed to ensure affordable housing for low and middle incomes. By limiting sales, the social housing stock remains stable and housing seekers can be better assisted.

229612.explanation: People who have lived in Amsterdam for a longer time thus retain housing security. It limits displacement by newcomers in the social housing market. It can contribute to stable and liveable neighborhoods.

229613.explanation: Selling unused buildings generates funds that the municipality can use elsewhere. It prevents buildings from falling into disrepair or remaining vacant, which can cause deterioration.

229614.explanation: A balance is needed: the municipality must set frameworks and minimum requirements, but not regulate every detail. It depends on the type of project and its impact on the environment.

229615.explanation: It can limit owners in their property rights and income opportunities. Tourist rentals can contribute to the local economy. A total ban can lead to illegal rentals that are difficult to enforce.

229616.explanation: It stimulates housing market turnover, making it easier for families and young people to find a home. Older people receive financial support for moving and possible adaptations in their new home.

229617.explanation: It ensures that the social housing stock remains stable and prevents the city from shifting too much towards expensive owner-occupation or private rentals. New construction projects contribute to a balanced housing market and social diversity in neighborhoods. It helps low and middle incomes to continue living in the city.

229618.explanation: People may have legitimate reasons to rent out a home (for example, work elsewhere or temporary stay). It can limit the market and restrict owners' freedom. Strict rules are sometimes difficult to enforce and lead to administrative burdens.

229619.explanation: It simplifies property rights and prevents extra annual costs for homeowners. It can make homes cheaper and more attractive for buyers. It increases financial security and predictability for residents.

229620.explanation: It encourages people to work without immediately losing their benefits. It increases financial independence and reduces poverty. It can raise participation in the labor market.

229621.explanation: A partial allowance or a safety net arrangement could be a good compromise. It depends on available budgets and the frequency of requests for help.

229622.explanation: It offers homeless people a safe place, especially during bad weather or at night. It can reduce health risks and crime. It is an important step towards social shelter and support.

229623.explanation: Agree: It ensures that people with low incomes are not hindered from receiving necessary care. It prevents people from postponing medical care for financial reasons, which could be more expensive later. It increases social equality and health among vulnerable groups.

229624.explanation: It can relieve the municipal budget and free up money for other tasks or investments. It may encourage people to become more independent and less reliant on municipal support.

229625.explanation: It promotes participation and engagement in society. People keep or develop work skills, which can increase their chances of regular employment. It can contribute to the community by supporting useful services or projects.

229626.explanation: Agree: It saves the municipality money that can be used for other services. It encourages all residents to contribute to municipal costs. It prevents regulations from becoming complicated and administratively heavy.

229627.explanation: A pilot can show whether a basic income contributes to financial security and poverty reduction. It can motivate people to work or volunteer without fear of losing income. It provides valuable insights for future social policy.

229628.explanation: Agree: It makes mobility more accessible to everyone, including people with low incomes. It can make the city more attractive and livable.

229629.explanation: It makes travel cheaper and more accessible for children and youth, for example to school and sports. It can reduce social inequality because families with lower incomes pay less for transportation.

229630.explanation: It stimulates volunteering by lowering costs and barriers for volunteers. It supports organizations that provide social contributions, such as churches, community centers, or aid organizations. It can improve mobility for volunteers and promote participation.

229631.explanation: A direct connection provides fast and efficient travel time for commuters and students. It strengthens the accessibility of Southeast and promotes mobility in the city. It can stimulate metro use and reduce pressure on other modes of transport.

229632.explanation: It can make the city center less accessible for residents, visitors, and people with mobility issues. Fewer parking spaces can negatively affect the economy of shops and hospitality. Alternative transportation may still be insufficiently developed to compensate for the lack of parking.

229633.explanation: It offers a direct and fast connection between North, South, and Schiphol, facilitating travel for commuters, travelers, and tourists. It improves airport accessibility. It strengthens the public transport network and can bring economic benefits.

229634.explanation: Good car accessibility is important for residents, visitors, entrepreneurs, and deliveries. It can support the economy of shops, hospitality, and services in the city center. It promotes accessibility for people with mobility problems or elderly who depend on cars.

229635.explanation: A balanced approach can work, for example combining car-free zones with accessible routes and limited vehicles (such as emergency services or destination traffic). The measure can be assessed per street or neighborhood for effects on accessibility and livability.

229636.explanation: It increases traffic safety for cyclists, especially on busy roads. It can reduce the number of serious traffic accidents. It promotes a calm and predictable traffic situation for all road users.

229637.explanation: It can help reduce human trafficking and exploitation within the sex industry. It can decrease nuisance and crime in certain neighborhoods. It promotes a safer image of the city and can protect vulnerable workers.

229639.explanation: It helps elderly people live independently longer, which improves their well-being and quality of life. Professional support nearby can reduce health problems and loneliness. Investing in these types of housing can save long-term healthcare costs because patients...

229640.explanation: It encourages quality improvement and responsibility in youth care institutions. It protects vulnerable children and families from insufficient or poor care. It promotes transparency and trust in municipal policy and the youth care system.

229641.explanation: It promotes personal dignity and comfort in vulnerable situations. Tailored care according to culture or faith can increase effectiveness and satisfaction. It can prevent discrimination or discomfort and stimulate inclusive care.

229642.explanation: Youth centers offer young people a safe place to meet each other, engage in activities, and get guidance. They can prevent boredom or problematic behavior among youth. Local centers strengthen social cohesion and youth involvement in the neighborhood.

229643.explanation: A breakfast at school helps children start the day healthy and focused. It can contribute to equal opportunities, especially for children from low-income families. Healthy nutrition fosters learning, concentration, and overall health.

229644.explanation: Angling offers recreation and relaxation for residents and tourists. It can be a low-threshold way to connect with water and nature in the city. Restrictions can reduce social and recreational opportunities.

parties.name: Hart voor Amsterdam/Partij voor de ouderen
parties.fullName: Hart voor Amsterdam/partij voor de ouderen

229570.explanation: It is important to listen to the residents.

229571.explanation: Combat racism on all fronts.

229572.explanation: The districts should have more power to solve problems in the neighborhoods and not remain dependent on the whims of the central city.

229574.explanation: Municipal taxes should be lowered entirely, including advertising taxes for businesses. If they cannot advertise, the local shops will quickly be destroyed.

229575.explanation: Tourists bring in money, so no increases.

229578.explanation: Accommodate entrepreneurs as much as possible.

229579.explanation: No shops but buying houses from owners to help people with minimum income get housing.

229581.explanation: Ridiculous, keep it in Amsterdam, we have enough problems here.

229584.explanation: Priority for Amsterdammers who have been waiting 12 years for a home.

229587.explanation: Provide more places where people can dispose of bulky waste by bringing it themselves.

229588.explanation: Finally, about time. Various motions and initiatives have been submitted to the municipal council.

229602.explanation: Bad idea to build the Meervaart in the green area.

229607.explanation: If you want to reduce tourism now, start here: stop selling soft drugs to foreigners.

229613.explanation: Convert into apartments for the elderly.

229617.explanation: But also more senior housing together with students.

229622.explanation: Highest priority, it is a disgrace that these people cannot get a warm place to sleep dry and warm.

229625.explanation: But only if they can work.

229628.explanation: Lower the price if you want to discourage car usage.

229634.explanation: Such as for disabled residents.

229570.explanation: This is good for democracy. Citizens should not be selected by lottery but by an independent committee. Some advice should also be binding because that is good for democracy: power must be distributed and not concentrated in a small group of politicians.

229571.explanation: There is definitely a need for this; there is a lot of racism in the city, so an INDEPENDENT coordinator would be welcome; this person should not be appointed by their clients but by an independent committee or citizens' council.

229572.explanation: The current administration is appointed while district elections are held. What is the point of elections if they appoint the officials? Moreover, the district board only has an advisory role. We propose either to give them more authority or to abolish them.

229573.explanation: The municipality should also request businesses and other employers to give employees a day off. The same applies to an Islamic holiday, which is currently not mentioned.

229574.explanation: The property tax (OZB) has already been raised several times and the municipality already has substantial income from this tax.

229575.explanation: We already have too many tourists.

229576.explanation: It's good for the economy and a matter of supply and demand.

229577.explanation: We already have too many tourists.

229578.explanation: Yes, otherwise it is double payment.

229579.explanation: This way, the mass of tourists can be reduced and more people can live in the city center.

229580.explanation: Then the municipality has no say over Schiphol, and we must maintain input.

229581.explanation: According to a motion from 2015, this should have been done already. We are now talking about, among others, bus company EBS. Conversely, Amsterdam engineers and companies should also not work on Israeli projects such as the new metro line, which has happened. This ban should apply until Israel ends the occupation.

229583.explanation: Yes, because returning is not as easy as said. If they don’t get shelter, we see them on the streets and they are forced into crime. They need to have a livelihood by now. So that’s why shelter is necessary.

229584.explanation: This is a misunderstanding by many. Refugees do not get 'priority' just because they receive nationality. These people have often lived for years under poor conditions in a reception center. And they are entitled to housing. You cannot keep them in a reception center once they have a Dutch passport.

229585.explanation: Dutch is becoming increasingly unimportant in Amsterdam: with English, you can do more!

229586.explanation: Asylum seekers arise because of unfair world trade: the West profits at the expense of third world countries. So we should not be surprised if people from those countries want to flee to richer countries: the West is stealing their wealth.

229587.explanation: There is a lot of bulky waste, so it would be easier if it were collected once a week by default. Collecting by appointment does not work for everyone. Choose a method that works.

229588.explanation: There is no need for that. We also see many parking spaces for people with disabilities are empty, while in general there are not enough parking spaces. Respond to supply and demand.

229589.explanation: Events and festivals make the city more enjoyable. However, agreements must be made about the large amount of trash/waste.

229590.explanation: That would be unfair to families with many children.

229591.explanation: It is bad for animals to live in captivity: they become unhappy (depressed) because they need a lot of space and freedom.

229592.explanation: Makes the city more fun and attractive.

229593.explanation: Trees and plants are important but can also be planted elsewhere.

229594.explanation: It is bad for the environment and surroundings.

229596.explanation: Sports are almost a basic necessity, and in times when everything is already so expensive, low-income people should be compensated. Tax the expats more who actually enjoy benefits while having high incomes.

229597.explanation: Local media must be independent, and that is not possible if they are funded by the municipality. This is also what we observe, and it harms the credibility of journalism and politics.

229598.explanation: This only costs money, harms livability, and is nothing more than marketing for the city.

229599.explanation: Subsidies compromise independence.

229600.explanation: It is good that there are schools with a three-year bridging class; this gives students more time to make choices.

229601.explanation: Should be income-dependent. Wealthier people can easily afford this money, so let them pay too.

229602.explanation: Meervaart has already cost too much money.

229603.explanation: Sports are good for mental and physical health, especially in (economically) tough times like now.

229604.explanation: Amsterdam already spends too much on safety without being effective. Safety issues are not solved with more enforcement officers or weapons but by addressing people’s dissatisfaction.

229605.explanation: There are already too many plainclothes officers (informants), and this undermines citizens' trust in the state and also among citizens themselves.

229606.explanation: This only increases violence. The idea that we can solve safety with weapons and repression is incorrect. Do something about the underlying causes.

229607.explanation: Otherwise, it would be discrimination.

229608.explanation: Many people do not separate their waste, so more enforcement is needed.

229609.explanation: In these modern times, it should be possible for believers to also be civil servants.

229610.explanation: It must be possible to cover your face, for any reason. It concerns your own privacy and is your own choice.

229611.explanation: If housing corporations continue to sell social rental homes, soon only the rich will be able to live in Amsterdam.

229612.explanation: Given the enormous housing shortage, this is not feasible. Ten years would be a better and more realistic period.

229613.explanation: The municipality should sell these buildings so that private initiatives can also arise.

229614.explanation: Let citizens also have a say. More participation is good for democracy.

229615.explanation: It is already too crowded in the city center and full of tourists.

229616.explanation: Not standard for all elderly but only those who need it or who are convinced by the money amount to move to smaller homes.

229617.explanation: This way you keep housing affordable in the city.

229618.explanation: The housing shortage is too great to sell homes to investors. This turns housing into a commercial product.

229619.explanation: Ground lease is a form of double taxation on top of the property tax (OZB), where the government profits from value increases.

229620.explanation: Social assistance recipients already get too little money, so they should receive more benefits to get by. No one likes to stay at home on benefits; that must be the starting point.

229621.explanation: Pets are often emotionally connected to their owners. When animals are ill, costs can rise quickly, and we prefer to treat them rather than let them die from illness and their owners' lack of money.

229622.explanation: There is a need for that. We have a large increase in homeless people.

229623.explanation: In these economically difficult times, it is hard for minimum income households to make ends meet.

229624.explanation: The rich receive many tax benefits, expats too even though they have enough money. Meanwhile, people with low incomes can hardly make ends meet.

229625.explanation: This only works if it is on a voluntary basis. If they are forced to work, it can be seen as 'forced labor.' Moreover, benefit recipients are already pressured too much by officials to find work.

229626.explanation: In practice, it is already difficult for low-income people to request debt relief; hardly anyone qualifies for it. The debt relief scheme should be expanded.

229627.explanation: This way we know if it is successful in practice; that is why it is a trial.

229628.explanation: Public transport should be cheap, not free. If it becomes free, public transport will be too burdened, at the expense of cycling.

229629.explanation: Only children from minimum-income families may use public transport for free.

229630.explanation: This way volunteers are rewarded.

229631.explanation: Yes, because there is demand for it.

229632.explanation: There is hardly any space for cars in the city center.

229633.explanation: Yes, because this means you can visit Amsterdam-West and Nieuw-West from Amsterdam-Noord by metro. That would be a huge improvement for transport in the city.

229634.explanation: The city center is already overcrowded.

229635.explanation: It is too busy now with pedestrians, cyclists, scooters, and cars. The streets are already narrow.

229636.explanation: This is too slow for cars.

229637.explanation: Window prostitution is a humiliation for the women involved; often they do it out of necessity and not for pleasure.

229639.explanation: Elderly people want to stay in their own neighborhood and this would be a great solution.

229640.explanation: Too many youth care institutions are guilty of human rights violations as established in the report 'lonely locked up.' Closed youth care must be stopped immediately.

229641.explanation: This falls under freedom of religion and freedom to make personal choices about what is best for oneself.

229642.explanation: There is a need for this as well. Many young people hang out on the street because they have nothing.

229643.explanation: There is no need for that.

229644.explanation: Yes, it is a part of culture.

229570.explanation: An advisory vote is not enough. We support binding referenda on major projects and large expenditures. Everyone should be able to have a say, not just a small group of people. In a citizens' council there will still be a small group that can restrict the freedoms of others.

229571.explanation: Focus on real crime, not symbolic policies. A coordinator to combat discrimination outside existing legislation is unnecessary. No role for the municipality in (positive) discrimination (free association). All human relationships must be fully voluntary on both sides.

229572.explanation: The distance between government and residents is growing, as is distrust. Decisions should be made at the most local level possible. We oppose further centralization of power. More influence for residents at neighborhood/district level.

229573.explanation: The collective labor agreement for municipal employees is binding. Amsterdam will not deviate from this.

229574.explanation: After years of increases, a decrease is appropriate. A tax on property is morally reprehensible. Tax has already been levied on the money used to pay for that property. The municipality of Amsterdam must balance the budget by cutting expenses, not by raising taxes.

229575.explanation: The tourist tax is now 12.5% of the overnight price (highest in Europe). We stand for freedom of enterprise. A tax on success (tax per customer/tourist) is morally reprehensible. It is forced skimming on free travel and trade, purely to fill the municipality's coffers.

229576.explanation: We strive for complete economic freedom. Every person may do whatever they want with their property as long as it does not cause demonstrable harm to others. Restaurants may remain open as long as they wish. In case of demonstrable inconvenience: agreements with entrepreneurs and residents.

229577.explanation: This is a task for companies and individuals, not the municipality. This also allows room for new ideas. The spreading of tourists thus occurs more organically because other parts of the beach can attract tourists themselves. There is an economic incentive to do this from the market.

229578.explanation: We oppose favoring specific groups (special interest). A tax on property is also morally reprehensible. Tax has already been levied on the money used to pay for that property.

229579.explanation: This is a major market disruption. Retail spaces in the center face heavy restrictions via the Environmental Plan. By giving these buildings a broader function, they naturally become interesting, for example for housing or other uses that contribute to livability.

229580.explanation: The municipality should focus on safety and basic services. Schiphol is not a municipal basic service. Public ownership leads to political interference and inefficiency. Private owners and investors can better invest, innovate, and take responsibility.

229581.explanation: Every individual and business may decide for themselves whether to boycott a country/company. This is not a task for the municipality. The municipality should only focus on its core task: safety and basic services. That is already difficult enough for the municipality. The municipality should not interfere in international politics.

229583.explanation: The law is knowingly being violated here. Shelter is not a task for the municipality. Provide space for private shelter. This way it will automatically be aligned with the support base and capacity within society. The municipality should only focus on its core task: safety and basic services.

229584.explanation: It is up to the landlord to decide who may live in the property. Through freer rules such as adding extra floors, splitting up or temporary living, and more freedom for housing cooperatives/project developers without heavy municipal interference, more affordable housing will also become available.

229585.explanation: Language is a personal matter. Integration is not a task for the municipality. Therefore, the municipality will neither discourage nor encourage Dutch lessons. People are free to learn the language, but not at the taxpayer's expense. There is automatically an economic incentive to learn it.

229586.explanation: The municipality should not go beyond what is legally required. Provide space for private shelter. This way it automatically matches the support base and capacity within society. Everyone is welcome as long as they can support themselves.

229587.explanation: This will solve nothing. People who now put out waste outside collection times will also not make appointments.

229588.explanation: It is in the interest of owners, shops, and hospitality to facilitate their visitors, including toilets. Therefore, no tax money is needed for this. However, the municipality is making this too difficult because it leaves little room for private initiatives in public spaces.

229589.explanation: Everyone may organize an event in complete freedom, and the municipality must facilitate this as long as there is no demonstrable harm. By regulating much more at the district/neighborhood level, the support base for each park is carefully coordinated with local residents.

229590.explanation: The municipal tax burden is already too high. Payment is already made for this direct service. Charging additionally by the amount is double taxation. If the waste disposal tax goes to zero, then paying per amount of waste is a good idea.

229591.explanation: Artis chooses to operate as a foundation itself. The municipality already facilitates Artis with the land and several buildings owned by the municipality. A zoo is not a basic facility of the city. Artis must raise funds from entrance fees, donations, and hospitality. Subsidies kill economic incentives.

229592.explanation: Ports and terminals should ideally be privatized or operate with minimal regulation. The government has no role in dictating which ships may or may not dock, as long as no demonstrable harm is caused to others.

229593.explanation: By regulating much more at the neighborhood level, the support for parking versus trees is carefully coordinated with residents and surrounding entrepreneurs. This cannot be determined citywide as every situation is different. Ideally, residents and local businesses are the owners of the public space themselves.

229594.explanation: The plans are too advanced and allow room for new companies. The problematic part is that the municipality itself is the developer of the land and sets the zoning plan. The municipality should leave such developments entirely to market parties and only facilitate.

229595.explanation: As long as not a cent of tax money goes to new wind turbines and there are no neighbors opposing wind turbines, they may be placed anywhere possible. In practice, it will turn out that this will hardly succeed without subsidies.

229596.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Sports are a personal matter and not a core task of the government. With the tax cuts we propose, there will also be more financial room for funds or sponsorships to help people.

229597.explanation: The municipality must only focus on safety and basic services. Local media are not a basic service and can be excellently carried out by private initiatives. If a media company receives money from the municipality, there is by definition no independent news.

229598.explanation: The municipality must only focus on safety and basic services. Sports events are not a basic service and can be excellently organized by private parties. The municipality has only a facilitating role.

229599.explanation: The municipality must only focus on its core task: safety and basic services. We want to phase out subsidies. Giving priority to a particular culture based on nationality or ethnicity is immoral.

229600.explanation: By favoring one form of education, the municipality directly interferes with the content of education. We want equal opportunities for all forms of education and less municipal interference with the curriculum and organization.

229601.explanation: Compulsory education starts only at age 5. The municipality would go further than necessary, which leads to high costs. We want less spending. Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. This is a personal matter, not a core task.

229602.explanation: Subsidies for a new theater are forced redistribution: taxpayers' money is used to finance something not everyone appreciates or uses. A theater is not a basic service and must be funded by private parties through entrance fees, hospitality, and sponsorship.

229603.explanation: The municipality must only focus on safety and basic services. Sports are not a basic service. Subsidies lead to forced taxes to finance individual choices. Sports are personal and not a core task of the government. Tax cuts create more financial space.

229604.explanation: There must be more enforcement to combat demonstrable nuisance and insecurity. This does not require more enforcement officers but simply a different prioritization. Enforcement officers should only be deployed to guarantee safety rather than bothering entrepreneurs and residents.

229605.explanation: As morally reprehensible as hissing may be, freedom of expression is absolute and may not be restricted by the state, as long as it remains words and causes no direct threat or concrete harm.

229606.explanation: Enforcement officers receive too little training to handle weapons. Deployment of more trained police must become the norm. We will not expand the government's monopoly on violence.

229607.explanation: Every person may do with their body as they wish, as long as it does not cause demonstrable harm to others. Soft drugs do not equal harm or nuisance. In case of harm or nuisance, enforcement is required.

229608.explanation: Under the pretext of enforcement, residents' behavior is monitored and judged. People no longer have privacy, and it is unclear what happens to the data. No municipal mass surveillance or behavioral monitoring.

229609.explanation: Enforcement officers are citizens with the same rights as others. As long as the clothing does not pose a safety risk or undermine uniformity in a way that undermines the function, there is no reason for prohibition. No one is harmed by someone's personal clothing choice.

229610.explanation: We strive for complete personal freedom. Every person may dress as they wish, as long as it does not cause demonstrable harm. Face coverings cause no demonstrable harm. The municipality should set an example in this.

229611.explanation: Freedom for housing cooperatives without heavy municipal interference. Housing corporations experience many obstacles from the government. With more freedom to build, split, or add floors to homes, the housing supply will increase. No additional municipal requirements on top of national rules.

229612.explanation: It is up to the landlord to decide who may live in the home. Through freer rules such as adding floors, splitting, or temporary living and more freedom for housing cooperatives without heavy municipal interference, more affordable housing will become available.

229613.explanation: The municipality should only focus on safety and basic services. The municipal organization can therefore be significantly smaller. The municipality should sell all buildings it owns if they are not dedicated to safety or basic services such as schools.

229614.explanation: Private market parties can much better assess where demand exists. They also have a direct interest in delivering quality. The municipality should only facilitate via zoning plans. Creativity and innovation never come from the municipality.

229615.explanation: Every person may do whatever they want with their property, as long as it does not cause demonstrable harm to others and does not restrict others' freedoms. Holiday rentals fall under free economic traffic. Restrictions are a gross violation of property rights.

229616.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. It is not the municipality's role to encourage or discourage where certain people choose to live. Giving money to elderly people if they move to smaller housing is a market-distorting incentive.

229617.explanation: Housing shortages are caused by the market-distorting obligation to build social housing. Without such obligations, there will be less regulatory pressure, making it easier to build homes. If there is demand for more affordable housing, these will be built as well. And 40% is more than the national average.

229618.explanation: Every person should be free to do with their property as they wish, as long as they do not cause demonstrable harm to others or restrict others' freedoms. Renting out or reselling falls under free economic activity. Restrictions are a serious infringement on property rights.

229619.explanation: People take better care of their own environment when they have full ownership of the land instead of leasehold. Perpetual leasehold is a form of forced payment that undermines property rights and is a hidden property tax to the municipality.

229620.explanation: This undermines the principle that paid work should pay off. Social assistance is intended as a temporary safety net. Transitioning to full paid work then pays off less or not at all.

229621.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Pets are not a core municipal task. They are personal choices. The proposed tax cuts also create more financial room for private funds or charities to help people.

229622.explanation: Shelter should not be a government task. It offers space for private shelters. This automatically aligns with the support and capacity within society. The proposed tax reductions also create more room for funds or charities to assist people.

229623.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Deductibles are not a core municipal task. They are personal circumstances that should not cause others to suffer. Tax cuts create space for charities to help people.

229624.explanation: Subsidies lead to taxes on others to finance personal circumstances. Income is a personal matter; it is not a core municipal task to artificially increase it. Tax reductions also create more financial room for funds or charities to help people.

229625.explanation: Every person may live their life as they wish, as long as they do not cause demonstrable harm to others. Mandating this is coercion and increases the barrier to paid work because it takes time and energy. The municipality would also exceed national regulations by doing so.

229626.explanation: Subsidies lead to taxes on others to finance personal circumstances. Income is a personal matter; it is not a core municipal task to artificially increase it. Tax reductions also create more financial room for funds or charities to help people.

229627.explanation: Free money does not exist. Numerous studies have shown that a basic income does not contribute to a higher standard of living or stimulate entrepreneurship. It removes all economic incentives to contribute to society.

229628.explanation: It would not be free; the taxpayer pays. Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Encouraging certain transport choices is not a core municipal task. Safety and basic services are core municipal tasks.

229629.explanation: It would not be free; the taxpayer pays. Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Encouraging certain transport choices is not a core municipal task. Safety and basic services are core municipal tasks.

229630.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Facilitating certain institutions in this way is not a core municipal task and is based on sheer arbitrariness.

229631.explanation: If a route is not profitable or a redesign of routes would be more efficient, different choices should be allowed. The public transport operator should be free to make such decisions.

229632.explanation: By regulating parking much more at neighborhood level, support can be carefully coordinated with residents and surrounding businesses. This cannot be decided uniformly across the entire center, as each situation is different. Ideally, residents and local businesses would own the public space themselves.

229633.explanation: We support binding referenda for major projects and expenditures. A referendum should be held when tax money is involved.

229634.explanation: We support freedom of transportation. No forced discouragement without clear necessity. The municipality must strike a good balance between different transport options. In Amsterdam, the balance has now swung too far against cars.

229635.explanation: By regulating parking and car use much more at neighborhood level, support is carefully coordinated with residents and surrounding businesses. This cannot be decided uniformly across the entire center, as each situation is different. Ideally, residents and local entrepreneurs would own the public space themselves.

229636.explanation: By regulating speed limits much more at neighborhood level, support is carefully coordinated with residents and surrounding businesses. This cannot be decided citywide, as each situation is different. Ideally, residents and local entrepreneurs would own the public space themselves.

229637.explanation: Every person may do with their body as they wish, as long as they do not cause demonstrable harm to others. Prostitution should be treated as a normal profession and decriminalized. It is not up to the municipality to decide whether someone may practice their profession.

229639.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. It is not up to the municipality to encourage or discourage housing choices. This cannot be decided citywide. More homes will also be built if there are fewer regulations and lower costs.

229640.explanation: If the municipality is the client or commissioner, it should have the freedom to stop dealing with institutions that provide poor services.

229641.explanation: Many municipalities make this possible by providing about 70% of the normal allowance through the personal budget (PGB) to the care recipient for their own choices. This should remain possible.

229642.explanation: Subsidies lead to forced taxes on others to finance individual choices. Entertainment for youth is not a core municipal task. This cannot be decided citywide either. By regulating more at neighborhood level, needs can be carefully coordinated with residents.

229643.explanation: Subsidies lead to taxes on others to finance personal circumstances. Parenting is a personal matter and not a core municipal task. Tax reductions also create more financial room for funds or charities to help people.

229644.explanation: Every person may do and leave as they wish, as long as no demonstrable harm is caused. Recreational fishing, where the fish is often released afterwards, causes no demonstrable harm to anyone.

229570.explanation: A citizens’ assembly also directly involves young tenants and starters in housing policy. Their experiences with housing stress come honestly onto the table. This ensures supported, future-proof choices and increases trust in municipal youth housing policy.

229571.explanation: A coordinator against discrimination and racism ensures equal treatment in the neighborhood. By taking signals seriously and linking policy, people feel safer on the street without repression or mistrust.

229572.explanation: Districts with their own administration are close to residents and see problems more quickly. Abolishing them increases distance and bureaucracy. Local control helps make humane policy and prevents decisions being imposed on people.

229573.explanation: A day off on Keti Koti recognizes historical injustice and its effects today. It creates space for reflection and respect and shows that the municipality chooses humanity over looking away. But the day off must apply to all Dutch citizens, not only municipal employees.

229574.explanation: Lower property tax (ozb) reduces fixed costs for vulnerable entrepreneurs such as Asian restaurants. This prevents the municipality’s policy from unintentionally undermining livelihood security and contributes to fair opportunities and preserving cultural entrepreneurship.

229575.explanation: Tourists cause extra waste and pressure on public spaces. A higher tourist tax makes them fairly contribute to clean streets and waste management, so residents bear less nuisance and costs.

229576.explanation: Extended opening hours give vulnerable restaurants extra revenue to absorb stricter rules. This helps the municipality support entrepreneurs to stay afloat, maintain jobs, and prevent cultural hospitality venues from disappearing.

229577.explanation: More tourism increases waste pressure and costs for residents. By not spending money attracting tourists, the municipality can invest in cleaner streets, less waste, and a fairer waste policy for residents.

229578.explanation: By charging property tax only to owners, fixed costs for vulnerable entrepreneurs decrease. This gives relief to restaurants already hit by stricter rules and prevents policy from unnecessarily causing closures.

229579.explanation: By purchasing retail properties itself, the municipality can keep rents affordable. This gives vulnerable entrepreneurs more certainty and prevents closures caused by rising costs.

229580.explanation: Precisely by not selling shares in Schiphol, the municipality retains influence over local decisions. This makes policy more humane, transparent, and gives residents more say over their living environment.

229581.explanation: Collaborating with companies that service the Israeli military links trade to military conflicts. By avoiding this, the municipality shows commitment to peace and ethical, responsible policy that does not support violence.

229583.explanation: Asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay must leave as soon as possible. The reception problem lies with EU policy. Temporary shelter must be arranged for people who immediately have no roof over their heads.

229584.explanation: Refugees with residence permits are often especially vulnerable but do not receive priority for scarce social housing. The municipality must ultimately provide housing. The Distribution Act, effective from February 1, 2024, must be understandable to everyone.

229585.explanation: Good Dutch language skills strengthen solidarity and participation. By investing more in language lessons, labor migrants can fully participate, contributing to a municipality that truly works for all residents.

229586.explanation: Limiting shelter increases inequality and division. More shelter promotes solidarity and justice, showing that the municipality works for all residents, not just a small group.

229587.explanation: Mandatory agreements increase stress for residents. Free drop-off points or flexible collection enhance cooperation and responsibility, better contributing to a clean, green, and accessible city.

229588.explanation: Free public toilets for people with diverse abilities strengthen inclusivity and independence. They make central places more accessible and support local cooperation by making public spaces fair and user-friendly for everyone.

229589.explanation: Fewer events in parks protect the local community and nature. This allows residents to safely and pleasantly share the space, builds trust and cooperation in the neighborhood, and keeps public spaces livable for all.

229590.explanation: Higher costs per amount of household waste mainly burden residents and discourage cooperation. Better solutions involve shared responsibility and smart waste management, so the city stays cleaner without additional financial pressure.

229591.explanation: Artis fulfills a social role as an educational and recreational site and provides employment. Municipal support helps maintain this, prevents unemployment, and ensures employees and visitors benefit from a valuable city school and zoo.

229592.explanation: Mostly older tourists with higher spending power arriving on large cruise ships benefit Amsterdam’s economy and international appeal.

229593.explanation: Green in the city protects quality of life for young and old, offers clean air and spaces for play and meeting, while also ensuring a city where everyone can live, thrive, and age healthily.

229594.explanation: An industrial estate in the Lutkemeerpolder threatens nature, recreation, living environment, and food supply for all ages. Stopping it protects green spaces, provides room for housing, and prevents people and the environment from being sacrificed for short-term economic gain.

229595.explanation: Dispersed placement protects living environments elsewhere and ensures both young and elderly benefit from clean energy without one neighborhood being disproportionately burdened.

229596.explanation: Free membership for adults with low income promotes health, social connection, and participation in society. This way, everyone gets the chance to live actively and join in despite financial barriers.

229597.explanation: Local media connect residents and provide insight into municipal policy. Less funding weakens independent reporting and engagement, making people feel more powerless and less able to participate in their own living environment.

229598.explanation: Investing in top sports events strengthens corporate profits, not society. Municipal funds are better spent on housing, sustainability, and digital inclusion, so residents gain more control instead of feeling powerless against markets and big interests.

229599.explanation: Subsidies based on quality, not background, ensure fair opportunities and creativity. This allows all cultural creators space to contribute to society without markets or origins determining participation.

229600.explanation: Extra funding for three-year introductory classes gives young people early opportunities to become socially engaged through internships and participation jobs.

229601.explanation: Free preschool promotes equal opportunities from a young age. This allows all children, regardless of background, to participate and develop, providing youth and families with a strong foundation for a resilient future society.

229602.explanation: New construction for Theater de Meervaart primarily benefits large institutions and market interests. It also intrudes into the nature of Sloterplas. Municipal funds are better spent on cultural initiatives by and for residents, so creativity and participation stay within the community.

229603.explanation: More investment in sports and exercise prevents health problems like obesity and reduces healthcare costs. This keeps residents active and healthy, easing pressure on care and contributing to a sustainable future society.

229604.explanation: More enforcement officers increase control but do not solve underlying problems. Safety arises from strong neighborhoods, equal opportunities, and respect-not through additional enforcement and repression. Residents must feel free and safe without excessive surveillance pressure.

229605.explanation: More plainclothes police officers help stop aggressive behavior without requiring women and vulnerable groups to adapt. This makes public spaces safer and better aligned with residents’ lived experiences.

229606.explanation: Use of force by enforcement officers increases fear and risks, emphasizing control over trust. Safety is created through respect, equal opportunities, and strong neighborhoods, allowing everyone to move freely and safely without weapons.

229607.explanation: Tourists generate income that the municipality can use for livable neighborhoods and local facilities. By maintaining regulated sales to foreigners, there remains room to build together a solidaristic, liberal, and financially healthy city.

229608.explanation: More camera surveillance at underground waste containers helps prevent illegal dumping. This keeps the city cleaner, reduces resident irritation, and manages waste more fairly and humanely.

229609.explanation: Banning headscarves or kippahs for enforcement officers excludes diversity and undermines community and equality. Safety is created through respect and inclusion, not by imposing uniform dress codes.

229610.explanation: Equality and trust must not be undermined. Safety in government buildings grows through respect and care, not through fear and repression.

229611.explanation: Selling social housing reduces the public housing stock and increases pressure on young people seeking homes. By explicitly requesting housing corporations not to sell, more affordable and collective housing remains available for everyone.

229612.explanation: Limiting social housing to long-term residents excludes newcomers and youth. To create a collective and public housing supply, homes must remain accessible to everyone living in the city, especially young people and essential workers.

229613.explanation: Selling unused buildings reduces public housing and fuels lucrative real estate trade. By retaining buildings, the municipality can create more collective or social housing and neighborhood facilities, effectively implementing youth housing policy.

229614.explanation: By deciding what is built on municipal land itself, the municipality can ensure public and cooperative housing. This prevents speculation and keeps the housing stock affordable and available for young people and other residents.

229615.explanation: Holiday rentals take affordable homes off the market and drive up rents. A ban protects public housing stock, makes homes available for youth, and supports cooperative and social housing in the city center.

229616.explanation: Older people moving to smaller homes free up larger houses for youth and families. Fair financial compensation encourages this turnover, strengthens collective living, and supports youth housing policy.

229617.explanation: Building at least 40% social rental in new construction keeps public housing stock stable. This prevents exclusion of youth and low-income groups and supports fair and collective housing policy.

229618.explanation: Owner-occupation prevents speculation and exploitative landlords, keeping homes available for youth and others. This supports collective and public living and keeps the housing market fair and accessible.

229619.explanation: Ground lease makes housing more expensive and encourages speculation. Abolishing it makes housing affordable, protects residents from real estate lobbyists, and supports a fair and public housing system.

229620.explanation: A higher earnings threshold gives welfare recipients a better chance to progress without losing income. This way, everyone can continue contributing and no one is written off in society.

229621.explanation: Reimbursing veterinary costs helps low-income people properly care for their pets. This keeps elderly, youth, and vulnerable people engaged and ensures no one is excluded from a caring community. Attention is needed to set a financial limit to prevent unlimited pet acquisitions.

229622.explanation: More sleeping places for homeless and roofless people ensure everyone can stay overnight safely and with dignity. This helps them sustain themselves without anyone being written off, making society more humane and caring.

229623.explanation: The personal healthcare deductible can be a barrier for people on minimum income to use care. By reimbursing it, everyone stays healthy and can participate with dignity in society without financial stress.

229624.explanation: Less support for low-income people increases inequality and poverty risk. To give everyone a chance to improve and live with dignity, the municipality must invest in care, housing, and opportunities for the vulnerable.

229625.explanation: Making welfare dependent on work punishes the vulnerable and limits their freedom. To allow everyone to progress and live with dignity, the municipality should provide support without unnecessary obligations or stigma.

229626.explanation: Waiving waste disposal fees helps people with low incomes make ends meet. Stopping this would place extra burdens on vulnerable groups and hinder everyone from living and residing with dignity in the city.

229627.explanation: A basic income trial provides residents with financial security, encourages independence, and creates opportunities for both young and old. This way, no one is written off and everyone can live, reside, and progress with dignity in the city.

229628.explanation: Free public transport ensures that everyone, young and old, can move freely and equally without income being a barrier. It promotes sustainability, reduces car traffic, and makes the city safer, more social, livable, and accessible to all residents.

229629.explanation: Free public transport for children up to 18 years old ensures everyone can travel safely and independently to school, sports, or friends, regardless of income. It promotes equality, reduces car traffic, and contributes to a cleaner, livable, and accessible city for youth.

229630.explanation: Free parking hours for volunteers of social and religious organizations encourage engagement and solidarity. It lowers barriers to volunteering, strengthens the community, and makes the city more accessible without financial obstacles.

229631.explanation: A direct metro connection from Gaasperplas to Central Station guarantees fast, accessible mobility for all residents, reduces car use and traffic congestion, and ensures Southeast remains well connected to work, school, and social facilities.

229632.explanation: Fewer parking spaces in the city center hinder accessibility for the elderly, people with varying abilities, and families, thereby excluding certain groups and limiting their mobility, independence, and social life.

229633.explanation: A direct metro connection from Amsterdam North to Schiphol improves the accessibility of these districts, makes public transport faster and more reliable, reduces car traffic, and aligns with a fair, clean, and public mobility system.

229634.explanation: Keeping the city center accessible by car is necessary for, among others, (cultural) entrepreneurs and people with different abilities so that the city remains livable and accessible for everyone, also in a future with clean and free public transport as the base.

229635.explanation: More car-free streets limit accessibility for people dependent on cars. A livable city can be better achieved through smart traffic management, free public transport, and low-traffic zones without completely closing everything off.

229636.explanation: Lower speed limits on roads with bike lanes increase safety for cyclists and pedestrians, reduce accidents, and make the city more livable. This makes mobility fairer, safer, and accessible to everyone, as intended by the plan for clean and social transport.

229637.explanation: Banning window prostitution restricts freedom of movement and drives workers underground, which actually decreases safety. Strong neighborhoods and equality arise from protection and regulation, not repression.

229639.explanation: Allowing elderly people to live independently longer strengthens their independence and safety, reduces loneliness, alienation, and pressure on care institutions, and contributes to strong, livable neighborhoods where care is nearby and accessible to everyone.

229640.explanation: Collaborating with underperforming youth care harms young people directly. The municipality must prioritize quality and safety so that young people receive the care and support they need and their voice and well-being are taken seriously.

229641.explanation: Choice in care increases safety and comfort, especially for vulnerable groups. Respecting faith, culture, or beliefs strengthens equality and trust, allowing people to truly feel free and safe in the care environment.

229642.explanation: Youth centers provide young people with their own place to think along, learn, and connect with the neighborhood. They strengthen participation, volunteer work, and social cohesion, and make young people actively involved in local decision-making and their living environment.

229643.explanation: A school breakfast gives all children a healthy start to the day, reduces inequalities among children, and helps them participate in school and neighborhood activities with concentration and energy, fitting with equal opportunities and strong neighborhoods.

229644.explanation: Free fishing with a rod encourages neighborhood participation, connection with nature, and social activities in the area. It is an accessible way for Amsterdam residents to relax together and experience social engagement.

229570.explanation: Pirates stand for democracy. That is why we want to give residents more opportunities to think along, decide, and participate. The citizens' assembly has proven to be a method whereby various groups are willing to move towards each other and reach broadly supported decisions.

229571.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to equal treatment. Therefore, it is good if a general coordinator monitors and enforces against all forms of discrimination and racism. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229572.explanation: Pirates stand for local democracy. In recent years, we've been able to mean a lot to residents from the city districts. The districts should actually be given more say again! We have therefore drawn up a plan for a more democratic administrative system. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229573.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom and abhor slavery and exploitation. That is why we believe the abolition of slavery may be celebrated. Additionally, on this day we want to reflect on modern forms of slavery and take measures to prevent them. Inform yourself: ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229574.explanation: Pirates stand for fair taxation. The municipality has many tasks and receives too little money from the national government to perform them properly. That is why property tax (ozb) for property owners is unfortunately necessary to spare poorer people and to pay for, among others, better youth care and poverty reduction.

229575.explanation: Pirates stand for fair taxation. Tourists use the facilities that we as a municipality pay for. Therefore, they should also contribute. Thus, increasing the tourist tax in tourist hotspots is fair so that Amsterdammers themselves can be spared!

229576.explanation: Pirates oppose prudishness and unnecessary regulation. Strict closing times mean you can’t even get food after a night out. We grant entrepreneurs and consumers more freedom, of course in good consultation with local residents. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229577.explanation: Pirates oppose unnecessary government spending. Why should your tax money be used to advertise to attract EVEN more tourists? Amsterdam is such a beautiful city, it sells itself! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229578.explanation: Pirates stand for a just tax system. Get the money where it is, from the big capital of the real estate tycoons! And not from the SMEs that keep Amsterdam’s economy running. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229579.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom. Project 1012 was a great farce where loads of tax money was wasted to buy out entrepreneurs and transform the city center into a dull uniformity. Better protect shops for local Amsterdammers! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229580.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to clean air and want to limit airplane noise. Therefore, the municipality should actually expand its influence on Schiphol to better protect local residents. This can be achieved by limiting transfer flights. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229581.explanation: Pirates stand for human rights and international law. Therefore, we do not want the municipality to be complicit in war crimes. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229583.explanation: Pirates stand for human rights. Therefore, every person deserves a roof over their head until deportation has taken place. This prevents people from roaming the streets, falling out of sight, and never leaving. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229584.explanation: Pirates stand for equal rights. Therefore, status holders with urgency deserve the same priority as other Dutch people with the same urgency. This also prevents the municipality from spending a fortune on extremely expensive emergency shelters. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229585.explanation: Pirates stand for a fair economy and a just distribution of burdens. Therefore, the companies where these labor migrants work should pay themselves for their employees’ language courses. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229586.explanation: Pirates stand for human rights. Every person deserves a roof over their head. This certainly applies to refugees waiting for a decision on their status. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229587.explanation: Pirates stand for effective policy and sustainability. Bulk waste collection by appointment is a bureaucratic monster. Moreover, many people with tight budgets currently collect half their household goods from the street. A beautiful circular economy! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229588.explanation: Pirates stand for an inclusive society. Therefore, it is important that there are sufficient public toilets for everyone, including women and people with disabilities. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229589.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom. Therefore, fun events can be organized as long as this is done in consultation with local residents, respecting nature and ecology. And in public parks, events should be freely accessible. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229590.explanation: Pirates stand for privacy. The only way to have residents pay more is a waste pass that tracks how much waste you throw away daily. Places where this has been introduced show it does not reduce waste but leads to more illegal dumping outside bins. So, a bad plan!

229591.explanation: Pirates stand for good education and a green city. Artis plays an important role in education about nature. Therefore, we want to continue the good relationship the municipality has with Artis. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229592.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to clean air. Therefore, we must ban cruise ships from the city. A cruise ship causes as much fine particulate matter emissions as millions of cars. Stop this madness! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229593.explanation: Pirates stand for a green city. That is why we want more trees and plants in the city. When we offer more space for bicycles, public transport, and shared mobility, car ownership becomes less necessary, and fewer parking spaces are needed. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229594.explanation: The Lutkemeer has Amsterdam's last fertile sea clay, carries a high flood risk as a ‘deep bathtub’, and is unsuitable for industrial use in terms of urban logistics, unless the city invests huge amounts in additional roads. Therefore, Food Park is a better solution, also financially!

229595.explanation: Pirates stand for a green and healthy city. Therefore, we should not build wind turbines close to homes (and in nature). This undermines public support for the energy transition, which is so desperately needed! Good alternatives exist, such as aquathermy and geothermal energy. amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229596.explanation: Pirates stand for equal opportunities and a healthy city. Healthy living is currently unaffordable for people with a tight budget. That is why we want to make sports possible for everyone, regardless of how much money you have. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229597.explanation: Pirates stand for democracy. Good information provision by local media is essential for this. That is why in recent years we have secured extra funding for local media, such as the Staatskrant and Westerpost. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229598.explanation: Pirates stand for efficient policy. That is why we prefer to spend tax money on good facilities for residents, rather than on megalomaniac prestige projects. Moreover, Amsterdam is such a beautiful city that events are happy to come even without us pumping tax money into them. amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229599.explanation: Pirates stand for equal rights for everyone. Therefore, we oppose discrimination and want proposals to be judged on their content. Of course, a good reflection of society can be considered. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229600.explanation: Pirates stand for flexible forms of education. A three-year first year of secondary school promotes broadening of education. Customization is essential to give both early and late bloomers the best chances. By adequate grouping based on learning speed and level, students receive better education. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229601.explanation: Pirates stand for equal opportunities for every child. That is why we see preschool, as part of education, as an important basic right. It must therefore be accessible to people with tight budgets. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229602.explanation: Pirates stand for accessible art and culture. That is why Nieuw-West deserves a beautiful theater with sufficient capacity. However, it must NOT be built in the water. Local residents have proposed a beautiful alternative, a better plan. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229603.explanation: Pirates stand for a healthy city. That is why we want to make sports accessible to everyone, including people with a tight budget. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229604.explanation: Pirates stand for a free and safe city. Replacing police with enforcement officers is a disguised cut in safety. That is why we want more focus on prevention, social cohesion, and detectives to catch real criminals, and fewer patronizing enforcement officers. Inform yourself: amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229605.explanation: Pirates stand for effective policy. The police already lack capacity to pursue serious criminals. Tackling harassment and verbal abuse is difficult in practice, while it heavily burdens the criminal justice system. Setting priorities is a necessity. More information: amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229606.explanation: Pirates stand for a free and safe city. Replacing police with enforcement officers is a disguised cut in safety. The monopoly on violence carries great responsibility. Tasks involving risk of violence belong with the police. More training in de-escalation is also desirable there. ppnl.nl

229607.explanation: Pirates stand for equal rights in a free city. A foreigner ban is discriminatory. Moreover, it means that Amsterdammers must identify themselves. This ‘weed pass’ policy already fails massively in southern Netherlands. That is why we have successfully opposed this in Amsterdam in recent years.

229608.explanation: Pirates stand for privacy. That is why we want less surveillance and repression. Spend that money on collecting bulky waste more often instead! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229609.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom and equal rights. Enforcement officers may reflect the city’s population and, like everyone, have freedom of expression and religion. The most important thing is that they remain neutral in performing their duties. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229610.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom and equal rights. That is why we do not want to exclude people based on their choice of clothing. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229611.explanation: Pirates do not see housing as a market but as a social right. That is why we want many more social rental homes. This is necessary to end the housing shortage and the endless waiting lists! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229612.explanation: Pirates stand for affordable housing for everyone. That is why we mainly focus on combating vacancy and adding extra homes. Additionally, reserving at least 50% for existing Amsterdammers is not a bad measure, so you can, for example, continue living in Amsterdam when you leave home. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229613.explanation: Pirates stand for affordable living space and also want to provide more room for social initiatives. Preferably, use those buildings for that! If we sell them off, the ‘market’ will make a monotonous product solely aimed at tourists and other wealthy people. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229614.explanation: Pirates stand for autonomous initiatives. We see much future in housing cooperatives, where not the government but residents themselves decide about their living environment. The government should set frameworks (for example, about affordability) but not bureaucratically nitpick every detail.

229615.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom and equal rights. Vacation rentals account for only 2% of overnight stays. Let Amsterdammers with tight budgets benefit a little as well from travelers who want to visit our city. Moreover, that stimulates cultural exchange. Better target the hotel industry!

229616.explanation: Pirates stand for affordable, suitable housing for everyone and effective policy. Some elderly want to move to a smaller home but would suffer financially. A relocation grant can help both elderly and families find appropriate housing. ppnl.nl

229617.explanation: Pirates stand for affordable, suitable housing for everyone. That is why we want 100% social construction until 65% social housing is achieved in every neighborhood. This is necessary to ensure that housing supply meets demand and to solve the housing shortage and waiting lists. amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229618.explanation: Pirates stand for affordable, suitable housing for everyone. Speculators drive prices up. Houses are for living in, not for profit! Therefore, we also want to combat vacancy with hefty fines. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229619.explanation: Pirates do not see housing as a market but as a social right. By abolishing leasehold, the municipality puts the city up for sale to the ‘market’. Moreover, leasehold owners also contribute somewhat to the facilities that increase the value of their property. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229620.explanation: Pirates stand for simple, effective social security. With an unconditional basic income, you can earn as much on the side as you want. As you earn more, your basic income decreases. This way, we encourage work but end poverty, bureaucracy, and privacy violations.

229621.explanation: Pirates want to end poverty. Commercial chains make good care for pets unaffordable for many. That is why we want this to be free for people with tight budgets. This can be arranged well via the city pass. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229622.explanation: Pirates stand for human rights. Everyone deserves a roof over their head: Housing First. That is why in recent years we have successfully submitted motions to use vacant properties for extra shelters and as temporary homes for economically homeless people. More info: amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229623.explanation: Pirates want to end poverty and see good healthcare as a basic right. The healthcare deductible is an antisocial penalty for being ill, especially for people with chronic diseases. Many people with tight budgets avoid care, worsening problems and increasing care costs. Abolish this!

229624.explanation: Pirates want to end poverty. That it still exists in a wealthy country like the Netherlands is sad enough. Cutting even more from poverty alleviation is outright antisocial. We aim to end poverty with an unconditional basic income. https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229625.explanation: Pirates oppose any form of forced labor. We want to end poverty with an unconditional basic income. Experience shows that people then engage in socially meaningful work voluntarily or paid, driven by intrinsic motivation. https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229626.explanation: Pirates want to end poverty. That it still exists in a wealthy country like the Netherlands is sad enough. Cutting even more from poverty alleviation is outright antisocial. We aim to end poverty with an unconditional basic income. https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229627.explanation: Pirates stand for simple, effective social security. That is why we want an unconditional basic income. This ends poverty, bureaucracy, and privacy-violating checks. Experience also shows people then do more socially meaningful work voluntarily or paid.

229628.explanation: Pirates stand for good basic facilities. Good and free public transport offers a great alternative to cars. Highways can then become bike paths where e-bikes and large cargo bikes can use the roadway and cars are guests. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229629.explanation: Pirates stand for good basic facilities. Good and free public transport offers a great alternative to cars. Highways can then become bike paths where e-bikes and large cargo bikes can use the roadway and cars are guests. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229630.explanation: Pirates stand for more support for social initiatives. It is wonderful when people take care of each other. Volunteers deserve more support. Free parking hours can be a good solution for volunteers who need this. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/
