229631.explanation: Pirates stand for good public transport. It is disgraceful that Metro 53 is being cut! Southeast is growing and needs better public transport connections to the rest of the city. A direct connection to the center is necessary, especially for people with mobility issues. The Connected City has a good alternative plan.

229632.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to clean air and good basic facilities, such as good public transport. With more space for bicycles and public transport, we can make the city center car-free. That means fewer parking spaces are needed. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229633.explanation: Pirates stand for good basic facilities, such as good public transport. Schiphol is already well connected by train. Moreover, we want fewer flights to protect local residents from nuisance. That also reduces traffic to Schiphol. The North-South metro line can be extended to Purmerend.

229634.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to clean air and good basic facilities, such as good public transport. With more space for bicycles and public transport, we can make the city center car-free. This is cleaner and safer. It prevents illness and death caused by air pollution. Inform yourself: amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229635.explanation: Pirates stand for the right to clean air and good basic facilities, such as reliable public transport. With more space for bicycles and public transport, we can make the city center car-free. This is cleaner and safer. And it prevents illness and death caused by air pollution. More info: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229636.explanation: Pirates advocate for good basic facilities. Good and free public transport offers a great alternative to the car. Highways can then become bicycle paths where e-bikes and large cargo bikes can ride on the roadway, with cars as guests, limited to 30 km/h. Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229637.explanation: Pirates stand for freedom and self-determination. Sex workers should be free to operate in designated areas with window prostitution. It’s better to talk WITH sex workers than ABOUT sex workers and to offer them more opportunities to work legally, independently, and safely. https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229639.explanation: Pirates value freedom and self-determination. Many older people appreciate being able to live and remain as independent as possible for as long as possible. This 'intermediate step' can actually also turn out to be cheaper than hiding people away in nursing homes. https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229640.explanation: Pirates see good youth care as an essential basic right. When youth care institutions repeatedly make mistakes, children suffer harm. Sometimes these children carry these effects for life. Children and young people deserve better care! Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229641.explanation: Pirates stand for good and appropriate care as a basic right. For some people, it’s important that healthcare providers respect their life beliefs. This is a relatively simple way to significantly improve the quality of care for these people. More info: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229642.explanation: Pirates stand for a free and safe city, with better facilities for young people. This is a proven effective way to prevent youth from drifting into crime and thus a simple, cheap, and effective way to improve safety and prevent nuisance. ppnl.nl/amsterdam

229643.explanation: Pirates advocate for a healthy city and equal opportunities for every child. That’s why we want to offer healthy meals at schools. This ensures no child goes hungry in class. This demonstrably improves students’ academic performance and health. More info: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl

229644.explanation: Pirates stand for the rights of humans and animals. Mutilating fish for entertainment is reprehensible. We prefer to use our sharp hook to bring down corrupt white-collar criminals ;) Inform yourself: https://amsterdam.piratenpartij.nl/

229570.explanation: LEF supports direct democracy where citizens are chosen by lottery. This way, all ideas are heard and everyone participates in discussion and decisions, instead of a small group of politicians. LEF wants citizen councils to be requestable on any topic and the outcomes of these councils to be binding, not optional.

229571.explanation: Racism and discrimination affect large groups of people, from skin color to sexual orientation and disabilities. Besides reporting points, it’s helpful if someone has the explicit task of ensuring that everyone in this city feels comfortable and can participate fully in society and facilities.

229572.explanation: Local democracy is very important. You cannot govern a city with 930,000 inhabitants with only 8 aldermen and 1 mayor. LEF supports boroughs with their own governance. They know what’s going on and what rules apply because they live there. One borough is not the same as another.

229573.explanation: LEF supports more public holidays. July 1 could be a day off with a memorial ceremony on June 30, like May 4 and 5. Breaking the chains is an important moment to collectively reflect on history and its impact today.

229574.explanation: Property tax (OZB) is an important source of income for the municipality. Without property tax, we cannot finance the future and livability of our beautiful city.

229575.explanation: Tourists place great pressure on the city’s livability. The extra revenues should go directly to measures to combat nuisance. Amsterdam&Partners, the tourism bureau promoting Amsterdam as a destination, should be abolished immediately. Campaigns against drunk Brits do not work.

229576.explanation: LEF supports a vibrant nightlife. This includes free opening hours for hospitality venues. Many people work at night to keep our city running. These residents should also be able to get a meal or do groceries at night.

229577.explanation: Limit the number of visitors to 18 million per year. Close 2 of the 5 runways at Schiphol. DO SOMETHING! Abolish Amsterdam & Partners, the marketing agency with a budget of 13.3 million behind failed campaigns targeting British bachelor parties. Negative publicity is still publicity.

229578.explanation: Currently, both groups pay. If the property tax on users is withdrawn, we cannot finance the beautiful projects that keep our city livable. It has to come one way or another.

229579.explanation: That depends on the new purpose of the buildings. If it becomes a brown café, a community center, or youth housing, then yes. If it contributes to further gentrification and sterility, then no. If it concerns landlords who leave properties empty and to decay, we support expropriation without market-value compensation.

229580.explanation: The municipality must retain its share in the airport to remain an activist shareholder vis-à-vis the national government, which owns 70% of the shares.

229581.explanation: LEF stands for peace. The municipality must not be complicit in genocide or apartheid in any way. Trade and exchange with Israel must stop as soon as possible. The war industry must be dismantled immediately. More power for women and children increases the chance of peace and reduces suffering.

229583.explanation: Bed, bath, and bread is the minimum we can offer people. Do we proudly carry the title 'merciful' in our city coat of arms or not? Exactly, we warmly take in people who are less fortunate.

229584.explanation: The waiting lists and rental prices are insane. There is much housing potential through improved flow. LEF introduces Woon Wens, an app that smartly mixes and matches people to housing suited to their current living situation. This creates more housing for everyone, including status holders.

229585.explanation: Yes, language proficiency is crucial to succeed in this society. Language lessons must be free for newcomers (they used to be and it worked well).

229586.explanation: The dispersal law was not passed for nothing. Every municipality must take its fair share, including the wealthy municipalities in the Gooi region and the racist municipalities in the South.

229587.explanation: Both options must remain possible. Fixed collection points and times, and for a small fee, home collection. For entrepreneurs, making an appointment for everything is not feasible.

229588.explanation: The municipality placed a beautiful self-cleaning toilet in Oosterpark. We want one like this in all Amsterdam parks and easily accessible squares. And don’t forget to make sidewalks more accessible and free of obstacles. Also include descriptions of cityscapes in braille.

229589.explanation: Parks are for everyone to relax in. There are already enough events. We want RAVE ISLAND. A boosted island near Pampus where youth can rave loudly without causing noise nuisance. There will also be a Rave metro to Rave Island with a night schedule.

229590.explanation: Yes, introduce a pass system so that polluters pay and zero waste is rewarded. LEF also wants more reuse of organic materials, for example by providing worm hotels in every neighborhood. We throw away far too much collectively.

229591.explanation: Artis receives an annual subsidy of 4.55 million from the municipality. There are 1.27 million visitors yearly. Increase the ticket price by 3.60 and you’re there. 4.5 million can be better spent on maintaining public parks (should we invest in animal suffering?).

229592.explanation: LEF wants clean air in every Amsterdammer’s lungs. This is incompatible with large cruise ships (and river cruises) that leave huge amounts of soot in the city. We want to encourage clean transportation. We prefer to invest in rail and international train connections.

229593.explanation: LEF wants all private cars replaced by self-driving taxis. 80% of parking spaces will make way for our Park City plan: a connected web of green spaces, consisting of food forests, walking boulevards, benches for neighbors, playground and gym equipment, clean air, and less noise. Simply wonderful!

229594.explanation: Local and fresh vegetables are essential for a sustainable and healthy city. More urban farming is needed, and fewer boxes in the landscape (distribution centers, data centers, and storage). That’s why the Lutkemeerpolder must be preserved. We want organic vegetables and fruit grown locally.

229595.explanation: The best way to achieve climate goals is not by building new wind turbines. Our motto is: don't work harder, work smarter. Reward energy savings by introducing a carbon coin, place more solar panels on surfaces that do not yet generate electricity, and focus on geothermal energy and seasonal heat storage (KWO).

229596.explanation: Sports are extremely important to stay physically and mentally healthy. Being a member of a club increases the chance to meet people and find an (unpaid) job. It also saves healthcare costs, so it is a good investment!

229597.explanation: Local media are crucial to checking power. AT5 receives 2.9 million subsidy annually. They produce excellent television. No extra money is needed but certainly no cuts either.

229598.explanation: Top-level sports should be self-sustaining. We prefer getting many children to participate in sports rather than a select few reaching the top. It’s about fun, not performance.

229599.explanation: Diversity is highly valued at LEF. Everyone has the right to enjoyable, special, beautiful performances where they can see themselves reflected. A diversity of stories enriches our city!

229600.explanation: LEF supports the broad citizenship class and personalized diplomas. Children aren’t produced factory-style with one diploma. Everyone composes a curriculum based on passion and curiosity. Teachers get better motivated students and students get more out of their school time.

229601.explanation: Free preschool is a success in some boroughs. It would be good if this becomes available everywhere. Children learn the most from each other. And you can never start early enough with learning and living together. LEF also wants more family schools where parents can get help and education.

229602.explanation: The Meervaart theater can be renovated perfectly. New construction in the Sloterplas costs unnecessarily much money. Moreover, the neighborhood mostly opposes the plans, and the municipality hasn’t addressed the rightful criticism of opponents. Respect their wishes and renovate the Meervaart.

229603.explanation: Sports, dancing, singing – they are all incredibly good for health. These facilities must remain accessible below the socially relevant minimum. This also saves a huge amount of healthcare costs and loneliness.

229604.explanation: There are already enough public order officers (BOAs) focused on safety. With what result? Enforce cleanliness in the city and target people who casually throw trash on the street. LEF wants to achieve safety by investing in jobs, neighborhoods, and equal opportunities. A clean city is a safe city!

229605.explanation: The problem runs deeper. The municipality must focus on education for young people (men) to bring about behavioral change at an earlier stage. Also, all adult men should be able to take courses on how to treat women with respect and regulate their emotions.

229606.explanation: If BOAs are equipped with batons and pepper spray, situations escalate faster. Enforcement officers can be better trained in nonviolent communication.

229607.explanation: Everyone should be able to buy and use a joint. We don’t want Maastricht-like situations with dealers on the street. Better to have everything in the open. Then you can guide and adjust.

229608.explanation: Enforcement is needed at places with the most nuisance (not every container getting a camera) because it appears that it doesn’t work otherwise. Investment in information is also necessary because many residents do not know the waste rules.

229609.explanation: Civil servants must have a neutral appearance and everyone must feel welcome in public spaces. That means no religious expressions in a neutral uniform. LEF supports the separation of church and state.

229610.explanation: Everyone should have access to public spaces, including municipal buildings. Everyone has the right to wear what they wish, whether clothing, aids, or jewelry. Unless one must have a neutral appearance for a civil servant role due to the separation of church and state.

229611.explanation: Housing corporations must be able to build new homes. That is not possible without the sale of homes. LEF introduces Woon Wens, an app that mixes and matches people across generations. So housing space and housing costs are distributed more fairly. Moving becomes fun when you know you’ll get nice neighbors!

229612.explanation: It is good that people with a social connection to the city can get housing. Police officers, nurses, and teachers are urgently needed in the city.

229613.explanation: No, the municipality must transform unused buildings into housing for low- and middle-income groups. Vacancy must be penalized, especially in the expensive parts of the city where it accounts for 5% or more of the residential space.

229614.explanation: Yes, the municipality must develop a housing vision and create extra space for housing cooperatives, free places, and fringes. Not every piece of the city should be stuffed full with a profit model. Let citizens co-decide what is built in the city.

229615.explanation: The total rental duration is currently max. 20 days. That is not much if you want to do home exchanges. The registration system works well and prevents the worst excesses of tourism.

229616.explanation: YES! That is the reward society we really want. When elderly people move and it costs little money, homes become available for young people and families. We are already starting this reward by mixing and matching young and old and exchanging services via the Woon Wens App. Hello 21st century!

229617.explanation: We support affordable rent. But percentages don’t say much. We prefer to introduce a Housing App where all Amsterdammers fill in their housing wishes. These are intelligently mixed and matched to appropriate homes. This helps create flow. That is more important than building more homes.

229618.explanation: Yes, the self-occupancy obligation is a good measure to combat speculation with real estate. It should become more attractive to rent out via so-called Friend Contracts, instead of only to couples and married people.

229619.explanation: It’s a beautiful idea that the land belongs to everyone, and everyone may benefit from it.

229620.explanation: Yes, that stimulates reintegration into the labor market. People must be able to enter a new work environment for a trial period of one year while retaining their benefits. LEF supports a universal basic income.

229621.explanation: The Amsterdam scheme ensures that minimum income earners receive compensation for veterinary costs. They are small amounts. That is nice. But if owners have a suffering pet, and the difference between treatment and euthanasia is thirty euros, it is sad if they have to choose the latter.

229622.explanation: LEF’s plan is to help all 20,000 homeless people get a house. Partly by adding houseboats. Partly by placing Tiny Houses where private cars are currently parked. Numerous municipal buildings are also empty. A roof is the first step towards recovery!

229623.explanation: This is a national matter. The municipality cannot take over all tasks that The Hague throws over the fence to Amsterdam. We do want to prevent care avoidance but that must be done through another counter, namely the health insurer or abolishing the deductible (both nationally regulated).

229624.explanation: People with a low income are already left to fend for themselves. We must actually do more! The rest of society benefits from this too. Your wellbeing and mine is the wellbeing of us all.

229625.explanation: LEF wants to abolish all benefits and allowances that discriminate. Instead, we want a basic income paid to everyone, without conditions, without having to fill out forms.

229626.explanation: LEF actually wants to abolish all allowances and instead introduce a basic income. As long as that does not yet exist, this scheme can remain in place.

229627.explanation: YES, LEF wants to start a basic income pilot as soon as possible. It rewards all forms of work: parenting, caregiving, study, volunteering. It prevents the poverty trap. It promotes equality, independence, social connection, and entrepreneurship. We think that’s GREAT!

229628.explanation: Free public transport is good for the environment, traffic safety, and social mobility. It has been working for years in Luxembourg. So it can work here too, right?

229629.explanation: Free public transport is good for the environment, traffic safety, and social mobility. It has been working for years in Luxembourg. So it can work here too, right?

229630.explanation: Volunteers (and caregivers) are indispensable for the city. Giving them free parking hours is a merciful gesture that suits our city.

229631.explanation: Do not cut off Southeast from the center. There are already few public transport connections. We support the completion of the small metro ring and also want a large metro ring to Abcoude, Weesp, IJburg, North, and Schiphol, from Southeast.

229632.explanation: The quays are collapsing due to all the (heavy) car traffic. That costs a lot of money. LEF wants car-free canals, as the canals were intended. The streets in the city center are too narrow and were never built for cars. Cyclists and pedestrians must be safe. Reward healthy and social behavior!

229633.explanation: LEF supports a large metro ring around the entire city. From Southeast to Weesp, IJburg, North, Houttuinen, Nieuw-West, and then Schiphol, Amstelveen, and Abcoude. You should be able to get everywhere by metro, even at night. The line to Schiphol is a first step. This does not have to go through to Hoofddorp!

229634.explanation: LEF wants a car-free city center. It is life-threatening with all the taxis, private cars, SUVs, and cyclists mixed together. Instead, the canals become green walking boulevards and people with mobility difficulties can still reach the center via small self-driving taxis, model electric Canta.

229635.explanation: That is good for livability, traffic safety and the environment. Every child must be able to play outside. Let’s create wide walking boulevards. Parking spots become vegetable gardens or extended bike paths.

229636.explanation: That is extremely unsafe. We have just converted everything to 30 km/h zones, with positive effects on livability, traffic safety, and the environment. Let’s not go back to the 1960s.

229637.explanation: Window prostitution is outdated. It is inhumane-for women and men-and structurally promotes exploitation. In the background, there is almost always an undercurrent of human trafficking, exploitation, sexism, and often racism. LEF addresses the problem with men. We are ready for the matriarchy!

229639.explanation: This is a good plan. Being able to live independently is important to avoid overcrowding nursing homes with new clients. This also benefits the life happiness of senior residents.

229640.explanation: The municipality should avoid commercial youth care and start its own organization where young people are helped from the heart, not from a profit motive.

229641.explanation: If communities want to organize this, they are free to do so, but this is not a government task, which provides secular care. LEF supports the separation of church and state.

229642.explanation: There must be a budget to organize Youth Cafés; hours during which every Amsterdam youth can receive coaching in an informal setting. For children, we organize these sessions in the library.

229643.explanation: Yes! And a warm lunch that is tasty and nutritious. No child should go to school or sit at school hungry. Every schoolyard gets a vegetable garden where students learn together to grow, prepare, and eat food and fruit. That is enormously good for brain development and health in later life!

229644.explanation: On the one hand: It is a meditative moment. Anglers also contribute to ecological research. Give people the freedom to pursue their hobby. On the other hand: would you like to be caught by a hook through your palate? Well then! Create fishing zones and rest areas for fish protection.

229570.explanation: Residents of Amsterdam know best what is happening in their city. A well-organized, independent citizens' panel strengthens local democracy, trust, and involvement, as long as advice truly counts and there remains room for individual freedom, inclusivity, and innovation.

229571.explanation: Discrimination and racism have no place in Amsterdam. A coordinator can strengthen policy, supervision, and cooperation, so that everyone gets equal opportunities regardless of background, identity, or belief. Freedom only truly applies if it applies to everyone.

229572.explanation: City districts bring governance closer to the residents. They ensure that decisions better align with what is happening in the neighborhoods. However, collaboration with the central city must be more efficient so that rules do not cause delays but contribute to a well-functioning, engaged city.

229573.explanation: Keti Koti is an important moment for remembrance and awareness, but that requires active involvement, not an extra day off. The municipality should better invest in education, dialogue, and initiatives that contribute sustainably to equality.

229574.explanation: The property tax (OZB) is increasingly burdening residents and entrepreneurs. Lower charges create space to invest, do business, and keep living in the city affordable. The municipality must work more efficiently instead of levying more. So, give society more space.

229575.explanation: Higher tourist taxes drive visitors away and harm entrepreneurs, especially in hospitality, retail, and recreation. It is better to focus on smart dispersion and quality improvement; this keeps Amsterdam attractive and strengthens earning capacity without extra charges.

229576.explanation: Entrepreneurs in hospitality and snack bars should have the opportunity to stay open longer at weekends if there is demand. An extension until 3 a.m. stimulates nightlife, employment, and entrepreneurial freedom. Clear rules keep the city livable and attractive for Amsterdam residents and visitors.

229577.explanation: The municipality should stop expensive promotional campaigns to attract tourists. It is better to invest in livable neighborhoods, innovation, and the business climate. Amsterdam naturally attracts visitors if the city remains vibrant, free, and attractive to live and work in. This is also a form of city marketing.

229578.explanation: Fairly distributing property tax (OZB) over owners and users prevents entrepreneurs and tenants from being disproportionately burdened. This keeps living and working affordable for Amsterdammers, encourages a healthy business climate, and prevents shifting charges to tenants.

229579.explanation: The municipality should, in principle, not buy up retail properties. That is government interference that hinders entrepreneurial freedom and a free market. Fewer rules and lower charges are better for a vibrant center that strengthens itself, livable for Amsterdammers and visitors.

229580.explanation: Sell Schiphol shares to free up capital for local priorities such as affordable housing, care, education, and innovation. This restores earning capacity, limits government interference in the form of shareholding, and gives entrepreneurs and residents more space.

229581.explanation: Entrepreneurial freedom and international trade are essential for Amsterdam as an open world city. Boycotts harm the local economy, innovation, and inclusivity without changing geopolitics. It is better to focus on our own values and equal opportunities for everyone.

229583.explanation: Amsterdam must pursue realistic policies: prioritize shelter for those allowed to stay here, focusing on integration, inclusivity, and affordable housing. Cross-border sheltering for rejected asylum seekers displaces local priorities and undermines national government policy.

229584.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit are entitled to inclusive support, but this does not mean priority for social housing as part of integration. This strengthens Amsterdam as an open, realistic city where everyone gets chances without disadvantaging local residents.

229585.explanation: More funding for Dutch language lessons helps expats integrate, work, and contribute to the local economy faster. This strengthens inclusivity, earning capacity, and keeps Amsterdam attractive to talent. This is much cheaper and more sustainable in the long term than benefits or labor market displacement.

229586.explanation: Amsterdam must set limits on asylum reception to keep pressure on housing, care, and neighborhoods manageable. Focus on inclusive integration of those who stay, affordable housing for Amsterdammers, and realistic policies that protect entrepreneurial freedom, realistic ambitions, and earning capacity.

229587.explanation: Bulk waste collection by appointment at home makes Amsterdam cleaner, more efficient, and safer. This reduces street nuisance and improves service for engaged Amsterdammers. This fits with fewer rules and more smart solutions for a livable, clean city.

229588.explanation: Freely accessible toilets on large squares, including those accessible for people with or without disabilities, make the city more livable and inclusive. This simple gesture strengthens involvement and comfort for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors without much bureaucracy.

229589.explanation: Events in parks bring liveliness and social interaction. This is essential for a vibrant Amsterdam. Smart planning preserves green spaces and enhances inclusivity for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. The public space belongs to all of us, so a proper balance of interests is needed.

229590.explanation: Those who produce more household waste pay more. This encourages conscious behavior and efficiency. We endorse this principle. At the same time, excessive barriers should not be raised for waste collection. Garbage will need to be collected more frequently, and illegal dumping must be fined.

229591.explanation: The municipality must continue financially supporting Artis because it is a unique educational and green facility in the heart of the city. Municipal support prevents access being restricted to high incomes only and contributes to an accessible cultural learning environment for all visitors.

229592.explanation: Large cruise ships must remain welcome in Amsterdam because of the economic benefits for the port, entrepreneurs, and employment in our city. Regulated docking with environmental measures keeps the city vibrant and livable. Tourism remains a vital source of income.

229593.explanation: Removing parking spaces for trees undermines entrepreneurial freedom and accessibility for residents and visitors. This is essential for a livable city. It is better to combine smart solutions with maintaining parking capacity so that Amsterdammers and entrepreneurs do not become victims of ideological choices.

229594.explanation: Developing the Lutkemeer polder strengthens earning capacity by making room for entrepreneurs and innovation. We consider this crucial for a future-proof Amsterdam. Costs matter, but stopping harms jobs and growth more; prioritize smart realization for a livable city.

229595.explanation: We oppose this absolute restriction. Wind turbines in more locations, including outside the Western Docklands, accelerate the energy transition and innovation without hindering entrepreneurial freedom. Smart placement, in consultation with stakeholders, preserves livability for residents and businesses.

229596.explanation: Sports clubs must finance themselves through their own revenue and entrepreneurship, not with public money for individual memberships. It is better to invest in accessible sports facilities for low incomes so that freedom and inclusivity are maintained for everyone without much bureaucracy.

229597.explanation: Local media inform Amsterdammers about what happens in the city, essential for involvement in society, elections, and policy. Less subsidy undermines pluralistic journalism, which actually strengthens inclusivity and democratic freedom if it operates independently.

229598.explanation: Of course, together with partners. Top sports events bring liveliness, economy, and pride to Amsterdam. Think of jobs and visibility for entrepreneurs. Cooperation with private parties keeps it efficient to control costs and engage associations.

229599.explanation: Cultural background should not play a role in subsidies for theater and dance groups. For us, quality, innovation, and audience value count. This safeguards individual freedom and inclusivity for all Amsterdammers, so culture flourishes on talent and commitment, not origin.

229600.explanation: More funding for secondary schools with a three-year introductory class is unfair and inefficient. Provide resources based on performance, capacity, and innovation for all students. This keeps education inclusive and future-proof for all Amsterdammers.

229601.explanation: Free preschool for all children aged 2 to 4 provides equal opportunities from the start, strengthens inclusivity, and prepares talent for the future. This keeps Amsterdam attractive for families and also promotes involvement in education during an important formative stage of the child.

229602.explanation: Theater De Meervaart connects Nieuw-West with culture, talent, and social interaction. Investments in this strengthen inclusivity and liveliness in growing neighborhoods. Efficient new construction ensures that limited available resources are used optimally.

229603.explanation: Sports and exercise are crucial for a healthy, engaged city, but much more money requires sharp choices. Prioritize affordable housing, care, and restoring earning capacity first. Efficient investments through partnerships with clubs and entrepreneurs work better than simply adding extra budgets.

229604.explanation: More community enforcement officers (boa's) focused on safety make Amsterdam more livable and attractive for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. Efficient enforcement strengthens trust and individual freedom without excessive bureaucracy. Livability is an important priority for neighborhoods and the city center.

229605.explanation: We are not in favor of plainclothes officers for minor verbal offenses such as shouting or swearing. Real safety comes from focusing on serious crime and livable neighborhoods. Moreover, our police capacity is limited, and handling this nuisance is better done through enforcement.

229606.explanation: Boa's equipped with batons and pepper spray enhance their effectiveness for real safety in Amsterdam neighborhoods. This protects individual freedom and livability for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. However, boa's must be trained in their use to ensure proportionate deployment.

229607.explanation: Coffeeshops that sell soft drugs to foreigners provide controlled access, reduce illegal street trade, and keep Amsterdam economically attractive. This maintains livability through regulation without chaos. And tourism supports entrepreneurs and employment in neighborhoods.

229608.explanation: More camera surveillance at underground waste containers effectively addresses bulk waste and keeps Amsterdam’s streets and neighborhoods cleaner. This promotes livability for residents and entrepreneurs. Privacy must be carefully considered, and prevention efforts should also be implemented.

229609.explanation: Boa's should wear neutral clothing without religious symbols such as headscarves or kippahs to ensure authority and objectivity. This strengthens trust among all Amsterdammers, prevents polarization, and keeps enforcement professional. So unity above division when it comes to law enforcement.

229610.explanation: Face-covering clothing in government buildings undermines openness, security, and trust in democracy. Asking individuals to leave immediately ensures equal treatment and a professional atmosphere for all Amsterdammers. As far as we are concerned, there is no room for anonymity in public services.

229611.explanation: The sale of social rental homes by housing corporations worsens the housing shortage in Amsterdam and further squeezes vulnerable groups. Preserving these homes prioritizes livable neighborhoods and affordable housing for residents. Allow more room for new construction and sustainability efforts by corporations.

229612.explanation: Reserving half of the available social rental homes for Amsterdammers with four years of uninterrupted residence prioritizes loyal residents and combats housing tourism. This keeps neighborhoods livable and affordable and prevents displacement in the housing market.

229613.explanation: The municipality of Amsterdam should sell unused buildings to create financial space, reduce bureaucracy, and use real estate more efficiently for housing or entrepreneurship. This strengthens livable neighborhoods without wasting taxpayers' money. Sales can be made to market parties or residents.

229614.explanation: Amsterdam should set frameworks, but detailed determination of construction on municipal land stifles market initiative and delays housing development in Amsterdam neighborhoods. Flexibility for entrepreneurs and residents accelerates livability and construction procedures, whereas bureaucracy actually slows the city down.

229615.explanation: A complete ban on holiday rentals in Amsterdam's city center discourages entrepreneurship and economic vitality for owners and local hospitality. Regulated rental keeps neighborhoods lively and affordable, with smart limits instead of rigid bans. Flexibility over paternalism.

229616.explanation: A monetary allowance for elderly people moving to smaller homes should not be a collective provision. It creates the wrong incentives without solving structural housing flow. Tailored support for moving costs for less wealthy elderly is negotiable.

229617.explanation: Enforcing at least 40% social rental in new construction maintains the current skewed distribution and hinders differentiation for middle incomes, especially now that Amsterdam neighborhoods need to become more diverse and livable. No rigid quotas, but a flexible mix for more affordable owner-occupied and rental homes.

229618.explanation: A requirement to live in a purchased Amsterdam home limits property freedom and entrepreneurship, such as renting or flexible use. However, we see housing as a right, so we will act against vacancy, illegal rentals, and landlords who are solely after financial gain.

229619.explanation: Abolishing leasehold costs Amsterdam hundreds of millions annually in structural income, essential for investments in livable neighborhoods, maintenance, and social provisions. Retaining leasehold with reforms, such as one-time buyouts, balances interests between owners and the city without financial chaos.

229620.explanation: Increasing the income threshold for welfare recipients encourages self-reliance and labor participation, provided it is linked to mandatory job applications and reintegration programs. This prevents abuse, keeps benefits affordable, and strengthens livable neighborhoods through more working residents.

229621.explanation: In specific cases. Amsterdam already offers the ADAM scheme for low-income individuals with a City Pass (up to €300/year for consultations, chip implants). Expansion only for necessary care with prevention requirements.

229622.explanation: More sleeping places for homeless and roofless people do not solve structural problems and attract more nuisance to neighborhoods, at the expense of livability. Focus on reintegration, addiction care, personal responsibility, and sustainable housing.

229623.explanation: The municipality paying the deductible for low-income residents is a waste of scarce resources and discourages personal responsibility for health. Preferably focus on prevention, addiction care, and work activation to tackle poverty structurally; no extra subsidies but incentives for self-reliance.

229624.explanation: Spending less on assistance for low incomes undermines livable neighborhoods and increases inequality in Amsterdam, where vulnerable groups are already under pressure due to, among other things, the housing shortage. Targeted support with possible counter-performances maintains balance. Cuts must not cost social cohesion.

229625.explanation: Sustainable transition to regular labor participation is the main principle for those eligible. Doing socially meaningful work, such as neighborhood cleaning, green maintenance, or elderly care, can contribute but is not an end in itself.

229626.explanation: Stopping the waiver of waste disposal charges for low incomes disproportionately affects vulnerable Amsterdammers and undermines social cohesion in neighborhoods. On the other hand, we share responsibility for a clean city.

229627.explanation: A pilot with basic income in Amsterdam is not a priority for us. It discourages work and does not structurally solve poverty but increases dependency. Preferably focus on targeted reintegration and incentives for self-reliance to preserve livable neighborhoods. No experiments, but proven approaches.

229628.explanation: Free public transport for all Amsterdammers is a high cost item and leads to overload without structural solutions for traffic jams or livable neighborhoods. Regulated fares with discounts for low-income residents work better. Prioritize maintenance and expansion of public transport.

229629.explanation: Free public transport for all children under 18 is not a core task of local government and discourages personal responsibility, without structural benefits for livable neighborhoods or education. Targeted discounts for low-income or students work better. No free rides, but incentives for smart use.

229630.explanation: Free parking hours for volunteers of social and religious organizations is an unfair advantage, burdens the municipal budget, and undermines equal treatment of Amsterdammers. Additional implementation costs will also arise. Encourage volunteer initiatives in other ways.

229631.explanation: The direct metro connection from Gaasperplas to Central Station maintains essential accessibility for current and future Southeast residents, stimulates economic vitality, and keeps Amsterdam more inclusive. This strengthens livable neighborhoods and is ideal for daily commuters and students.

229632.explanation: Reducing parking spaces in Amsterdam's center harms entrepreneurs, visitors, and surrounding neighborhoods without structural traffic solutions. Equal priority for cars, bicycles, and public transport keeps the city vibrant. So no exclusion but a smart balance in scarce public space.

229633.explanation: Extending the metro line from Amsterdam North to Zuid-WTC station to Schiphol improves accessibility, relieves the railway, and stimulates economic vitality for the entire region. This keeps neighborhoods livable and attractive and is a strategic investment for commuters, visitors, and entrepreneurs.

229634.explanation: Amsterdam's city center must remain easily accessible by car to support entrepreneurs, visitors, and suppliers and maintain economic vitality. Exclusively focusing on bicycles and public transport limits trade and livability. A smart balance keeps the city vibrant for all Amsterdammers.

229635.explanation: More car-free streets in Amsterdam's center harm entrepreneurs, suppliers, and visitors and undermine economic vitality without structural traffic solutions. A balance between cars, bicycles, and public transport keeps neighborhoods lively and livable. No exclusion with us.

229636.explanation: On roads with separate bike lanes, the maximum speed should be 50 km/h to increase traffic safety and better protect cyclists in busy Amsterdam neighborhoods. This keeps the city livable for all road users, without extreme reductions that hinder entrepreneurs.

229637.explanation: A ban on window prostitution in Amsterdam would undermine entrepreneurship and economic activity in the Red Light District without effectively addressing underlying problems such as human trafficking. Regulated prostitution keeps neighborhoods safer and more livable through control and voluntariness.

229639.explanation: Residences with professional support for the elderly in every Amsterdam neighborhood with a municipal contribution is not a core task and increases bureaucracy, without solving structural housing flow. Focus on market forces, regulation, and stimulation. No mandatory multi-year subsidies.

229640.explanation: The municipality of Amsterdam must stop doing business with youth care institutions that repeatedly fail, to protect vulnerable children and spend taxpayers' money efficiently. This safeguards the quality of youth care. Therefore, result-oriented investments that serve our youth's interests.

229641.explanation: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who fits their religion, culture, or worldview to ensure trust and effective assistance. This respects individual freedom in Amsterdam neighborhoods, without segregation. Freedom of choice is very important.

229642.explanation: Providing a youth center in every neighborhood is supply-driven and wastes resources without proven need. A demand-driven setup, only where there is clear need and necessity, with responsible management, keeps neighborhoods livable and promotes shared responsibility.

229643.explanation: School breakfast at all primary schools is not a core task of local government. Parents/caregivers are primarily responsible for their children's breakfast. Targeted aid for true low-income families works better. So no collective multi-year provision, but tailor-made support for a basic need at school.

229644.explanation: Allowing fishing everywhere in Amsterdam with a rod leads to nuisance, pollution, and unsafe situations along busy waterways and in neighborhoods. Targeted, regulated fishing spots with permits keep the city livable.

229585.explanation: Works positively in the integration process.

229590.explanation: Waste disposal charges are for that.

229608.explanation: No opinion as we currently doubt the effect of that.

229614.explanation: Zoning plan is decisive.

229616.explanation: With a horizontal rent price, i.e., for the new home no higher rent than that of the home being vacated.

229618.explanation: We believe the municipality should not intervene there. However, a strict approach to excesses is essential. No prolonged vacancy.

229621.explanation: Make pet health insurance mandatory. The costs should be borne by the owner.

229625.explanation: If possible

229626.explanation: We believe that it is precisely residents with a low income who are entitled to waivers.

229628.explanation: Yes, for the elderly

229639.explanation: Each district must proactively work on this with the aim of enabling the elderly to live at home as long as possible.

229642.explanation: Agree, no further explanation needed

229643.explanation: If necessary, depending on the situation. Only if there is a clear need.

229570.explanation: Amsterdam is full of engaged residents with ideas, concerns, and solutions. With your contribution/vote and the selection of Steering Committees and resident groups, we gain the space to fulfill specific wishes. Initiatives like Red Amsterdam Noord demonstrate the value of local involvement.

229571.explanation: Coordination of steering committees and resident groups focused on discrimination and racism must ensure coherence in policy, support for reports, cooperation with residents and organizations. With your contribution/vote, we strengthen our groups.

229572.explanation: Districts know their neighborhoods. They know what is happening on the streets, in neighborhoods, and among residents. This allows them to respond faster and better to local problems and needs. Help preserve the voice of the neighborhood. Without district administrations, policies will be disconnected from everyday life.

229573.explanation: The municipality of Amsterdam must send a clear signal to the national government against modern forms of exploitation.

229574.explanation: Affordable housing for everyone! The municipality can lower the property tax (OZB). With your vote, we can pressure the municipality to work more efficiently and reduce unnecessary expenses. Homeownership even for the lowest incomes, for you.

229575.explanation: By increasing the tourist tax, visitors contribute more to the costs they cause, such as extra pressure on infrastructure, cleaning, and enforcement. The additional revenue can be used to improve livability for residents.

229576.explanation: If snack bars and restaurants are allowed to stay open until three o'clock instead of two, the nightlife crowd will be better spread out. This results in less nuisance, greater safety, and extra income for entrepreneurs.

229577.explanation: Money can be better spent on what residents really need: affordable facilities, maintenance of public spaces, and a pleasant living environment. Ensure a fair distribution of the municipal budget. Support us and choose livability over mass tourism.

229578.explanation: Only the owners should pay property tax and not the users, which is fairer. We believe that only owners should pay this tax, giving entrepreneurs more room to invest. Support us and help build a stronger, livable local economy.

229579.explanation: The market mechanism automatically determines the price and quantity of products and retail spaces through supply and demand. This preserves the attractiveness of the city center and better supports entrepreneurs.

229580.explanation: Schiphol is an important source of jobs and income for our city. If the municipality sells its shares, this could cost jobs and reduce income for the municipal treasury. We believe the municipality should keep these shares to protect employment.

229581.explanation: Supporting such companies contributes to military conflicts, and investing in them is ethically irresponsible. Vote for us to ensure fair and responsible municipal investments.

229583.explanation: Amsterdam must continue to provide shelter to asylum seekers who are not allowed to stay in the Netherlands, so they have temporary safe accommodation and receive help in a kind and hospitable country.

229584.explanation: Housing shortage is a major problem in our city, and everyone waits a long time for social rental housing. Therefore, the municipality should not give priority to refugees with residence permits, so the distribution remains fair for all residents. With your vote, we can achieve this.

229585.explanation: The municipality should invest more money in Dutch language lessons for labor migrants. This way, they can communicate better, integrate faster, and we get more work, which helps reduce unemployment. With your support, we can make this initiative possible and ensure a stronger, working economy.

229586.explanation: Amsterdam already shelters many asylum seekers, but there is a limit to reception capacity. The municipality should not take in more asylum seekers than currently, to maintain available resources and quality of shelter. Let’s make this message heard and ensure a sustainable and feasible reception.

229587.explanation: In all of Amsterdam, bulky waste must be collected daily at fixed times. This creates more jobs, ensures a cleaner and safer city, and improves waste processing. Support us to make Amsterdam more livable.

229588.explanation: All major squares in the city center should have free public toilets for people. This makes the city more accessible, inclusive, and comfortable for everyone.

229589.explanation: The municipality should allow more events in parks. More employment, active residents: events engage more working people in the local economy. Extra income for the municipality and tourism. Better experience for Amsterdammers in a vibrant city.

229590.explanation: Residents of Amsterdam pay a fee based on the amount of household waste they produce. Those who generate more waste pay more. Incentive to reduce waste, fair distribution of costs, environmentally friendly effect.

229591.explanation: Self-sufficiency: Artis is a tourist attraction that generates income from ticket sales, catering, and activities, so municipal subsidies are not needed. Preserving employment: Artis continues to provide jobs for staff, guides, and suppliers.

229592.explanation: Tourism and economy: cruise ships bring thousands of tourists annually who spend money on hotels, restaurants, museums, and shops, stimulating the local economy. International profile of the city, income for the municipality: port fees, tourist tax, and direct revenues for the city.

229593.explanation: Amsterdam may not remove parking spaces for trees or plants, so residents retain sufficient parking and the city remains accessible.

229594.explanation: Amsterdam must stop the development of an industrial area in the Lutkemeerpolder because it involves high costs, and to protect nature, agricultural land, and the living environment.

229595.explanation: New wind turbines may only be placed in the Western Port Area, so energy generation remains possible without disrupting Amsterdam's cityscape and landscape views.

229596.explanation: The municipality should reimburse the costs of sports club memberships for adults with low incomes, so everyone has the chance to play sports and stay healthy.

229597.explanation: The municipality should provide less financial support to local media, enabling them to operate more independently and allowing public funds to be used more efficiently.

229598.explanation: Amsterdam gains international attention, attracts more tourists, and local economy and employment are stimulated.

229599.explanation: The cultural background of creators should play an important role in awarding municipal subsidies to cultural projects, so a more diverse and inclusive offering of theater, dance, and other cultural performances emerges.

229600.explanation: Secondary schools with a three-year introductory class do not need extra municipal financial support because they can organize their education effectively with existing resources.

229601.explanation: This ensures equal opportunities. In this important developmental phase, children learn social skills, language, and structure through play. When preschool costs money, children from lower-income families are especially at risk of missing these developmental opportunities.

229602.explanation: Public funds are scarce and could be better spent on more urgent social needs. Think of affordable housing, education, healthcare, and sustainability. These services directly affect a broader group of residents.

229603.explanation: A healthier population. Regular sports and exercise reduce the risk of obesity, cardiovascular diseases, and other health problems. Investing in this can lower healthcare costs in the long term. Sports also increase social cohesion in the neighborhood.

229604.explanation: More municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) focused on safety because this not only increases safety in the city, but also creates additional employment. Boa positions are accessible to people with various educational levels. This offers opportunities for job seekers.

229605.explanation: The police must focus on their core task: catching criminals and combating serious crime.

229606.explanation: "Safety without weapons: Make Amsterdam safer by supporting BOAs more, not with weapons, but with knowledge and resources!"

229607.explanation: This is an important source of income from tourism. Millions of tourists visit the city annually, many of them specifically because of the tolerance policy. These visitors spend money not only in coffeeshops but also on hotels, hospitality, shops, and public transport.

229608.explanation: A cleaner and more liveable city. Otherwise, people dump their waste illegally in other places, causing messy streets, bad smells, and pests, which reduce residents' enjoyment of living here.

229609.explanation: "Equal Opportunities for All Enforcement Officers: Make Amsterdam a City of Equality, Clarity, and Employment" - Equality in employment - Clarity and transparency - Support and training: "Amsterdam: A City for Everyone – Sustainable, Equal, and Safe"

229610.explanation: The municipality must allow face-covering clothing in government buildings to ensure communication, safety, and equal treatment. For all religions and beliefs.

229611.explanation: These homes are important for people with low incomes. When social rental homes are sold, affordable housing disappears from the market, making it increasingly difficult for starters, singles, and families with limited budgets to find a home.

229612.explanation: This contributes to stability in the city. People who have lived in Amsterdam for a longer time often have social and work ties to the city and invest in their neighborhood and community. Equal opportunities: everyone who meets this condition gets a fair chance at housing.

229613.explanation: This generates money that can be used for important things such as education, healthcare, roads, or social projects. It is a waste to leave expensive buildings empty, while the municipality could better spend these resources on matters that directly benefit many residents.

229614.explanation: Assistance in keeping the city clear, liveable, and sustainable. By setting clear rules, the municipality can, for example, ensure sufficient housing, buildings that fit the environment, and consideration for greenery, traffic, and safety.

229615.explanation: By renting out homes to tourists, many rental and owner-occupied properties disappear from the local housing market, making it harder for residents to find an affordable place to live.

229616.explanation: By giving elderly people a cash amount when they move to a smaller home, the municipality promotes a more efficient housing market and helps both older residents and new home seekers.

229617.explanation: By designating at least 40% of newly built homes as social rental housing, the city tries to ensure that people with a smaller budget like you also have a chance at a home. Your contribution/vote.

229618.explanation: Protect your home during long absences – prevent burglary! Protecting your house is not only your responsibility but also a shared concern for our community. An empty home attracts unwanted visitors and can lead to significant financial and emotional damage.

229619.explanation: For a fairer housing market: Amsterdam must completely abolish leasehold. It reduces the financial burden for homeowners. Together we can ensure that living in Amsterdam becomes fair and affordable for everyone again. It increases transparency and predictability of housing costs.

229620.explanation: Together we make a difference! Support our campaign for a fairer system, in which social assistance recipients get more freedom to work extra and thus improve their financial situation without losing their benefits. Caring for a just and motivating arrangement!

229621.explanation: Pets are a source of comfort and companionship for many people. But for low-income residents, veterinary costs can be an insurmountable financial burden, often making animal care impossible. Let's not abandon animals and their owners! Accessible animal care!

229622.explanation: Together for a warm society! Support our call for more indoor spaces and shelters so that no one has to sleep in the cold. Let's work together to ensure humanity and safety for everyone!

229623.explanation: For people with a minimum income, the healthcare insurance deductible often represents an additional financial burden, causing them to delay or avoid care. With your contribution, we can set up a scheme where the municipality pays the deductible for low-income individuals.

229624.explanation: Wij willen geen geld gebruiken voor reguliere hulpverlening. In plaats daarvan willen wij investeren in initiatieven die direct de minima ondersteunen-door het geld in hun buidel te steken. Op die manier kunnen zij zelf bepalen wat nodig is, en wordt de hulp meer op maat en persoonlijk.

229625.explanation: This work contributes to society and simultaneously helps individuals build self-esteem and skills. If someone cannot participate due to health issues, age, or other valid reasons, they are exempt. Everyone is encouraged to contribute or develop in their own way.

229626.explanation: Fair and equal treatment for all residents, while also considering the financial situation of low-income people. Low-income individuals struggle to pay for services. A healthy and happy community.

229627.explanation: A basic income regardless of income or job. That is why we want to conduct a trial with a republican basic income in Amsterdam. To give everyone the chance to lead a happy and independent life. To stimulate and diversify Amsterdam's economy.

229628.explanation: We believe every Amsterdammer has the freedom to travel wherever they want without the burden of high transport costs. Therefore, we want to run a pilot with free bus, tram, and metro transport for all Amsterdammers. Contribute/vote to realize free public transport in Amsterdam.

229629.explanation: To encourage minors in Amsterdam to go to school or activities and to promote their development and social interaction, we propose making bus, tram, and metro transport free for all children up to 18 years old.

229630.explanation: Support for volunteers: we believe in the power of volunteers. We know the work they do is essential for the community. Volunteers make an unmistakable daily contribution, and their commitment deserves appreciation.

229631.explanation: Besides the direct connection to Central Station, the metro link must also maintain the strong connection with the Bijlmer. The Bijlmer is a vital and historically important neighborhood in Amsterdam. It is crucial that this district remains easily accessible.

229632.explanation: Reducing parking spaces in the city center and building parking garages on the city's edge is not only good for the city but also for residents, visitors, and the environment. Together we can make Amsterdam more sustainable and livable for future generations.

229633.explanation: It improves accessibility, reduces traffic congestion, and contributes to a more sustainable Amsterdam. Together we can ensure this important connection becomes a reality. Let's create a faster, greener, and better future for Amsterdam.

229634.explanation: The city center, with its iconic canals, cultural hotspots, and business centers, is the heart of the city. As any city grows, so does the demand for mobility and accessibility. A balance between the need for accessibility and the responsibility to keep the city livable and sustainable.

229635.explanation: By banning cars from busy areas, more space is created for pedestrians, cyclists, and greenery. This improves air quality, increases traffic safety, and makes the city center more attractive and livable for residents and visitors. Car-free streets reduce noise pollution.

229636.explanation: Together we can make Amsterdam a safer, more bike-friendly city. A 50 km/h speed limit on all roads with bike lanes is a simple but powerful step. Help and support our campaign.

229637.explanation: The municipality must ban window prostitution to combat exploitation, improve public order, and protect vulnerable people’s safety.

229639.explanation: Due to municipal financial contributions, this care becomes accessible to everyone without elderly people feeling like they lose their independence.

229640.explanation: Support this initiative and help improve the quality of youth care in Amsterdam! Cooperation with these dubious institutions undermines care quality and can have serious consequences for children's development and safety. It's time to demand change.

229641.explanation: Care must fit the person, not the other way around. Together we can ensure in the Netherlands that the care someone receives is respectful, personal, and appropriate.

229642.explanation: Every young person deserves a place where they feel safe and at home. With a youth center in every neighborhood, we can support young people, help them develop their talents, and strengthen social cohesion locally. A place for meeting, activities, development, and support.

229643.explanation: Give Every Child a Healthy Start! Not every child begins the school day with a healthy breakfast. Their learning performance and well-being should not be limited by this. We want to provide a school breakfast at all primary schools so every child can start the day energetic and focused—regardless of home situation or wealth.

229644.explanation: Angling and Sustainable Fish in Amsterdam: fishing with a rod is a beloved hobby for many Amsterdammers. It offers relaxation, nature experience, and connection with city waters. At the same time, we must ensure that the fish population and ecosystem remain healthy.

229570.explanation: The district committee actually had this role. But since political parties have 'hijacked' it, it is wise to assign this role to citizen assemblies and give the district committees a local supervisory task.

229571.explanation: And this should fall under the Municipal Ombudsman and otherwise the NCDR to ensure independence and be safeguarded from political pressure.

229572.explanation: The city of Amsterdam is too large to be governed centrally. The city is growing. Decentralized governance can much better perceive local needs and act accordingly, and if necessary, provide counterbalance to the central city. The district committee plays a crucial role in this, in our opinion. (see also question 1)

229573.explanation: Keti Koti is a culturally very important day in the history of the Netherlands. The abolition of slavery is a milestone in the history of our country and its (former) colonies. Reflecting on this, as on May 4 and 5: commemorating the victims and celebrating freedom.

229574.explanation: People with less money, starters, and tenants must be able to live in Amsterdam. So an increase is not allowed. But a decrease is not practical either. The municipality needs money for youth care. Currently, the municipality receives too little funding from the national government for this. The property tax (OZB) raises money for, among other things, youth care.

229575.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. Due to our popularity, the maximum number of tourists agreed upon with residents is systematically exceeded. The municipality violates agreements with its residents. The number must go down. A higher tourist tax will help with this.

229576.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. Residents have the right to their sleep at 3 a.m. If operators and the municipality can control the nuisance and give local residents genuine participation, 3 a.m. can be discussed as closing time from our point of view.

229577.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. Due to our popularity, the maximum number of tourists agreed upon with residents is consistently exceeded. The municipality violates agreements with its residents. It is contradictory to then spend more money on promotion.

229578.explanation: Smaller companies and shops should be considered regular tenants. When it concerns large companies with an annual turnover of more than €300,000, the users can contribute to the property tax (OZB).

229579.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. A monoculture of shops in the city center does not benefit residents either. When the municipality owns retail properties, it can better control the variety in the offering.

229580.explanation: The municipality should actually increase its share and thus its input. As the municipality of Amsterdam, we are among the few who can offer counterbalance. Counterbalance against those who choose money over the livability of those living under the flight path of Schiphol.

229581.explanation: An army that commits genocide cannot be supported by a government that opposes genocide. It goes against humanity and the human values we aspire to. Moreover, it is a powerful signal to the Amsterdam Palestinians and other Jewish voices in our city Mokum.

229583.explanation: Mercy is part of Amsterdam's coat of arms. Amsterdam is a city historically made up of people seeking asylum and still doing so. We strive for a more humane Amsterdam. As long as they are in Amsterdam, we care for people within our city limits and do not leave them out in the cold.

229584.explanation: People with a residence permit also need a roof over their heads. They are a vulnerable group who fled war and instability. The municipality is attentive to every vulnerable group within its city limits. Moreover, the municipality has a legal duty to fulfill.

229585.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. Although we have international stature, we must continue to support those who want to learn the language. Language education is important. For our children, for those who come to live in Amsterdam, and for their children. It contributes to better integration.

229586.explanation: Amsterdam must make its contribution according to the dispersion law. If possible, a little more. Historically, Amsterdam is a city of refugees and has mercy in its coat of arms for a reason. If Amsterdam can do more, we must do so. The housing crisis is NOT their fault.

229587.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable and thus a clean city. The city pollutes because there is already too much bulky waste daily next to the containers, as people take much less account of their living environment.

229588.explanation: Public spaces are far too little focused on people with disabilities and women. Many more public toilets must be made available that can be used by far more people than only those who can urinate standing. Incidentally, there are also few men who can defecate standing.

229589.explanation: Parks are public places with nature where residents and visitors can enjoy and find peace. They promote livability. For commemorations, this is fine. To screen off the park for commercial interests, preferably not. Also because of nature and tranquility.

229590.explanation: The polluter pays. Therefore, a breakdown by district or district area may also be made. It is suspected that wealthy people generate more household waste than people with less to spend. Furthermore, the packaging industry should also contribute without passing it on.

229591.explanation: A zoo is an outdated concept. In this mobile world, there are better alternatives. However, no money also means no care for the animals still there. Their welfare remains important for Amsterdam as long as they have to live in captivity.

229592.explanation: Large cruise ships are environmentally polluting. They also use a tremendous amount of electricity when moored in Amsterdam. Connection to the power grid is already difficult for new buildings, schools, and small businesses. Electricity and connections should be prioritized for them instead of shore power.

229593.explanation: Amsterdam is becoming greener. So is the fleet of motor vehicles. But electric cars must also have somewhere to park. Making public transport worse, more expensive, and less accessible does not reduce car usage. Making parking spaces scarcer and more expensive creates inequality and despair for residents.

229594.explanation: The Lutkemeer polder is one of Amsterdam's most fertile areas. Building it full of distribution centers is nonsensical. Instead, increase the green ambition there with more urban agriculture, an urban park, and ecological sustainability.

229595.explanation: Public health above all! There is a lack of genuine participation from residents and consideration for health. Distance to residential areas plays an important role! Wind turbines are indeed industrial wind catchers, but they are necessary for our sustainability goals.

229596.explanation: A livable city is also a healthy city. Among low incomes, there is often an accumulation of (health) problems. Sports can provide improvement. A low income should not be an obstacle to working on your health.

229597.explanation: There is a legal obligation to maintain local media. Less money could endanger this. In Amsterdam, there is a divide: AT5 and Salto. Diversity at AT5 is abysmally poor. At Salto, it is excessive. This must be greatly improved. Perhaps even a different operator.

229598.explanation: This is not the municipality's task. The municipality already pays too much for safety at football matches. If the municipality must facilitate top sports events, then the (national) sports federations must raise the money, possibly with subsidies from the national government.

229599.explanation: The underrepresentation in Amsterdam’s cultural world must be addressed. As long as cultural makers with a cultural background are still underrepresented, there is no equality. This must be rectified by taking cultural background into account.

229600.explanation: Children are placed too early into a school type that leads to unequal opportunities. Underassessment happens too often among children with a migration or non-urban background. Three-year bridge classes can provide a better picture of what a child can truly handle. This should be promoted.

229601.explanation: Just like nearly free childcare, preschool must also be free. The child learns social skills at an early age and is prepared for school. It frees up time for people who go to work or contribute socially, such as through informal care and volunteering.

229602.explanation: Nieuw-West deserves an iconic location. It does not have to be only in the Center or in South. Moreover, it helps spread culture and visitors throughout the city. It promotes exchange and interaction between different districts. Like the Slavery Museum will do for East.

229603.explanation: A livable city is a healthy city. Especially among children, the dangers of obesity and diabetes loom. Children exercise too little, and encouraging more sports participation can only be welcomed. We would like to see more diversity in sports and activation across more diverse groups.

229604.explanation: Safety in the city is unfortunately still too fragile. More BOAs (public order officers) can help with this, as long as they do not think they can take over the police's role. BOAs should primarily focus on prevention and de-escalation. A role supporting neighborhood officers would be very suitable.

229605.explanation: Women feel and are unsafe in our city. This must improve a lot! But it is doubtful whether fines for 'Romeos' change male behavior. Moreover, much insecurity is primarily behind the front door rather than on the street.

229606.explanation: BOAs must de-escalate and enforce. Intervention should be left to the police. Amsterdam BOAs have been piloting with batons. When and how they use batons is not well documented. Additional weapons like pepper spray would, in our view, lead to increased use of these.

229607.explanation: We try to control the drug chain: to guarantee product safety and exclude street dealers. Excluding foreigners from this chain pushes them into the hands of criminal sellers. Foreigners have already died from contaminated drugs.

229608.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable and thus clean city. The city pollutes because already every day too much bulky waste is left next to containers, as people consider their living environment much less. Enforcement can improve this.

229609.explanation: Religion plays no role in being neutral or appearing neutral. An expression of faith in a uniform does not either. Modified uniforms for women also say nothing about neutrality. If it did, then only male atheists should be allowed to work in uniformed services.

229610.explanation: This is discrimination. Especially government institutions and those providing assistance must never be discriminatory.

229611.explanation: There is an enormous shortage of social rental housing. Selling these reduces the availability of homes of this size.

229612.explanation: In principle, we support giving priority to Amsterdammers for social rental housing. But Amsterdam must remain merciful and humane: there must also be room for people from outside Amsterdam, such as those coming from a women's shelter, a COA (Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers), or a homeless person with a baby.

229613.explanation: The municipality must start using buildings it currently does not use! Obviously, they can help tackle the housing shortage, but also accommodate small entrepreneurs or creative incubators. Or be used as storage to relieve Lutkemeer. There are many possibilities.

229614.explanation: The municipality should determine what is built on municipal land-but broadly speaking. The details should in principle be left to the people and institutions that settle there, unless the impact on local residents, the environment, or diversity is too great.

229615.explanation: Amsterdam must remain a livable city. Due to our popularity, the maximum number of tourists agreed upon with residents is routinely exceeded. The municipality is violating agreements with its residents. The number must be reduced. Some form of 'holiday home swapping' should still be possible.

229616.explanation: If the money is spent on compensation for adaptations that cannot be carried over and on adaptations of the new home to the needs of the elderly person(s). Additionally, there must be help for the move and for the distribution and/or storage of belongings that no longer fit in the smaller home.

229617.explanation: 40% is the minimum. But many more social rental homes are needed in Amsterdam. The distribution should move more towards 50% social, 40% mid-range, and 10% expensive housing.

229618.explanation: Housing scarcity in Amsterdam is very high. When buying a home in Amsterdam, one must live in it oneself. Prices for homes and land are now so high that a middle-income worker cannot find a place in Amsterdam. Speculation in Amsterdam must be strictly countered.

229619.explanation: Although leasehold is an outdated concept, the municipality can still use this instrument to steer land ownership and what can be built on it. It can also help prevent speculation.

229620.explanation: More and more people must pay more for their house, groceries, and general living expenses. But their benefits do not increase proportionally. A higher amount allowed for additional earnings can lead to more experience, self-reliance, and hopefully eventually an independent life without benefits.

229621.explanation: From an animal welfare perspective, we must guard against avoiding care and animal suffering. Lack of funds must not be a reason for this. For some, their pets are full family members. A happy and healthy pet reduces stress, creates a feeling of happiness, and offers unconditional love to their owners.

229622.explanation: Amsterdam is a merciful city that can be more humane. Shelter for vulnerable people is a task for our city. Currently, there are far too few sleeping places for homeless and roofless people. Moreover, these are good moments to also talk to them and check their health.

229623.explanation: Low-income earners also receive healthcare allowances. This is a national problem and must be solved by the national government as well. We believe the deductible should be income-dependent. However, this should not become a problem that ends up on the municipality’s plate.

229624.explanation: Amsterdam must be more humane. That means that the arrangements the municipality has for people with minimum income must remain and be expanded if necessary.

229625.explanation: As long as the government (municipality) does not independently determine what is 'socially meaningful' or prescribe what benefit recipients (are required to) do. The initiative from the benefit recipient is important here. Cooking for neighbors, volunteer work, or informal care can also be included.

229626.explanation: Waiving a municipal levy can be just enough to make ends meet for people with low incomes while living costs rise disproportionately and benefits and wages remain low.

229627.explanation: Introducing a basic income is still far off. But a pilot involving about 5,000 benefit recipients should be possible in Amsterdam. Of course, they will be well monitored, looking also at societal benefits rather than only financial ones.

229628.explanation: If we want everyone to use public transport more, not just the less affluent, then we must encourage that for everyone. Besides making it free, the frequency and accessibility of all areas must also be increased.

229629.explanation: If free public transport for everyone is not achievable, then at least for all Amsterdammers under 18. What you learn young sticks for life.
